reviews.text:
My first time staying at Apurva Kempinski.
My first impression was excellent. All the staff were very friendly and prompt during check-in.
Guests were escorted to their rooms and explained all the areas and activities at the hotel. Our room was upgraded from a regular view (outside the hotel) to a garden view (inside the hotel). Other than the view, everything was the same.

Upon arrival, the room was provided with a bathrobe and slippers for the little one as requested, and free amenities (soap, shampoo, toothpaste, and toothbrush) were provided.

However, based on my experience staying at similarly priced hotels, I feel Kempinski doesn't pay enough attention to other small details.

1. The shower in the bathtub was blocked, so I had to move to the other shower area to rinse off.
2. The TV remote had low/broken batteries (often wouldn't work).
3. The cleaning service took the towels and didn't replace them immediately.
4. The breakfast, with an additional price of around 1 million rupiah, wasn't worth it. The fried rice was hard (very hard). The miso soup was salty. The Cawanmusi was salty. The wine was still dirty and dusty. The signature turmeric and tamarind jamu (herbal drink) was too sour.
5. I booked through the website at 12 noon on the 29th, and no payment link was sent until 11:00 PM on the 30th. It stated that if payment was not made according to the payment link instructions, the order would be considered canceled. The hotel phone number couldn't be reached; I had to wait until 8:00 AM for the reservations department to process the payment link. Fearing a lack of rooms, I booked through a third party. Sadly, booking through a third party doesn't give me any additional member points, etc.

I rate the kids' activities at Kempinski 7/10. There are plenty of toys in the kids' club, but they aren't arranged well, so only a few can be played with, perhaps displayed on the toy shelves (not just thrown in containers, all the toys are mixed up). The dolls looked dirty. The guards were on standby upstairs, but none interacted with the lower play area.
Otherwise, it's quite good. The unique design makes kids happy when they enter. There are bicycles, cars, etc.

Just a few suggestions from me:
- A mini farm could be added for the kids' area.
- In-room service could be improved, especially in terms of the duration. One portion of oxtail soup arrived within an hour.

Cleanliness is excellent.
All the staff who passed by always greeted us and were very proactive in helping.

Will I return? Maybe when Kempinski is operational in Ubud.

Thank you for the service during my stay. All the staff were very friendly and helpful throughout our stay.

As a frequent Kempinski guest and Titanium member, I was genuinely shocked by our experience at the Kempinski Hotel Corvinus Budapest.
We originally booked four nights (14-18 December) but  to cancel and leave midway through our stay due to the consistently very poor experience. The breakfast, in particular, was far below Kempinski standards. It was disorganized, overcrowded and confusing. Guests were queuing for eggs while others could supposedly order à la carte — if you were lucky enough to get served. Basic items such as avocado toast or Eggs Benedict required an extra charge, which is completely unacceptable for a five-star Kempinski hotel.

The food quality and variety were disappointing and did not reflect the brand at all. I find it hard to believe that many of the five-star reviews come from guests familiar with Kempinski properties; several other negative reviews accurately reflect our experience.

Upon arrival, there was no welcome amenity in the room, which is standard at Kempinski hotels — especially for Platinum or Titanium members. Only after raising a complaint was something provided. In the room, there was only body wash provided — as already mentioned in other negative reviews — yet clearly nothing has changed. Ironically, the spa does offer proper hair shampoo, which only further highlights the inconsistency in standards.

The lobby atmosphere was another major disappointment. It feels like a busy passageway rather than an elegant hotel lobby, with constant foot traffic and no sense of exclusivity. Tables from the EZ Bistro in this passage were frequently left uncleared, giving the area the appearance of a food court in a shopping center rather than a luxury hotel.

Due to these issues, we requested the cancellation of our remaining two nights and had to book another hotel on short notice, which was both stressful and costly. While we ultimately found a proper five-star hotel elsewhere, this significantly impacted our vacation.

On top of the cancellation and apology from the Lady in Red, we received a chocolate box that was already past its expiration date! Sadly, this says a lot about the overall level of attention to detail and the quality of service/food.

I regret not requesting reimbursement for the nights we stayed, but at that point, we simply wanted to leave. I will be escalating this experience to Kempinski management, as this property seriously risks damaging the reputation of the brand.

I found this hotel on YouTube during the COVID-19 pandemic and finally made it to my visit.

[Overall]
I was extremely satisfied with this hotel. If I ever visit Bali again, I'll definitely return. The lobby had a spectacular atmosphere. It was worth the trip just to see it.

[Front Desk, Service]
We arrived at the hotel after 10 PM due to an overnight flight. I'd contacted them in advance to let them know I'd be late, and check-in was smooth. The staff were simple and unobtrusive. They were a little close, but not too shy.

[Room]
We stayed in the Grand Deluxe Lagoon. The overall design was simple yet relaxing. The lighting was a little dim overall. My wife and I had a few large suitcases, so we only had one space to unpack, which was a bit of a challenge. The water supply was limited, but we requested more, and housekeeping provided more the next day.

The shower and toilet were easy to use, with a typical Japanese feel. While it may have just been our room, we were bothered by noise on the first day, including footsteps from upstairs and children from the next room, so we had to consult the front desk the next day. We had hoped for a room change, but the hotel couldn't accommodate, and offered to change rooms. We liked our room, so we declined, but the noise was a bit of a problem.

[Meals]
The breakfast was excellent. Everything was delicious. I had bread, egg dishes, Balinese food, porridge, noodles, yogurt, etc.

I ate in the same place every morning, and the staff remembered my coffee and other orders and made smooth suggestions. Since I often overate at breakfast, I opted for a light lunch at the in-room dining area. It may be pricey by local Bali standards, but it's about the same price range as a regular meal in Japan. The meal was delicious and not particularly unappealing. In the evening, we went to the rooftop bar and Baiyun. Each had its own unique charm and was enjoyable.

[View, Pool]
When considering this hotel, the view of the palace-like hotel exterior from the pool was a must-see. The main pool was very large, so even when there were many people in the pool, it didn't bother me. I was able to relax by the poolside both mornings. By the way, when I walked over to the Ritz-Carlton next door, I saw that the pool was small, so I decided to choose the Kempinski. Relaxing in the pool while looking at the hotel from inside is the perfect holiday. It's also impressive to see the hotel gradually light up from the rooftop bar during the magic hour at dusk. The good seats fill up quickly, so I recommend going right after opening to get a good seat. If you go later, there won't be any seats and it will be difficult to get in.

Personally, it was a great hotel that I would like to return to.

I’ve stayed at many decent hotels and resorts, but Kempinski Nusa Dua is definitely a place you’d want to come back to.

-Villa
I stayed in a villa, which I highly recommend if you value privacy. Villa guests have access to a private lounge that’s available almost all day — breakfast(12pm), afternoon tea(5pm), and drinks in the evening. It really adds to the overall experience.

The facilities are excellent and still feel very new. All minibar items are complimentary, which was a nice touch.

What truly stood out was the staff. Every single person was attentive, warm, and genuinely caring — they are one of the biggest reasons I wanted to extend my stay.

One thing to note: some villas don’t get much direct sunlight. If that matters to you, I’d recommend requesting a unit with good sun exposure over the pool area.

If privacy is your priority, go for a villa. If you prefer a more open vibe and ocean views, the beach-facing suites are a better choice.

-Beach & Pool
The beach has just the right amount of waves — great for light water sports or simply chilling in the water. There are three large main pools, so it never feels crowded.

-Food
I’m quite easy-going when it comes to food, and overall the dining here is solid. Any restaurant you try on the property will be reasonably good and satisfying.

-Spa & Massage
The spa facilities are spacious and well-designed, but availability is quite limited. I only managed to book a foot massage for once, which was unfortunately underwhelming — it was just basic rubbing rather than a proper treatment.

But prices are relatively affordable compared to other nearby five-star resorts, especially considering the quality of service and facilities.

Overall, I had an amazing stay and would definitely come back here for another vacation.

We spent 3 nights at the Apurva Kempinski, it was amazing.  The hotel is gorgeous and is HUGE! The grounds are beautiful but what really makes the experience so special was the people working there. The warmth and friendliness we encountered from every single team member was outstanding. From the moment we arrived and were greeted by Bima until we left we had nothing but positive experiences. Our room was a junior suite with plunge pool which gave us access to the Cliff Lounge - absolutely outstanding, the team that works there are exceptional, Mary as concierge took extra special care of us. In the lounge we met Wira and Dadi, the smiles and special service were wonderful. The breakfast served in the lounge was fantastic. The pool area is beautiful and has many loungers and areas to relax.  There is a children’s area with water slides and if I could say anything negative is that it can get quite loud.  Our room was above the pool and we could hear children screaming at the pool. My suggestion would be to make those rooms family only. Our room was large and gorgeous. We enjoyed the plunge pool.  Our room attendants, Ayu and Adi took great care of us. We dined at three restaurants, Koral which is the aquarium restaurant. Not only was it a beautiful unique experience the food was outstanding. We had dinner at Pala and tried the Indonesian cuisine which we loved. The last night we dined at the Reef Beach Club which was also great. Overall an awesome experience and we will definitely be back.

This review reflects our personal experience during our stay, and it’s important to note that experiences may vary depending on the season and the room you are allocated.

The Good:
There’s no denying it, the Apurva Kempinski is visually stunning. The architecture is awe-inspiring, the landscaping immaculate, and the scale of the property truly impressive. The gardens and pool areas are spectacular, offering plenty of space to explore and unwind.

Our room was excellent, spacious, modern, and well maintained. Housekeeping staff were friendly and attentive. The Samsung smart TV allowed easy screen casting, and the automatic Toto toilet was a high-tech highlight (apparently worth around AUD $12,000!).

The Not-So-Good:
The resort’s sheer size, however, can work against it. While guests receive a resort map, signage throughout the property is minimal, especially at night. The dim, ambient lighting makes navigation tricky, and the lack of illuminated or elevator signage doesn’t help.

Getting around can also be confusing. The lift system feels like solving the Da Vinci Code, we stayed in Zone 3, Level 5, and could only reach the main building via one particular lift. It required either walking outdoors or in between corridors, between buildings, which was fine for us but could be a challenge for anyone with mobility issues.

Around the Pool:
The pool and garden areas are breathtaking, but unfortunately, the all-too-common issue of “towel reservations” persists, guests claiming loungers early and disappearing for hours. It would be great to see staff take a more proactive stance in discouraging this behaviour with signage and gentle enforcement.

Staff repeatedly ask guests for their room numbers by the pool. While it’s understandable that this is meant to ensure only registered guests use the facilities, a quick digital scan of your room key, similar to systems used at many modern resorts or even Disney’s MagicBand concept, would streamline the process and enhance the overall guest experience.

Fresh towels around the pool were often hard to come by, and while the offer of chilled water was a lovely touch, clean glasses were rarely available, meaning guests frequently had to track down staff to request more. A familiar pattern starts to emerge here: the resort excels in presentation and amenities, yet falls short on consistency, with basics like towel and glass replenishment not being maintained to the standard expected of a property of this calibre.

Gym:
The gym facilities are excellent, well-equipped for both beginners and regular gym-goers. There’s an abundance of clean towels, cold bottled water, hand sanitiser, and refreshing chilled flannels, all of which add to the experience.

However, one noticeable misstep is the outdated check-in process. Guests are required to manually write down their name, room number, time of entry, and signature before using the gym. A far simpler and more efficient approach would be to scan a room key, since all of this information is already stored electronically.

In the Room:
While bottled water was replenished daily (a nice touch), toiletries were not. Over our five-night stay, shower gel wasn’t restocked, and the two bottles were only half full on arrival. This seems inconsistent with the resort’s otherwise high standards, especially given the nightly rates. Small details matter, and skimping on basics like shower gel leaves an odd impression.

The shower itself was spacious, but the water never got properly hot, and the sink plug didn’t hold water, small annoyances that detract from the overall experience.

Dining: 
Despite the Kempinski’s reputation, the breakfast buffet was disappointing compared to other four or five-star resorts in Bali (such as Pullman Legian or Ayodya in Nusa Dua).

For a resort of this calibre, the Western breakfast options were surprisingly limited, three types of melon, some grapes, and mini bananas. Pineapple was scarce, and there were no berry fruits at all. Bread choices were basic (white or brown), with sourdough available only occasionally and often self-serve from a communal loaf.

Hot dishes such as eggs, beans, and hash browns had to be ordered from the table rather than from a live cooking station, which slowed service considerably. The absence of chicken bacon was, frankly, unacceptable for a luxury breakfast in Bali 😄. Maple syrup was another surprising restriction, it was kept hidden behind the staff service station, with distribution tightly controlled, and had to be requested and returned immediately after use. Items such as crêpes or donuts, which are often readily available even at lower-rated resorts, were completely absent.

Water Policy:
One surprising inconsistency, while water is freely available in rooms, by the pool, gym and even in their shuttle buses, it becomes a premium commodity item in the restaurants. We were charged AUD $9 per bottle at dinner, and staff never mention this in advance. It’s a small thing, but one that leaves a sour taste (no pun intended) after an otherwise elegant meal.

Construction Noise:
It’s important to note that there is an ongoing construction project next door. While our Zone 3 room (facing the Ritz-Carlton) was relatively unaffected, guests by the pool or closer to the site are likely to hear significant noise. While the Kempinski obviously cannot control the construction, it should proactively inform potential guests before booking. Not everyone may be as understanding or tolerant of the noise, and transparency would help manage expectations and avoid disappointment. The hotel currently makes no mention of this on its website, even though the work reportedly continues seven days a week and is not expected to be completed until 2029.

Final Thoughts:
The Apurva Kempinski is undeniably a magnificent property, its grand architecture and breathtaking setting make a powerful first impression. However, beyond the surface, the experience doesn’t always live up to the resort’s prestigious reputation or price point. Inconsistent service standards, underwhelming dining options, and small but persistent oversights detract from what should be a flawless stay.

What’s most surprising is that several lesser-known resorts in Bali are going above and beyond to delight their guests, while the Kempinski appears to be resting on its name rather than setting the benchmark for luxury hospitality. With greater attention to detail, improved communication, and a more guest-focused approach, the Apurva Kempinski could easily reclaim its place as the true standard of excellence it aspires to be.

Stayed in the 2 bedroom Villa for 3 nights. We enjoyed the villa and the overall stay despite the rainy weather. 

The villa is stunning, spacious, clean and has amazing bathrooms. We also enjoy the villa pool and patio where we spent most day time at. 

The villa lounge is also a nice option for all day tea, coffee and light snacks. 

Explored the different restaurants except for the underwater one for dinner and the food and drink options are great.  

The spa is also one of the best ones we ever tried with amazing set up. 

Access to the beach is also superb and the beach was super clean! We really enjoy hanging around at the pool right by the beach. Super relaxing

The only downside is that the villa is located right up front before the main lobby so access to the hotel facilities is a bit far. Overall great stay!!

Stayed here for 6 nights in October 2025, just my husband and me.
Where to start...

Location: a little isolated in Nusa Dua however I doubt anyone needs or wants to leave the complex during their stay! We ordered from Grab some items like soft drinks (as cheaper from local mart than the $6.90 for can of coke from the hotel).

Rooms: we had a Cliff Suite with a plunge pool. Bed was enormous and so comfortable. Bathroom/bedroom and dressing area well-appointed. We were lucky enough to get a double balcony area, so pool + sun lounges which I understand are not the norm for Cliff Suites so very lucky there. The plunge pool was quite large and warm enough for night time dips before bed.

Breakfast: we ate this at the Cliff Club Lounge however I believe you can also eat at the Pala Rooftop Bar however we didn't try this. Breakfast spread at Club Lounge was a bit repetitive but very tasty, lots of asian fusion flavours prioritised in hot dishes e.g. roast duck/chicken, sushi, noodles, fried rice, a few different curries, sausages. Any 'traditional' foods were to be ordered from the A La Carte menu. Between the two of us we tried almost everything on the A La Carte, special shout out to the lobster scramble, eggs benedict, corn fritter and the iced latte's made to order.

Room service: good range of options but quite pricey, two mains and a few cans of coke cost about $90 AUD. Food came in a special trolley to keep it at the perfect temperature and displayed beautifully by room service staff

Spa: we had a coupon as part of our Luxury Escapes package however after the massage realised we'd been given the wrong massage and the hotel amended this by allowing us to organise an additional 60min massage free of charge which was very accommodating of them

Restaurants: we ate at every restaurant on offer at Apurva Kempinski but I'll detail each one separately, overall an incredible experience

Staff: so friendly, couldn't do enough for you and eager to problem solve any issues. An example of this was our Luxury Escapes Lunch/Dinner voucher not specifying all of the restaurants it could be used at, leading to us accidentally paying for a meal we could have used a voucher for, however they allowed us to use multiple vouchers in one day so we could maximise these before the end of our trip.

Resort: incredibly impressive buildings, especially looking down towards from the beach from the Spa level is breathtaking. I wouldn't consider this hotel to be particularly disability friendly/accessible as there was lots of walking required to get to lifts, and lots of stairs up and down around the pool and inner buildings.

Other: beware of monkeys!! Even though we did not leave any food or personal items around the pool we still had three cheeky monkeys open our door and steal our fruit bowl and snacks from our table when we were getting ready for dinner. Staff did come and clean up afterwards. Equal parts horrified and very amused as we've been to Bali many times and never had a monkey thief. They were very cute but remember to lock your balcony doors even if you are still in the room.

Overall, an experience that will stay with me for a long time. What a treat to have been able to experience a hotel like the Apurva Kempinski.

Bali is a beautiful island - this was our first visit. We arrived after a busy week in Tokyo with one aim - to chill!  

I’m lucky that I’ve traveled the world and stayed in some great hotels. I must say that the Apurva Kempinski is up there with the best. On arrival the check in process was slick, what struck me was the scale and ora of the reception/lobby area - simply stunning.  We arrived in the dark which probably added to the atmosphere. 

We checked into our two bed villa which was perfect for a family of 4 with 2 teenage boys. Decor and finish is all to a high standard. The private pool and outdoor lounge area is great for those lazy morning chill outs.  

Two very minor improvement to accommodation suggestions - would love it if both the privacy and black out blinds were electric. There must be about 24 pull blind cords! Second, water pressure in the showers could do with being a bit stronger. Not bad if that’s your only criticism!

The hotel pool and beach area are great (a bit of a treck from the villas). One thing I found strange was the route from villas to pool/beach area took you through the hotel drop off/pick up area, you had to dodge staff, guests and cars alike - would be nice if they could create a more private route.  The boys (and I must confess me) loved the daily beach volleyball!

I would never tire of the view from the hotel lobby down to the beach - spectacular!

The “Villa Lounge” breakfast and afternoon tea was great - loads of choice and super friendly staff. The other restaurants are all good - with a wide range of choice/theme. Beach/grill, Chinese, Japanese, Indian, seafood.  We had a full board package booked through Luxury Escapes. The dinner and cocktail inclusions are administered through a paper voucher system - all a bit of a faff! 

Now onto one of the stand out parts of our stay - the “service”. The Balinese have a great way of providing excellent attentive service in a super friendly way. Not too much, not too little - just right!! 

In particular a shout out to Adi who took the best care of us in our villa on a daily basis. His attention to detail was phenomenal - from Day 1 he knew the whole family by name, striking the perfect balance of friendliness and helpfulness.
To the management at Apurva - give this guy a promotion or pay rise. He is one of the best members of any hotel staff I’ve come across!!

Sad to leave Bali! We would come back and we would come to the Apurva - a great hotel, with standout staff!

Upon arrival, we were not given any instructions on how to transport our suitcases, so we carried them to our room ourselves. We stayed in room 2075, the second-highest grade room, and were surprised to find water seeping from two places under the floorboards, even though it wasn't raining. The wood floors creaked. The staff knew about the problem, but simply soaked up the water every day. I don't understand why they closed the rooms and didn't make any fundamental repairs. I was staying at the official website at the regular price, so I'm not happy with being charged the normal rate for a room like this. The hot pot at the restaurant was delicious, and the staff were extremely courteous. Thank you. Finally, I arranged for a car to the airport, and the staff asked if I had confirmed the number of luggage, date, time, and car type, and even gave me a paper with the recommended information, but they were completely unable to arrange it. I was shocked. The porter at the driveway on the day simply said there was no reservation, so I called Gojek myself and headed home. While the hotel was certainly large and luxurious, I was disappointed with the flooding on arrival and departure, as well as the flooding in my room, which I had high hopes for.

We stayed at the Kempinski Corvinus for three nights as a party of husband and wife with our 14 year old daughter having booked via American Express Travel.

First the positives:

Great Facilities-Gym with a variety of equipment and a pleasant swimming pool, if not large.

Good location near the main shopping street and not far from the Danube.

Largely friendly staff.Particularly in the spa/wellness suite, at breakfast and the minibar/coffee restocking man.The senior staff are also very courteous, helpful and respectful. 

The reception will print out tickets for you (needed in paper form for a Hungarian Parliament visit) on a complimentary basis.

The negatives:

Poor welcome/first impression. Upon arrival the doorman opened our taxi door and then promptly disappeared. No offer of help with luggage or getting us a trolley. It was bizarre ; I mentioned it to the receptionist on checking in who was lovely and very professional indeed, wish I’d got her name.She said she’d feedback to the team concerned.

No way of messaging guest services on an app or via the TV (unless I missed it). Any interaction with Staff or services seems to have to be in person or on the telephone which seems out of step with the modern world. I did use the reception email address but the email went unanswered. 

The larger rooms (I found out) all have connecting doors with poor sound proofing.
I was woken at 5am by a screaming/wailing child next door, the sound felt close enough to be in the same room. I woke up in a startle to the sound and,although I initially dozed back off for a few minutes, I was woken by a further scream a few minutes later which went on for over 20 minutes so that was it for the night. This lack of sleep spoiled my last day in Budapest which is a Shame as it is a lovely city. I reported what had happened to reception (and I know it’s not the hotel’s fault when other guests are noisy ; we all have a different style of parenting) but she told me the lack of soundproofing is a known issue in these rooms.  

I felt it to be unreasonable to be awoken in this manner in a 5 * hotel, surely modern building design would have a soundproofing solution?

We asked to be  moved to a quieter room which the receptionist did agree to. She was friendly but there was no expression of empathy or regret which would have gone an awfully long way.

I rang guest services who assured me they would send someone straight up to move our luggage to the new room so we duly sat with our cases by the door. Twenty-five minutes later no-one had arrived so I went down to get the new keycards myself and we moved ourselves.  No apology from guest services for not showing up.

I found the  way we have been treated during this stay, until the point of departure, to be disappointing. It felt to me  like  they just didn’t care about the guest experience on the basis that it’s a capital city, people will book regardless so they don’t have to try too
hard. Either that or it’s a staff training issue, general disorganisation, communication issues  or the staff are just too busy to manage the workload/guest interactions.

That said when we checked out one of senior staff did speak to us in person and apologise for our experience and wrote off approx $30 USD of extras which was a welcome gesture and appreciated. She was warm and genuine, if only we had been treated in this way whilst we were still resident at the hotel.

We stayed in a sister Kempinski hotel in Berlin in 2023, the experience could not have been more different, it was absolutely out of this world (see my Tripadvisor review).  Maybe some of that excellence could be brought to the Corvinus?

I did contemplate whether to post this experience, had our issues been taken seriously whilst we were still resident in the hotel to improve our experience. I wouldn’t have done.

All in all our experience of this hotel did fall short.
If you are expecting a 5* experience I’d advise managing your expectations.I do hope the hotel can address its staff/welcome and soundproofing issues and get its house in order. If it did it could be truly fantastic , no question. 

Our three nights in Budapest, however, showed us that there are also a lot of other really nice hotels to choose from.

Gorgeous hotel with incredible gym and spa amenities. I had the best massage (office myofacial release) I have ever had at Apurva with Lisna! I felt more mobile following my massage as I have chronic back pain/stiffness from repetitive desk work. She recommended some home care strategies that I deeply appreciated. :)

Apurva Kempinski Nusa Dua  has truly surpassed our expectations with this exceptional luxury resort. Opting for the five-night Luxury Escape Cliff room package was the best decision for our 20 year wedding anniversary. It included all the trimmings like endless beverages, afternoon tea, and exclusive access to private pools. We were immersed in an experience so incredible that words can hardly do it justice, the hot pot dining experience is a must, couples massage, and the endless cocktails at the Rooftop bar from 5-7. The all-inclusive nature of the package ensured that indulgence was limitless, and we found ourselves delighting in exquisite cuisine to the point of near food coma and a very minimal bill from a couple of drinks at the main pool area. 

Beyond the culinary experiences, the staff were nothing short of outstanding-always smiling, ever helpful, and willing to accommodate every request with effortless grace. The facilities redefine opulence, exceeding five-star standards with ease. Every corner of this property radiates luxury, making it a truly phenomenal getaway. We even got to see a three day wedding set up however sad that we had to leave before the main event, I would have loved to see what the bride looked like. I couldn't recommend it more highly and we even found ourselves looking for another package before we left.

Great hotel, seems too big at first, but that settles quickly. Super nice property, very well maintained. Lots of nice restaurants and super sun loungers. For us always with a sign how good a hotel could be. The Ritz Carlton next to it didn't have nice loungers, we were glad to have chosen the Kempinski! 

Important, Hilton is probably expanding their hotel next to it for the next 3-4 years, the noise can be heard on the room side to the construction site, we then got another room, plus a meal in the restaurant of your choice for free and a bottle of wine in the room, because the first night was unfortunately unbearable! So great reaction from the hotel, which you can also expect from a house like the Kempinski, especially since we had not only two days checked in here. That's how I imagine the solution.

Staff super friendly, food in the evening fantastic, in the morning there is something for everything, even for European guests, of course the breakfast is Asian oriented, as many guests from China etc., which is of course absolutely ok here. 

The beach is beautiful, but the water is full of plastic , a problem of the region, but is cleaned daily! 

Ideal also for weddings, the wedding site a dream that is beautifully decorated, had been able to witness a big wedding, but not as a wedding crasher 😃

Spa was a dream and the gym is super equipped! All around think that with best hotel on the coast Nusa Dua. Thank you for this lovely experience.

This hotel has issues. Staff say it was renovated but we noticed many things to be lacking most significantly the air conditioning.

The back of the hotel is like a letter C. The rooms here are exposed to much direct sunlight and get rather warm. In our room the temperature was always at 23-24 Celsius which is 78 Fahrenheit. That is just too hot to sleep in or be comfortable. The AC system is rather fake in that is only cools the outside air a little. In our case I do not think this worked at all. I mentioned it to the front desk but nothing changed. That is poor.

Some other noteworthy issues we that the breakfast area was both crowded and dirty. Getting coffee took a long time and everything was chaos. The room was also quite noisy in that you could here all the people and noise from the fashion street place behind the hotel. The windows are not sound proof. We opened them to get cooler air but even when we closed them nothing changed.

One last odd note is that bathtub is child sized or made for people under 5 feet tall. That makes it useless.

On a positive note we found Nobu in the hotel to be very good and we found a great Italian place called Alelli about 100 yards away in another hotel. The concierge team were also friendly and first class. The location of the hotel is excellent but there are many hotels nearby probably with working air conditioning.

The hotel's architecture is magnificent and gorgeous! The stone floor in the lobby has a beautiful sheen, reflecting different colors depending on your angle. I loved it!

The hotel changes its look from morning to night. Walking through the hotel's streets or strolling along the beach is a truly relaxing experience. The large pool is also wonderful—perfect for a lazy afternoon!

The staff were all very polite and courteous!

The room's soundproofing wasn't great. At midnight, I could hear people eating and putting away their dishes in the room across from me. There was a locked door in the middle of the room, presumably connecting to another room, and I could occasionally hear their noises.

Also, the room was incredibly humid (we were on the third floor), and the air conditioner had to be set to 20 degrees Celsius to avoid feeling damp and sticky.

The Japanese restaurant for dinner is definitely a Taiwanese option; the prices are exorbitant! (Unless you're incredibly wealthy and don't mind.) There were two of us, each ordering a set menu. One cost NT$1,450, and the other was just over NT$3,000. There were only ten dishes, and the portions were incredibly small. Seriously, I'm not exaggerating. The flavors weren't authentic, but that's understandable given the high cost of sourcing ingredients. Australians and Europeans might enjoy it, but there are plenty of Japanese restaurants in our country, and most people have probably had authentic Japanese food, so there was a gap in expectations.

However, the food at Reef Beach Club was good, the prices were reasonable, and the staff were friendly, though a little overly enthusiastic, suggesting they were under pressure to meet sales targets. Overall, the experience was good. The breakfast was also better than at the other place, but don't expect too much; it can't compare to what you'd get in Taiwan. Their croissants were surprisingly delicious, though. A must-try! Another thing to note is that seating in the hotel lobby, whether inside or outdoors with a view, requires a drink before you can sit down. We didn't realize this at first and went to sit down to rest. The waiter politely (and tactfully) handed us menus and asked if we wanted to order anything. We then realized we needed to order something... So, can't guests just sit in the lobby and admire this magnificent building, which was painstakingly built? What a shame!

Also, the pool closes at 7pm, which seems a bit too early. I'm not sure why, perhaps to keep the hotel quiet?

Also, although it has its own beach, at low tide, the water is completely dry; it's practically bottomless. What's the point of swimming? We went to another beach club in Uluwatu, which also has its own beach and water. They've taken the trouble to fence off the water, so even at low tide, the water isn't completely dry. There's still plenty of water to play in, even paddleboarding. I think this five-star hotel could use a little more attention!

We had the most wonderful one-week stay at Apurva Kempinski with our family, including our young toddler. From start to finish, everything was flawless. We split our time between the Grand Deluxe Room and a stunning Two-Bedroom Villa — both were spacious, beautifully appointed, and incredibly comfortable.

The service was truly exceptional — attentive, warm, and thoughtful. A huge highlight was the Villa Lounge staff, who not only greeted us by name but went above and beyond to ensure we had everything we needed. A special thank you to April and Sekar for their outstanding hospitality. They even remembered to pre-order my masala tea every morning — such a lovely touch that made all the difference.

We also celebrated my partner’s birthday at Koral Restaurant, and it was truly unforgettable. The ambiance, the unique setting, and the impeccable service made it such a memorable evening — a perfect way to mark a special occasion.

The views from the resort are absolutely breathtaking — we often found ourselves just pausing to take it all in. For dinner, Baiyun Hot Pot quickly became a favorite of ours. The food was delicious and the experience consistently excellent.

Apurva Kempinski gave us a perfect blend of luxury, comfort, and warm hospitality. We left with full hearts and can’t wait to return!

This review is for two aspects of the Apurva resort we stayed at just recently: one is the reception patio overlooking the lower resort area where we waited after registering for our room. We had arrived in the morning so our room was still occupied until noon and then had to be cleaned and prepared for us. So, we decided to get a drink and some lunch while waiting on the patio. The wait person who served us is named Sudiasih. She was excellent as a wait person. Very friendly and helpful and sweet. Couldn't have asked for better service from her. The second aspect of our review is our stay at the resort for about 8-9 days. We were very pleased with our room just outside the main pool and the fact that we had a pool associated with our patio area made our stay even more pleasurable and calming. The room itself was great and fairly large as rooms go. We did have an issue with the coffee machine. It would not make a full cup of coffee and wouldn't work properly for us so we gave up using it. We usually like to make our coffee in the a.m. before going to breakfast at other resorts so it was annoying that the machine didn't work properly for us. Also, one of the two bathroom sinks had the stopper break so a plumber had to come in to try to fix it but he didn't speak English and failed to fix the problem. Another person who did speak English did fix the stopper but the stopper would not open completely when I wanted to drain the sink. I had to pull it open more to let the water drain completely. I wish the grand palace of the reception area had ceiling fans lowered enough to provide some relief with the excessive heat and humidity in that vast area. I personally felt the various restaurants were a little too full of themselves as the food provided wasn't that great. Making reservations at the Japanese and Koral restaurants, for example, seemed silly given the food wasn't that great. We've had better in our home town of Boston. The Pala restaurant on the roof deck had great ambiance but again the food was meh. The ground floor restaurant of the Pala for dinner offered a vegetarian rice and veggie dish but I was shocked at how little veggies were in the rice. We felt like throughout our stay we were constantly handing over our credit card to pay for a bottle of water and a second cocktail because the resort does not offer a fully inclusive meal and drink package. Yes, I know, we should pay for what we consume but while breakfast and dinner were paid for, any tiny bit of extra was charged. Seemed silly to constantly have to settle a bill for every meal. I wish the Apurva would have provided a better meal and drink plan. One final comment regarding the resort is they are isolated in that there is nothing else to walk to from there so you have to hire a driver to go anywhere nearby, if there is anything to go to near the property. Because of the isolated location, the resort provides almost NO entertainment and shopping outside of the resort shop and some musicians that sing contemporary American pop tunes. We were bored. No fire dance, no traditional Balinese performance, not much going on for excitement. The beach wasn't anything to write home about. A sign at the beach cautions about jellyfish and sea urchins. Great. You can't really go out into the water much because of the coral and/or seaweed very close to the shore. This is common with Bali beaches at Nusa Dua. On our next visit to Bali in the future, we will stay at a resort outside of Ubud. I regretted our Nusa Dua stay. It's too far from the action. Changgu, Seminyak, Ubud, those are the areas to be in when visiting Bali. Next to the Apurva resort is another property where the forest and vegetation was completely razed obviously to make way for another resort. The macaque monkeys that lived in that area were pushed out of their habitat and forced to move to the Apurva property and Mulia or other properties nearby. One evening we chose to eat dinner at the Atelier and were shocked that the monkeys were actually on the green roof of the hotel and could jump down onto your table and grab your food or whatever. As we sat on the patio we could see them just above us. I felt really bad for them as their habitat was destroyed and they were not relocated to inland forests where they would have access to food, water and shelter.  The Atelier was a joke. This so called restaurant offered only tapas and mezze which wasn't much in quantity. the wait person said we'd only need about four pieces per selected plate. Well, I was hungry after that "dinner." The Atelier where you need a reservation, you could also be seated inside instead of the patio and it was disgusting inside that space. Spray paint cans on a massive table and some awful artwork on the walls like an messy artists studio as well as spray painted mess on the windows facing the patio. The room made no sense whatsoever. What the hell was it supposed to be? A woman sat at the piano once again singing American pop tunes. That restaurant needs an overhaul!

We had a great dinner at Chinese restaurant Bai Du.

All the staff were very friendly. Especially our server, Ayu, was so kind.
Another female server (I'm sorry I don't know her name) who can speak Korean also greeted us warmly.

The atmosphere was so nice, and their kindness was excellent.

We ate hotpot, and they served unlimited vegetables. Desert was included.

I strongly recommend this restaurant.

They have a Korean-speaking staff member!

She was very pregnant, and she even read the Korean in the children's book. (She was incredibly kind, but I don't know her name, so I apologize to her.)

We went with two children and adults, and ordered two sets of hot pot for 680,000 won each.
We ordered five plates of dim sum, one pork belly dish, two bowls of rice, water, and tea, and the total came to 2,980,000 won. We were very satisfied.

Our kids love it, so we ordered a lot. The hot pot comes with unlimited refills for vegetables and noodles, so if you use it wisely, you can enjoy it on a budget.
Also, a bottle of water was 70,000 won and green tea was 60,000 won, but they kept refilling it. Ordering green tea and asking for an iced cup to keep it cool is a great way to save money. A bottle of water wasn't enough for a family of four, so we ordered more, but the adults had tea, so it was more than enough.

More than anything, the staff were incredibly friendly, making our meal comfortable.
They even gave us dessert at the end of the meal, so we were very satisfied.

Oh, and since it's hot pot, you can search for your perfect sauce and enjoy endless amounts. My husband made a sauce he learned from a Chinese friend, and we almost exploded. Highly recommended!

Our stay at the Kempinski was nothing short of spectacular. We stayed at the junior suite with private pool and beautiful gardens view! From the moment we arrived, the staff exuded warmth and professionalism, ensuring every aspect of our visit was seamless and luxurious.

The Cliff Lounge was the highlight of our dining experiences. Perched with breathtaking views of the ocean, the ambiance was both serene and sophisticated. The cuisine was exquisite, with each dish crafted to perfection. A special mention goes to Amar, the assistant chef, whose attention to detail and passion for culinary excellence elevated our meal to an unforgettable experience. His recommendations were spot-on, and he even took the time to share some of the inspirations behind the dishes, adding a personal touch that made the evening truly special.

The room service was impeccable, arriving promptly with beautifully presented meals and thoughtful touches. The housekeeping team ensured our room was always pristine, and the luxurious amenities provided added to the overall comfort.

The concierge team was phenomenal, going above and beyond to make our stay memorable. Whether it was arranging excursions, providing local insights, or accommodating last-minute requests, their dedication was unparalleled.

The beaches at Kempinski were a dream come true. With crystal-clear waters and soft white sands, it was the perfect place to relax and unwind. The beachside service was excellent, with staff readily available to bring refreshments or set up loungers to our liking.

Kempinski truly embodies the essence of luxury and exceptional hospitality. We left feeling rejuvenated and eager to return for another unforgettable stay. Highly recommended!

Our stay at Apurva Kempinski was wonderful. Facilities are exceptional and the staff were extremely accommodating and absolutely wonderful with our children especially the guys working by the kids pool (Tommy and ?)! We stayed in a 2 bedroom villa - it was lovely, extremely comfortable beds and pillows, but the rooms are on two separate levels which is not ideal when you have very small children who have to navigate down a large staircase if they wake in the night. The staff kindly lent us a baby monitor but it didn't make noise on our phones to alert us when the kids moved!
 they heavily dose the kids pool with chlorine and there were undissolved chemicals that you could see on some days. it really hurt our daughters eyes and our eyes to swim without goggles. The Japanese restaurant Izakaya was amazing - food and staff were great.  The choice of restaurants was great. We had booked through Luxury Escapes and found that although lunch/ or dinner was included in our package, they do limit the options you are offered at all restaurants (noting Koral is not included). The options were ok but it was a bit disappointing that they didn't disclose this at the time of booking. The Reef Beach Club was great for children - a very relaxed vibe and good live music. The size of the meals was massive though, we couldn't ever get through 3 courses - would have rathered be offered all options but have the monetary value be capped so we didn't waste so much food. Same at Pala, the entrees were so massive (basically same as the mains)! My partner has a big appetite but even he found that it was too much food.  The service is a bit slow as well which is fine if you don't have children but when you are rushing to get them home to bed it got a little frustrating at times. The  Jalak Kids Club facilities were great but the organised activities weren't great - they didn't really 'facilitate' them - a bit more effort to hold coordinated activities and get all kids involved at the same time might help to get kids to feel comfortable to be left in the kids club without a parent (this should be the point). The cost of the cupcake decorating and donut decorating at $25 is a bit of a rort especially since it took about 5 minutes. 
The gym and yoga classes were great as were the massages at the Spa! 
The ladies in red were very willing to help with bookings and questions we had via WhatsApp which was really helpful. All in all a great stay but definitely some room for improvement.

While this is certainly a five star hotel it was not a standout. We will be trying elsewhere next time. 

The stunningly grand entrance and lobby level was certainly a feature. It's an enourmous hotel of 475 well-appointed rooms and extensive grounds.

The general service was professional and friendly. It was not personal, which is understandable being such a huge hotel but still very different to our favourite Bali hotel, The Legian. The concierge staff in person and the "Lady in Red" by whatsapp (who turned out to be a new person each day) were somewhat helpful but I expected them to be much more so. They were slow to reply, not that confident in suggestions and I had to follow things up myself on multiple occasions.

The rooms were beautifully appointed and spacious, with bespoke lights and joinery in a contemporary Asian style. The beds were very comfortable and our ground floor interconnecting rooms came with acess to a semi-private pool.
Room service was good but could have been better service.

The breakfast buffet was welcoming, a little formal, had an extensive food offering and a la carte. The coffee is the only improvement needed. Service was great.

The pools were okay but not amazing, not great pool service at the main pool or kids pool due to loose, aged decking with pool chairs looking into each other rather than out at a nice view. No music or atmosphere, and annoyingly, no direct pool service (you could go to the staff to ask for extra towels or place an order but they did not come to you for some reason).

The water slides were a hit with our kids and the reason we chose this hotel over some of the other Nusa Dua options.

We later realised the area with complete pool service qnd large daybeds was at Reef.  
Reef also has a large restaurant which we ate a few dinners at, as they offered a great varied menu of western food, quality kids meals and excellent service.

The other hotel restaurant we loved was Izakaya with delicious, clean and fresh Japanese food. They gave attentive service in an elegant but warm atmosphere. Our kids and allergies were all happily catered for.

The section of ocean in front of the Kempinski was not nice. We were disappointed to find plastic litter floating throughout, encountered some dangerous sea life, sections of fake sand, a strong seaweed smell and many times of day with low tide where it couldn't be used at all.

The gym and kids club were clean but had a strange smell that was off-putting.
The gym classes I asked about were booked out a few days in advance. So I booked the paid pilates reformer class for the future date and had a great intermediate-advanced instructor named Rana.

The kids club was almost empty however you still had to book in advance and even pay for half the activities.

Personally I like having more flexibility on holidays and would have liked more options of drop-in classes or things I could book at short notice for myself and the kids. I certainly didn't expect to pay extra for them.

In summary, while it is a great hotel but I expected more for the price and reputation, from the service, the pool areas and the kids club which would have made us come back again. We'll definitely come again for dinner though, if we return to Nusa Dua in future.

The Apurva Kempinski is a huge and luxurious hotel. 

We were lucky enough to stay in one of the Cliff Oceanview Suites and we got a whole package of "bonuses" with that: a separate breakfast zone (available till 12:00!), a separate lounge & reception area, tea-time snacks and drinks served every day and cocktail hour at the rooftop restaurant/bar. 

The views from our room were simply stunning - a vast panorama of the ocean and tropical trees, def in top 3 best views we've ever had. 

The room has nice amenities like a Toto toilet with automatic heating, cleaning, etc. The bed is large and comfortable. 

The private pool at the suite had an amazing view as well. 

Hotel has a couple of restaurants: 
- Japanese one is amazing, a must-visit. 

- Reef Beach Club has great dining options and is on the beach, makes for a great dinner with a view. 

- The rooftop restaurant is great as well. 

- Koral restaurant is sadly disappointing. It's "inside" the aquarium, so you are surrounded by different sea animals, which is a great experience. But the food (note that we are vegetarian, it's a seafood restaurant but they do offer vegetarian tasting menu) was a huge letdown. At all the other restaurants, we've had amazing dinners, but here, the food was simply not worth the price and nowhere near "fine dining" - it felt "overengineered" but lacked basic taste foundations. Wine pairing was dissapointing as well, as it felt it lacked some love - we didn't hear anything interesting about the wines or why they were paired with food. Go just for the views or maybe sea-food is better. (The assistant manager did try to amend our experience by inviting us to private cocktails, but unluckily, we got extremely sick and couldn't accept the invitation). 

However, what's truly best about Kempinski is its service: 
- Everyone is kind, smiling, and super helpful with anything you need. 
- Staff at all the restaurants was also smiling and helpful and even shared some interesting stories about Indonesia 
- What REALLY stands out is the concierge desk. Gaby and Jessica went truly above and beyond to make our stay memorable, and they succeeded. We felt super taken care of and there was nothing that couldn't be arranged. Huge shoutout to this team, they made our stay magical. 

Overall, if you want a truly luxurious experience in Bali, then Kempinski is worth the price, especially the Cliff Suite, which we feel provides excellent value. Albeit the resort is really huge, it still felt cozy. (But if you don't like big resorts - it might be overwhelming for you).

As last note: one big minus for us was that they have a Chinese live-seafood restaurant with animals kept in very small spaces - it's animal cruelty.

We stayed in the Cliff Private Pool Junior Suite for 8 days.  The suite with its private pool was uncredible.  The hotel is a 5 star resort in every respect.  The staff professionalism and attention to detail was expectational.  They could not do enough to make our say more relaxing.  The onsite restaurants were quality.  Although not near the major shopping precincts it is only a short taxi trip away and does not prove a major problem.

We cannot fault the facilities, staff, food, hotel amenities.  The hotel management should be proud of their staff and the service they provide.

This place is just jaw-dropping from the moment you walk in. The one word I'd use to describe the property is "grand". First, check-in. We stayed in an ocean junior cliff suite, so we had a dedicated check-in team in the Cliff Lounge. The check-in process was more complicated than it needed to be, getting hung up on me not having the credit card I made the booking with about 8 months ago. The stay was prepaid in full, so I don't see what the issue was, but it was apparently their policy. The room was absolutely spectacular, with a beautiful view of the ocean, a very large bathroom with everything you could need, a massive and comfortable bed, and of course the large private pool. Shortly after getting to the room, someone arrived with a cake and a bottle of champagne for us (it was our honeymoon, so complimentary). The cake was chocolate and delicious, the champagne was honestly terrible and we each only had a few sips. But hey, it's the thought that counts I suppose. We then explored the massive property, from the several pools, the gym, spa, restaurants, etc. The daily breakfast at the Cliff Lounge was about 6.5/10 in quality, especially compared to some other resorts in Bali. The staff is always very friendly and attentive, the food just isn't anything special. The Cliff Lounge also has afternoon tea from 3-5 and complimentary drinks from 5-7, which we took advantage of. The light bites at tea are fine, but I wasn't expecting much (mostly desserts). The cocktails at the lounge are honestly fantastic. We had dinners at Bai Yun (average), Pala (great) and the Izakaya (underwhelming). The gym is better than most gyms you'll find at a resort and the spa was fantastic (and also massive). The architecture and details at this resort are truly next-level and had me in awe during our entire 4-day stay. Upon check-out, we again hit a snag on some prices charged to our room that were incorrect and took forever for them to fix. We were irritated how not a single person during our entire stay asked how anything was, almost as if they were programmed to not receive feedback (which seemed to be a common thing across all places we stayed in Bali). There were multiple times when we wanted to discuss an issue but were never given the opportunity. Overall, I'd say this hotel is a 7/10 (so 3.5 stars), but a 3 is too harsh so I gave it 4. I probably wouldn't return and would instead check out another resort if I came back to Nusa Dua.

We spent about ten days at the Kempinski in Bali.

The hotel is grand, luxurious and majestic (located on a hillside). 
The hotel lobby is worthy of the biggest palaces (you would spend hours walking around:-) )

The gardens are beautiful and it is ideally located on the beach, while offering very nice pools (huge by the way). The beach is very well appointed, clean with possibility to do some water sports. It should be mentioned that many facilities are present for children (playground, small pools,...);

The rooms are spacious, luxurious and very clean (with turndown service in the evening; bedding is super comfortable.

All restaurants are excellent and tasty (don't forget to book!).
Breakfast is royal (we have never seen such a great choice) and very very good quality.

The staff, in great numbers regardless of the location of the hotel, is attentive, smiling and always ready to help.

Thanks to Ardi for his sympathy at the Reef Club and above all, we would like to highlight a special mention for Aristana, our (hotel) driver and guide, who introduced us to the beauties and culture of Bali while adapting to our program with extreme kindness. Aristana made our stay the most magical:-)

In short, this hotel is just perfect for alternating visits and rest periods. We can only comment positively on the Kempinski Bali.

The only negative point, but which has nothing to do with the hotel itself is the presence of plastic waste in the sea (all over Bali) despite the best efforts of the hotel staff (5 people work continuously to remove it from sunrise until sunset!!!); 

Bali is a paradise island and a treasure trove of biodiversity, so there is an urgent need for the Balinese government to take steps to put in place an environmental policy for waste management on the island.

In conclusion, this hotel is one of the best we have visited!

It is with a heavy heart that we had to leave this place and so we will come back, for sure.....:-)

My family and I stayed at the Kempinski in January and had an unforgettable experience. We booked a beautiful pool villa, and it exceeded our expectations. The rooms were incredibly spacious, providing ample space for everyone to relax and enjoy.

One of the highlights of our stay was the staff, who were extremely attentive and went out of their way to ensure we had everything we needed. Their service truly made our stay special.

The spa is also a must visit absolutely gorgeous. 

The private pool was a fantastic feature, especially for the kids, who spent hours playing and swimming. It provided a great family-friendly environment. However, I do wish the pool had been closer to the ocean, as it would have been perfect to enjoy the sea views while swimming.

Overall, our stay at the Kempinski was delightful, and we highly recommend it to families looking for a luxurious and accommodating getaway.

This was the second leg of our three week honeymoon holiday in Bali.  The first week we stayed at a smaller Balinese owned resort, Kayon Jungle in Ubud which set the tone for our holiday.  The service was impeccable primarily because they managed to anticipate our every need before we knew we had them.  We were hoping for the same level of service and care at Kempinski.  The grounds were beautiful, stunning, until we reached our room.  We initially wanted an upgraded room but were told there was none available.  However, when I went on the official Kempinski website, a plunge pool room was available (which we were more than willing to pay for).  Our room had a faulty AC unit, leaking vent, and poor airflow despite the maintenance team attempting to fix three different times. To rectify, front staff management kindly apologized for the inconvenience and gifted us Rose (wine) and a pastry dish.  Sadly, we could hear the plumbing from our neighbors toilet and shower through our room, which was the same for ours.  Every time we flushed the toilet we can hear it running beneath the bathroom floor. Overall, a poor design plan for plumbing.  The in room service staff were phenomenal.  They always greeted us with respect, removed their shoes before entering our room (most likely as a custom) and always made sure we weren't in need of anything else.  I can not say the same primarily for the staff at Pala and The Reef poolside service.  We added the breakfast at check in, understanding that we would sign for the bill to then be charged to our room.  Every morning, we were told that we did not have the breakfast included in our stay which we were aware of upon check in.  However, each morning we were embarrassingly told we did not have the breakfast package.  Front staff should have relayed to the Pala team that our daily breakfast were to be charged to our room as agreed to check in.   Overall staff seemed disingenuous.  Smiling courteously when approached but immediately "annoyed" as if we weren't watching.  My husband and I have never felt so uncomfortable as we did staying here, as if we were a bother, or quite frankly, they did not want to serve us.  The beach is subpar, always murky but they do try to keep the lounging areas clean.  We went to two different beach clubs in the area and the water was clear and enjoyable.  Understanding that this is a family resort, there should be an area designated for adults only.  The pool near the Reef was a prime spot to keep adults only but this was not the case.  Cocktails and drinks were sub par, Koral Restaurant was underwhelming. Out of all the restaurants, we thoroughly enjoyed Izakaya by Oku and the Pala Rooftop Bar.  We expected top tier service for such a grand "5-star" resort which was not met.  Even upon checkout, we weren't gifted anything like most high end resorts we've experienced would.  Lastly, we were told that we could not have our mini bar restocked because we've exceeded our deposit balance and that we needed to go to the front desk to rectify the balance.  That was a major inconvenience and interruption of our holiday, when in fact we were checking out the next day where we could have settled the balance then.  I have to mention the spa experience as well.  Our technicians were phenomenal both days and the treatments themselves were great.  Unfortunately, the ball was dropped again.  Our two day spa package came with a lunch.  It seemed as if our lunch reservation wasn't booked.  Spa management brought us down to the restaurant and reassured us that we would be seated within half an hour.  We waited 45 min before getting up and speaking with the restaurant staff who seemed to have forgotten we were waiting.  Frustrated, we asked for the meals to be sent to our room which took another 30 min to arrive.  If I could rate the hotel as far as the grounds, cleanliness, pools, that would be an easy 10/10.  But when it comes to service, this is where Kempinski failed.

We stayed here in April, we stayed in the lagoon access room for 7 days. (Room 3137)

Positives 

1. Hotel itself

The hotel is just awesome, it is a big hotel but it is stunning. The lobby area is just beautiful and all the amenities and grounds are just so photogenic.

2. Beach

Whilst the beach was good, I would not say great by Australian standards, but when compared to surrounding properties, the beach is really good. Whilst I say this, the beach is usable, clean and non dangerous. We did go to the Hilton and the beach there was just dangerous.

3. Room

We stayed in Deluxe lagoon access room. Whilst the pool villa would have been nice and we would probably choose one of these next time, the lagoon access was great. The room amenities, the space, location and access to the pool was great. Whilst you share the pool/lagoon with other rooms, on our stay, no one in the adjoining rooms used the pool, we had it to ourselves. We were located on the southern wing facing away from the main pool. I was a bit sceptical at first but this was great as the there was a great combination of sun and shade during the day so you did not get too burnt. We thought our room was maybe one of the better located rooms on the lagoon as we had a greater pool area directly out the front of room.

4. Dining

The breakfast was great, room service was great, the restaurants were great. We could not ask for better dining. We did not want to risk Bali belly so we decided to eat at the hotel only, and we can say we were the only ones on the plane that were not queuing for the toilet :) You do need to do Koral, it is a must do (expensive but worth it - we did similar dining experience in Guam and this was better) TIP - I tried to book online before we went but was booked out, I went to the restaurant at lunch opening and asked for reservation and told we were staying at hotel and got in that night. 

5. Staff

They are just awesome, we normally travel to Thailand (land of similes) and Vietnam which have great friendly people. The staff were credit to the hotel. Normally I would wake up before 6am and do some walking/exercising, and at that time the gardeners were out and even they were happy to say hello and have a chat. The staff were just as great as any other hotel we have been too.

Negatives

To be honest there are not a lot of negatives. The main negative, which to be honest is not the hotels fault, is the actions/behaviour of other guests. As I said I got up early and went for a walk along the beach/up the stairs/other hotels, and by about 7am (when staff were setting up the pool lounges) people were already reserving the lounges by putting personal items there. One morning I used some lounges at the front of the main pool (the prized location), the lounges  on the side were reserved from just before 7:00am but the people did not arrive till after 11:30, just totally rude and inconsiderate for other guests. It would be good if there was a 30 min time limit for unattended lounges on the beach, reef club and around the pool (just a suggestion)

Summary 

We went to other hotels in Nusa Dua for evening drinks and intended to have dinner there, but we always came back to the Kempinski for dinner. The hotel is the best hotel Nusa Dua, so don’t hesitate booking, you will not be disappointed.

A perfect hotel for kids friendly staycation. With its private beach, many activities to do during our stay. Such as canoeing and beach camel riding. The services are perfect, they served each guest individually, that make us feel special. The staffs are friendly and never forget to greet the guest every time we pass by. Our 2 rooms are spacious and luxurious with traditional touch. The breakfast serve so many various meals include the most unique ever, honey in its authentic hexagon beehive form. Stroll around the large area of the hotel by easily using buggy car. No worries for feeling thirsty, because they serve infuse water around the pool. Many instagramable photo spot we can take include the Aquarium Restaurant. 

Here are the detail story

When we arrived, all cars have free vallet service. Entering the lobby, sparkling floor and huge ‘gebyok’ (javanese gazebo) make it looks so luxurious. During check in time, they gave us a traditional welcome drink and a wet towel, and kids activity book with coloring pencil for my little brother. 

Because the hotel is very large, to go to our room there is a staff with traditional dress assigned to accompany and show us the way to our room. She explains all about hotel facilities and activities all the way down to our room. Unlike other hotels, all rooms in Kempinski Bali, located at the lower level of the lobby, due to Bali’s special regulation that buildings are not allowed to be higher than Pura Besakih (the largest temple in Bali)

Entering the room, we easily put and organize our luggage in a small walk in closet, and rilex for a while in a fancy sofa and watch the tv, or enjoying the outdoor scenery from our balcony.
The wide bathroom devide shower and the smart closet area in 2 little glass room. While the rest are for bath tub and 2 wastafel area.

In the Kempinski private beach, we can ride a camel, canoeing and swimming. They make sea dam, to keep the beach swimming area stay shallow and safe for the kids. From the beach, my little brother can run freely in the grass field before going to the infinity and kids swimming pool. 

After swimming, my little brother go to the kids club for indoor and outdoor playground. We also make a reservation for kids aquarium restaurant tour tomorrow.

For breakfast, there are 2 restaurants we can choose. We choose pool view restaurant rather than bay view restaurant, because we had aquarium tour to catch while having breakfast.
The Aquarium restaurant is very beautiful with so many fish swimming around. The breakfast provide western, japanesse, indian, turkish, and indonesian food include jamu, pure honey and traditional snacks.

Surprisingly nice stay despite the huge complex size (it's a huge hotel) - let me break it down:
Length of stay: 4 nights
Welcome: It's a huge place - literally. We were quite flabbergasted about the dimensions of the hotel (especially coming from a small boutique hotel in Ubut). You enter in a truly breathtaking lobby with beautiful art, sculptures and reflecting floors. Very impressive indeed. We had a very warm welcome from the staff. That's a good start.
Room: We had a nice spacious gardenview room with a big comfortable bed. Nice bathroom. The room was quiet and always clean. 
Staff: Very friendly and helpful. They seemed like efficient bees taking care of guests and property. Some guest groups never learned respect and behaviour and left a mess everywhere - the staff cleaned after them silently and effectively. 
Food: We loved the "Reef" Restaurant on the property. Surprisingly good meat and fish - nice atmosphere in the evening with pool and sea view.  The Japanese Restaurant was also quite good (a little cold atmosphere). The breakfast area was a little too sterile and much too loud for my taste. However, the quality and selection of the breakfast buffet is exceptional. 
Pool: Very nice pools, clean and welcoming. Heated water :-) A real treat. 
Outside toilets: Always clean with nice exotic soaps 
Beach: Nice sand beach for Bali standards, clean, but don't expect the Maldives
Internet: We had wifi connection at any time. I only used it for emails and some YouTube videos. So I can't say how it handles more intensive bandwidth demands. There were plenty of Instagram girlies changing outfits all day long and uploading stuff. So I guess the wifi is fast enough also for that. 
Massage/SPA: We didn't take advantage of the facilities but the brochure looked very classy. 
Overall it was a satisfying stay. It's not cheap but we got value for the money. I guess we will come back.

So before I begin my glowing review of this property let me get my two "not great" comments out of the way. 
- I booked this reservation in October and requested a room with two double beds ( apparently an odd request) and was told I would need to confirm closer to arrival to check availability. I booked for arrival a day early because my flight was due in the morning and check-in is not till 4pm. After flying for 20+hrs I did not want to delay access to my room. Despite several email requests within a week of arrival I was never able to confirm either  notation of the early arrival nor the bed requests. When I arrived they still had difficulty finding my reservation. It worked out, but too much stress. 
- The arrival pastries they provide must be eaten the same day ... if not housekeeping will take it away. Not the fruit though, which was interesting. Also housekeeping will remove the wash cloths and not replace them. 

Now the good part. This hotel, once you get access is amazing. The grounds are huge, the decor phenomenal, the rooms airy and comfortable, the breakfast dining area spacious, near the pool with awesome views. The pool is great too ... but the SUN will burn you to a crisp if you are not careful. Fortunately they have well placed planters, umbrellas and shady spots to come in from the harsh sun. The beach looks lovely ... but not really for swimming too rough.  And as the property is huge... they have carts available to shuttle you around to the beach, the pool, the breakfast dining area. 

And they have a spa .... yes you deserve a treatment and you will love the pampering, the strong hands of your masseuse and the ambience in the treatment rooms. If I were staying longer in Bali I would only want to do it at Apurva and spend more time exploring the hotel and trying other restaurants. It's a long way to get there... but you will love it once you've arrived.  ( just bring your own washcloths)

I recently stayed at the Kempinski Bali and overall, I have mixed feelings about my experience. One aspect that stood out to me was the size of the hotel. While some may appreciate the grandeur and extensive facilities, I personally found it to be too big for my liking. It felt overwhelming and lacked the intimate and cozy atmosphere that I associate with Bali.

Additionally, the hotel design and decor did not fully capture the essence of Bali. The architecture and interior felt more like the airport in Dubai and lacked the cultural charm that I was hoping for. I had expected to be surrounded by traditional Balinese elements and experience a sense of local culture, but instead, I felt like I could have been in ANY luxury resort destination.

On the positive side, the hotel did offer a wide range of amenities and facilities. There were several pools, restaurants, and bars to choose from, ensuring that guests had plenty of options for dining and entertainment. The rooms were spacious and well-appointed, providing a comfortable place to retreat to after a day.

The service at the Kempinski Bali was great, with friendly and attentive staff members who were always full of smiles. They were quick to respond to any requests or inquiries, and I felt well taken care of throughout my stay. However, I couldn't help but feel that the impersonal nature of such a large hotel impacted the level of personalisation as you hardly get to know that one bartender that mixes your drinks, or that one receptionist that helps you around that you experience at smaller boutique hotels in Bali. I have never even met our concierge - only communicated with her via WhatsApp. But I guess for the hotel that size they do need A LOT of staff. 

One aspect of my stay at the Kempinski Bali that I must commend is the quality of the food. Despite my reservations about the hotel's size and lack of authentic Bali feel, the dining experience exceeded my expectations.

The hotel boasted a variety of restaurants and dining options, each offering a unique culinary experience. From international cuisines to traditional Balinese dishes, there was something to satisfy every palate. The chefs demonstrated their skills and expertise in creating delicious and flavorful meals.

The presentation of the food was also impressive. Each dish was beautifully plated, showcasing the attention to detail and artistic flair of the kitchen staff. It was evident that they took pride in their work and strived to provide a memorable dining experience for guests.

The restaurants staff were attentive prompt in their service and made sure that we had an enjoyable and satisfying dining experience. 

Ban Yan is a Chinese restaurant at Kempinski, we had the pleasure of dining. To my surprise, it turned out to be one of the highlights of our culinary experience and, in my opinion, one of the most underestimated restaurants in the hotel. I felt that Ban Yan was somewhat underrated compared to the other dining options at the hotel. It may not have received as much attention or recognition, but it definitely deserves to be celebrated for its outstanding Chinese cuisine. It has exceeded my expectations and proved to be a hidden gem within the hotel. If you have a craving for authentic and delicious Chinese food, I highly recommend giving this restaurant a try. You won't be disappointed by the flavors, presentation, and overall dining experience it has to offer.

Dining at Koral restaurant in the Kempinski Bali aquarium offers a unique ambiance, but the food is not exceptional. I mean it was decent, but not necessarily better than what you can find at other, more affordable restaurants within the hotel. While the ambiance is unique and worth experiencing, keep in mind that you may be paying more for the setting rather than the exceptional quality of the food. The experience is worth trying for the novelty though.

While the Kempinski has many positive aspects, there are a few limitations that some guests may find disappointing. Firstly, the hotel does not have a deep pool, which could be a drawback for those who enjoy swimming or diving in deeper waters. The deepest they have is 1.2 meters. 

Additionally, I was surprised to learn that the hotel this size doesn’t have a library. Library would have been a valuable amenity for guests to borrow books and enjoy reading by the pool or in a cozy setting.
