Its absence is a missed opportunity for relaxation and fostering a sense of community among guests.

Another potential drawback is the hotel's policy regarding camera usage. Due to privacy reasons, guests are only allowed to use cameras on their phones and not separate cameras. We were not even allowed to use a kids Polaroid camera. While this policy is in place to respect the privacy of other guests, it may be seen as restrictive for those who prefer using standalone cameras for capturing high-quality photos or videos.

In conclusion, while the Kempinski Bali offers a range of amenities and comfortable rooms, I found it to be too big and lacking the authentic Bali feel that I was hoping for. If you prefer a larger, more sprawling resort experience, this may be the perfect fit for you. However, if you are seeking a more intimate and culturally immersive stay, I would recommend considering other options.

My husband and I had a fantastic time. This was my second visit, and his first, and I was once again wowed by the beautiful resort, delicious food and drinks, as was he. We were particularly impressed with the amazing service we received everywhere in the resort and most notably in the Cliff Lounge, the highlight of our stay!

The resort itself is gorgeous, and the facilities are plentiful and exceedingly well-maintained. The gym is large and well equipped, the pools (main pool, Cliff Lounge pools, private suite pool) were clean and a fantastic temperature, our room was huge and well laid out with fantastic amenities and an insanely comfortable bed, and the massages we had in the spa were some of the best we've ever had! And my favourite part of my stay, the dining options. All of the restaurants we tried (Selasar Deli, Koral, Pala, Reef) offered comprehensive menus with lots of choices and the food and drinks at all of them were high quality; every dish we had was delicious, and the service and atmosphere at all venues was exceptional. Breakfast and afternoon tea at Cliff Lounge were excellent as well, again with so much variety and of a high quality, and with fabulous service by Amar and his team of wonderful staff.  

In particular I was impressed by the extra touches...the corner room on a high floor, which I had requested in my booking and which had a lovely sun balcony and also a small view of the sea, the late check out that was happily arranged for us, and all of the help making (and adjusting) our restaurant bookings, including getting us into Koral at the last minute. We really appreciated all of this, and is why I have and will keep returning to the Apurva whenever I visit Bali!

The Apurva Kempinski Bali, is what I would call the definition of a wow factor! 

I arranged for a full day at this breath taking and incredible resort and it far from exceeded my expectations on every and all levels. I truly was completely blown away from start to finish in every way possible. This resort not only has the extreme wow factor on decor, design and overall architecture but the service levels were equally as impeccable and impressive. 

My best friend brought her 5 year old son and nanny and the hotel arranged a day bed for them in the kids area which has a large pool with water slides. So they spent the whole day having fun and eating by the pool.

We started our day with the incredible Sunday Brunch at the Pala Restaurant & Rooftop Bar. This is by far the best Sunday brunch I have ever had. The huge selection of items and various cuisines offered were incredible and the visual of the entire buffet was stunning. The quality and taste off food was exceptional and will be hard to beat.

We then spent several hours treating ourselves to some treatments in the lavish and luxurious spa which again did not disappoint. The decor and design is truly breath taking from the minute you enter. The individual spa suites are on another level and the treatments are equally as impressive and relaxing. I am a spa lover and really loved the massage oil that was used which turns out to be made specially by them. I loved it so much I even bought some. This just shows the level of attention to detail this hotel has gone to impress and deliver to its guests.

We finished of our day of luxury and pampering in the Bai Yum Hot Pot and Dim Sum restaurant which was the perfect way to end our day. We had such a huge brunch that this was just what we needed for dinner. Just like the rest of the day the restaurants style and food offerings exceeded our expectations and were simply delicious.

I highly recommend a trip to this luxury resort on your next trip to Bali. I will be back in January to celebrate my 50th Birthday as I know it will be another amazing experience.

Before my wife and I decided to held our wedding ceremony in Apurva Kempinski Bali, we have browsed more than 60 other options in Bali. We have never been to Bali and I am glad that our hunch was right. Apurva is absolutely the best accommodation experience we had and the best wedding dream that could possibly come true.

When I saw the photos of the hotel, I was stunned by the majestic building by the cliff, and I am sure that is my dream venue to have our wedding overseas. Right after I made the deposit, my wife told me Apurva Kempinski Bali was the hotel to host the 2022 G20 Summit and I know the reason the second I arrive the lobby.

The Cliff Building:
	1. All my guests including myself were stunned by every part of the hotel. The interior design is filled with Balinese culture and color. There are literally hundreds of photo taking spots in the hotel.
	2. If you are not impressed by the pendopo-style lobby, check out the Indian Ocean view with a few steps forward to the open space expanded from the lobby. I am no architect with professional criticism but a regular would definitely enjoy the sunrise, sunset and breathtaking sunny scene there.
	3. Don't miss the walkway from the center of the building. Standing there and looking at the scene of the resort, the ocean and the sky make me realize how tiny we human are.

Environment:
	1. Everywhere filled with green. It pulled me out from the daily stress even I just planned a comprehensive wedding.
	2. At the center of the resort, there is a huge swimming area. It is not a simple pool but designed with layers to fit the needs of families, kids, couples or even a single guy who just want to take a break. Drinks and towel is served next to the swimming area at multiple spots so I don't need to take much belongings with me.
	3. I wanted to get to the beach on my last day of staying but didn't want to go out of Apurva, so I sunbathed at the beach inside the resort area. Sunlounger is everywhere on the beach. I ordered a local coconut and read beside the other guests who were playing volleyball, soccer, swimming and riding camels on the beach.

Facilities:

	1. I never tried a spa treatment before so I cannot compare, but the spa experience and massage I had in Apurva was absolutely luxury. All my wedding guests had it and liked it.
	2. The Cliff Lounge is exclusive to Cliff room tenants. I got premium service at the Cliff Lounge which locates at the 9th and 10th floor. You'll get free drinks and free tea buffet which the food served changes everyday. I also got exclusive pool and sunbathing area there.
	3. I didn't have time to attend the gym room but my groomsmen enjoyed it. They even bumped up right before the wedding ceremony to look good in shape at the last mile.

Room:

	1. I've got a Cliff Room with private pool in the room. The room view is astonishing and I enjoyed the resort and ocean view from the private pool with my wife. 
	2. Not only room, but basically all the toilets of Apurva are mechanical - flush, clean, turn up the lids with a control panel. Very fancy.
	3. I had welcoming fruit and local dessert waiting for me at the room. It is very thoughtful because I was hungry after a long flight while I didn't get the chance to eat on the road.

Service:

	1. The security check in Apurva starts from the entrance. Everyone and every vehicle has to go through security check before entering the premise. It makes me feel absolutely safe.
	2. The spots within the hotel are distanced because the area so big and they have buggies to carry the visitors from point-to-point which is very convenient.
	3. Every staff, I mean every, always put on a smile to welcome us, even we just met on a hallway. They proactively reach out to help. If you need anything, they are just one call away from dialing "0" and they've got 3 phones in a single room.
	4. Their staff is flexible - I requested them to check-in quickly for my wedding guests because we needed to go out for dinner. All we were asked to do was sending the passport image over WhatsApp and they prepared everything in no time.
	5. When we checked out, they called to the room and asked if their staff can help us to send our luggage to the lobby, so that we don't have to take them by ourselves. 

Dining:
	1. Koral Restaurant - Must try restaurant in Bali. It is an aquarium restaurant with fusion food. Tried the Koral Gourmet set and I recommended to my wedding guests who love it too. The grilled lobster and Waynu were really impressive. Everyone got the chance to take a picture with the diver who know how to interact with the guests.
	2. Pala Restaurant - As a Cliff room tenant, I could go to the Cliff Lounge for a relative private breakfast buffet, but the one in Pala Restaurant is mighty impressive. Japanese, Indian, Indonesian, Chinese, you name it - the cuisine type is so diverse, and they serve some Indonesian drinks called Jamu. It is not for everyone to be honest, but definitely worth a try if you come to Bali. Friendly advise, if you plan to stay for couple days, don't rush to try everything in one breakfast, it's just too much.
	3. Reef Beach Club - Tried their signature Reef Surf n Turf dish, which is an Indonesian-style grilled seafood platter. It fed 5 of us and we knew if we didn't try the grilled seafood while staying in Bali, we were going to regret it.

Wedding:
	1. It took me 7 months to plan my wedding - and I could not do it without the event team of Apurva. They are detail-minded, proactive, friendly, punctual and trustworthy. Here I especially want to thank Ririn and Elsa who have accompanied my wife and I throughout the preparation process and the big day. It was not easy to plan an oversea wedding - it's all based on trust, and I am glad that I found a world class team and venue to held my wedding.
	2. The Ocean Chapel ceremony was simple but grand. I didn't need much decoration - a sunny day was what I needed because the sunlight was the perfect element to light up the window and bamboo chapel. The Chapel itself the perfect background for photo taking from outside.
	3. I had the receptionist dinner in Kimaya Chapel which was very grand in pendopo-style interior design. When we rolled up the curtain of the window, the resort night view became the background and we were speechless. Their wedding package covered everything I needed and the crews were very professional and caring during the dinner.

Summary: The second we finished the wedding and went back to our room, my wife and I both said that we want to suggest come here to have their wedding. We agreed this was the best decision we have ever made to choose Apurva Kempinski Bali as our wedding venue. All the guests enjoyed the stay without a single negative comment. Once again, I want to thank Apurva Kempinski and the team who made our dreams come true.  I will definitely come back again in my next visit to Bali.

It’s hard to know where to start when talking about this hotel so I will start at the lobby when you enter this hotel. It’s absolutely stunning. Jaw dropping snd difficult to photograph or even describe. If you decide not to stay at this hotel you should even just visit one of the amazing restaurants just do that you can experience the lobby of this hotel. It feels like entering and grand temple. Even the flooring is breathtaking, like walking on gemstones. Also the lobby (as with the whole hotel) has a quiet peaceful feeling. The hotel is very large but it always seems so peaceful- it must be to do with clever design. The staff here are amazing. Every single person that works there is friendly, smiling and couldn’t be more helpful. The restaurants are all world class and the food devine and plentiful. The underwater restaurant really was one of the most amazing experiences of my life. The chef deserves a medal! The rooms were large, spotless and well appointed. The beds were so good that I slept better here than I do at home. I have arthritis and often wake during the night. Whilst here I slept through!!! The goose down pillows and quilt felt like sleeping on a cloud. I wish I could purchase these pillows in Australia. The pool area was beautiful. The breakfast buffet was probably the best I’ve seen worldwide. I have travelled extensively but this hotel is an experience that will be hard to beat! If you like long walks on the beach this hotel may not suit you. It does have a clean beach but the sand is like quicksand making it difficult to walk far. This doesn’t really matter as it’s still lovely to look out over and it’s nicer to swim in the pool. I booked through luxury escapes as I’ve had good experiences in the past booking with this company( although it was the same price as booking direct) If I booked here again I would book directly with the hotel.

In late 2022, my wife and I came across an advertisement from VIP Traveler offering 8 nights in Bali. The package included 5 nights at the Kempinski Apurva. This resort recently hosted the G20 Summit for many leaders across the globe, so we were very excited to stay here. We arrived on a Saturday around 1 PM. We were greeted immediately upon pulling up and escorted to the Cliff Lounge for a personal check-in experience. We were met by a two-person team walking us through all of our package inclusions and took care of the typical administrative check-in paperwork. Shortly afterwards, we met with what they call “Lady in Red” (Asako Onozuka). These are personal assistants that ensure that we have a great stay and address any issues or concerns that may arise. She was very pleasant and explained to us her role and ensured us that she would be available to assist us in any way needed.

ROOM
The room for our package was their Cliffside Junior Suite with Pool (RM 2062) Seeing the room online was nothing like seeing it in person. We truly loved how the room was set up and it was larger than a typical resort room of this type elsewhere. The extra large bed was comfortable, the furnishings were very nice looking, the bathroom was very large with a soaking tub overlooking the cliff into the ocean, and the shower was extra large. Then there is our personal pool right outside of our room. This was awesome as we could just get up and soak in the pool without having to trek all the way down to the main pool or beach area. Not that we didn’t do that as well:) The room was cleaned twice a day and we were provided plenty of bottled water throughout our stay. Housekeeping did a great job in taking care of our room. We had no complaints at all about our room!

MEALS
Our package was almost full inclusive. All breakfasts, one lunch or dinner per day, access to their Cliff Lounge throughout the day for included drinks and snacks, Complimentary afternoon tea, Complimentary evening cocktail hour, and a $100 credit to use at Koral (underwater restaurant).

Breakfast is served at two locations (Pala Restaurant and Cliff Lounge). The first is Pala Restaurant which is open for all guests. Cliff Lounge is only for specific guests dependent on the room booked. We went to Pala for our first breakfast to see what that experience would be like. The buffet was plentiful and had many options for anybody and everybody who dined there for breakfast. It was very busy as this is the main breakfast area, but the food was stocked the entire time we were there and did not experience any issues. The staff were very polite and provided exceptional service. The only negative I would have to mention is that I ate entirely too much as they had a really good spread of food (LOL).

During the rest of our 5 day stay, we decided to eat breakfast at the Cliff Lounge. We felt that this venue provided a much quieter environment with the same food experience. The spread was not laid out like at the main buffet at Pala, but we were afforded the option to order any other items from a menu selection. We ordered their lobster scramble which tasted very good. Their cook to order French Toast was fantastic! The presentation was great and the taste definitely left a positive impression.

We went to Izakaya (Japanese Restaurant) for dinner at the Kempinski. We realized that this restaurant required a reservation in advance, but decide to go and ask if they had any openings. We lucked out and were able to get a table. Our included package meal at this restaurant came with an appetizer, main course, and dessert (Fixed menu). We were quite disappointed that the package restricted us to a fixed menu only. I specifically asked VIP Traveler about this and they assured me that I would be able to select from the standard menu. We ordered their Crispy Back Tiger Prawns and Wagyu Gyoza for appetizer. The main course was Tiger Prawns with soft shell crab with rice and pan seared salmon over rice. For dessert, we ordered two rosella cherry gel with mousse crumble. The food tasted good, but nothing out of the ordinary. Definitely left the restaurant wishing there was more have been experienced.

On our second night, we went to Bai Yun (Hot Pot) for dinner. This was a very good experience where we had plenty of food and assorted items for Hot Pot which made it a hands on experience. I am personally not a fan of cooking my own food at a restaurant, but this experience was a good one. The staff were very attentive and assisted us whenever we ran into any issues or confused about anything. Our assortment included chicken, beef, squid, fish, octopus, tofu, and many vegetables to cook along with the previously mentioned items. We enjoyed our dinner at Bai Yun.

On our third day, we went to the Reef Beach Club for dinner. This was probably the most relaxed location of all as it was a very casual setting. We were seating inside and provided great service from start to finish. We perused their menu and ordered Calamari, a Ceasar Salad, a linguini dish with seafood, a pasta plate with meat, and then closed it all out with their Bali Fruit dessert. All the food tasted very good and we definitely left stuffed. Out of all the restaurants, this one had the best portions. Once again, the staff were very attentive and ensured that we enjoyed our dinner.

Having had a good experience at the Reef Beach Club for dinner, we decided to try them out for lunch the following day. This time around, we ordered their popcorn chicken, nachos, Beef kabob plate with steamed rice, a chicken burger, and once again, we finished it all up with their Bali Fruit dessert. All tasted great this time around as well and we enjoyed our lunch. As we ate lunch a bit later than usual, we decided to skip dinner as we were still full from lunch.

Our final dinner at the Kempinski was at their popular restaurant called Koral. This restaurant is an underwater experience where you can see all kinds of fish swimming around you as you enjoy your dinner. Once again, the staff were very attentive and ensured the customer had a very positive experience. We ordered their 4 course meal which included a stuffed blue crab appetizer, a salmon as the second course, a crusted snapper for the third course. We were surprised to have received a complimentary half lobster along with our third course and then we finished up with dessert. Overall, it was a very good experience as well.

As we were finishing up dinner at Koral, a scuba diver was in the tank and he went to every table to be seen with all the fish swarming him and waving at the customers so that we may videotape the experience. It was a nice gesture to make this experience even better.

RESORT
The resort is magnificent! When we first saw it online, we were in awe. Understanding that marketing is the key to get people to visit the resort, looking at the resort in person did not quite hit us like it did online. Don’t get me wrong, the resort is magnificent, but having already seen it online, it made the physical sight a little less impressive to us. This did not change our thoughts of the resort we initially had though. We still enjoyed every aspect of the resort, the look, the views, the room, the pools, the overall grounds, and especially the staff. I have read other reviews stating that the experienced less than expected service, but we experienced the opposite. The service was great all around and the staff bent over backwards to please our needs. If you come to Bali expecting staff speaking perfect English, you’re definitely setting yourself up for disappointment. I truly commend all the staff that make every effort to understand and provide the customers with what they may be requesting. One must fully appreciate the locals, their culture, and their efforts to provide an exceptional experience. The Lady in Red program made our check-in process very smooth and we enjoyed everything that was included in our package deal.

VERDICT
We truly enjoyed our stay at the Kempinski Apurva. We did not have any negative experiences and had a wonderful stay. This resort is successful not because of the look, views, or facilities. The resort is successful because of the dedicated staff that make every effort possible to ensure that all customers have a positive experience. We would stay here again if the opportunity presented itself.

The Balinese architecture/craftsmanship, uninterrupted views of the Indian Ocean, and impeccable service of this grand resort will leave you breathless from the minute you step onto the property. My first 2 days I stayed in a beautiful private pool suite with floor-to-ceiling windows, and the bed and pillows were so comfortable I slept 9+ hours every night. 

As a suite guest, I had access to the cliff lounge, which gives you personalized service, and everyone there always greeted me with a smile ready to help with whatever I needed. Also, they serve complimentary afternoon tea and evening cocktail, my favorites!

On my first visit to the cliff lounge, a very polite gentleman named Wan spent a considerable amount of time answering all of my questions and helped me plan out my remaining days in Bali. He was very patient, as I had numerous questions about temples, waterfalls, restaurants, stores, orphanages, etc.

I also had requests such as floating breakfast and flowers for my bathtub, and all were executed flawlessly.  Everything was delivered at the precise time promised and the presentation was so beautiful.  At this point I was completely impressed with the service.

Lastly, I was having such an amazing time that I decided to extend my stay for 3 more nights!  I moved to a deluxe room, and although the hotel was near its full capacity, Wan made sure I got to stay in the same room for those additional 3 night. It took some juggling since it was so last minute, but he was able to make it work.

Thank you, Apurva and Wan, for unforgettable memories and the most luxurious R&R one can hope for when away from the comfort of your own home.

Om Swastyastu Bali!

It’s been around a year since my horrible stay at kempinski Bali. It’s still upsetting for me to remember.

On July 2022. I booked Ocean front private pool suite for 2 nights. All fully paid at the time of booking and I booked directly through the hotel website. 
When we checked in, the receptionist told us that we’ve been upgraded to ‘prestige suite’. We weren’t so sure but thought okay we’ll see first. 

The first red flag was that check in time was supposed to be at 3 PM. The room wasn’t ready until it was closer to 6 PM. I kept calling them to check if the room was ready. I have a young child that I really hoped we could settle in soon. 

After that long wait, only to find out that prestige suite is huge but very far from the beach and not with a sea view. I didn’t want to take it, it was not suitable for my family. Having a kid with us, my family wanted to have a room that is close to the beach and have a sea view, just as the room we booked and fully paid. We asked them to place us in the ocean front room. They didn’t have it available, turned out they were overbooked. 
Imagine paying fully non refundable rate in such expensive hotel, only to find out they can’t provide if for you. 
They offered me another sea view room, but it’s far, it’s not ocean front. I decided to take it instead of the prestige suite. I had to wait for it another hour for the room to be cleaned.
Upon realising myself (they never took the initiative to tell me) that room is actually cheaper than the ocean front that I paid, I asked them about it and all they offered me is free dinner vouchers in their restaurant, instead of refunding  the price differences.
I decided to take it, my family was so exhausted, we just wanted to settle. 

In the early morning after we had dinner in their restaurant, my husband and my daughter started experiencing stomachaches, and vomiting. Symptoms of food vomiting. I tried to reach guest service if they have a medical team to come to check. I explained them the situation. Guess what? The guest service team’s initial response was something like yes we have but there’ll be charges for it. 
Excuse me? We as hotel guests experiencing possible food poisoning from eating in one of your restaurants, and your immediate reaction is that we have to pay for the hotel’s doctors to check on us? 
I talked to them and after talking to manager on duty whatsoever they said yes they’ll send their medical team without charges.
The medical team was lovely and gave medicines to help.

On the last day, I moved to another room in this same hotel just for 1 night. And I forgot 1 small bag in the previous room, it was nothing too valuable inside, but I asked them to check for me. Asked the guy who helped to move our luggages if he can ask the housekeeping or someone to check it. He said they’d check and get back to me. No one got back to me. 
I called housekeeping to ask if anything left. Again, they said they’d call me back. And again, no one got back to me whether they found the bag or not. No one was bothered really. 

On the day of our check out, I was given a bill for our consumption. Thankfully, I checked them thoroughly, they inputted one amount of restaurant consumption that I didn’t recognise. And they had to check it for a while, wasting my time again. To find out that yeah, it wasn’t our bill, it belonged to the previous guests in our room. 

All these things, and the general responsiveness was slow. Sometimes, would take a few calls in order to reach the guest service. The WhatsApp number for restaurant reservations took hours to reply. Can’t believe this is called a five star hotel. 

It seemed like the hotel was too crowded during that season and this hotel is huge that it was too big for them to handle. 

The worst I’ve experienced in a hotel. 
I’d advise, in Bali, stay elsewhere! 
There are hundreds, if not thousands of great hotels in Bali. From five stars to the modest ones, that are eagerly waiting to serve guests with amazing hospitality.
And in Bali, where it is loved by many for its hospitality, Apurva kempinski really did not represent that to me and my family. 

Btw, all these problems above, I had shared them to their General Manager after our stay. After long wait of his response, the one we got was just a very generic response such as apologies for those things that happened during our stay. 
None of the issues were addressed specifically to show that he had actually cared about our experiences in the hotel.

Saya dan keluarga berlibur dan menginap di Kempinski pada bulan Juli 2023 kami menempati 2 kamar mulai dari tgl 23 Juli - 28 Agustus, terlihat perbedaan antara Hotel ini dengan Resort “RC” yg tetletak bersebelahan dgn Hotel ini yang biasa kami menginap disana.

“ Resort “ adalah dimana staff nya sangat memperhatikan kenyamanan guest nya selama tinggal disana. 

Kempinski adalah sekedar Hotel bintang lima dengan seluruh kemegahan fasilitasnya yg mementingkan jualan makanan dan minuman dengan segala peraturan nya.

Itulah yg kami rasakan selama tinggal di sana, bukan merasa seperti “ Home” yg hangat dan nyaman.

Kami sangat menyarankan staff yg di pool harus banyak di training kembali karena di tempat inilah guest banyak menghabiskan waktu mereka pada siang dan sore hari. Kami melihat malah anak2 yg sangat muda dan belum berpengalaman di tempatkan disana yg dimana kata2 kurang sopan mereka dapat membuat guest tidak nyaman.

Semoga masukan kami bermaanfat. 

Salam

We recently had the pleasure of staying at the Hotel, and it was an exceptional experience from start to finish. This review highlights the hotel’s outstanding services and amenities that made our stay memorable.

Upon arriving at the hotel, we were delighted that handing over our car for parking was quick and efficient. The valet service was meticulous and ensured our vehicle was well cared for during our stay. It was a great start to our visit, setting the tone for what was to come.

The check-in process was swift and hassle-free. The hotel staff were friendly and welcoming, making us feel at ease. Within minutes, we were in our room, taken aback by its beauty and impeccable condition. The room had been tastefully decorated and appeared to have been recently refurbished. It was evident that the space corresponds to the highest standards.

The room was not only aesthetically pleasing but also excellently equipped. From the comfortable bedding to the modern amenities, everything was top-notch. The flat-screen TV, high-speed internet, and a well-stocked minibar made our stay convenient. We also appreciated the thoughtful touches, such as a work desk, which was ideal for a brief work session during our trip.

The housekeeping service was exceptional. Every day, our room was thoroughly cleaned, and the housekeeping staff paid keen attention to detail. Returning to a pristine and tidy room after a day of exploration was a delight. The hotel facilities were superb and catered to every need.

As our stay ended, the check-out process was just as seamless as the check-in. The efficient hotel staff ensured a smooth departure, making us feel valued as guests.

In conclusion, our time at the Hotel was beyond compare. From the swift and careful valet service to the efficient check-in, the beautiful and well-maintained room, the excellent amenities, and the flawless check-out process, every aspect of our stay was superb. We wholeheartedly recommend the Hotel to anyone seeking an unforgettable and enjoyable stay.

We look forward to returning in the future.

This stay is the end of our 10 night Bali holiday, and it is our first visit to Bali We are still currently here - and if we could have flown home early - believe me, we would.  We are a family of four, and all of us are feeling exactly the same.  Instead of enjoying the second part of our holiday we are so looking forward to leaving - what a truly disappointing end.  We have been made to feel that we are second rate, we have been ignored, and the service in the lounge and the rooftop bar has been extremely poor.  At no point have we felt valued.  We started our trip with 5 nights at The Padma and it was a truly wonderful experience, the hotel and the staff were exceptional, and we absolutely loved our time there.  When booking this entire trip I was so looking forward to staying in this hotel and I really believed that we were going to be ending on a high and that we would be really wowed and spoilt, however this couldn’t be further from the experience.  No requests we have made have been able to be met, as the hotel seems to big and busy.  We requested a room upgrade - nothing available - totally except that - dinner in a restaurant that night - nothing available - all restaurants in the resort fully booked.  Had to have room service.  Want a drink after 10.30 - nothing is open.  Poor service in the club lounge - there is a two hour free cocktail period - from 5 - 7 when ordering a drink it takes 20-25 minutes to arrive, and so when they were serving them we asked for another - they said we were not allowed to order another drink until we had finished the first one.  We said this is ridiculous, by the time you are serving the second drink we would have finished the first, as it takes so long (I was drinking a glass of wine not even having a cocktail).   This long wait did not appear to be because they were busy as there were about 10-15 staff hanging around not doing anything and the bar area was quite empty, so I have no idea why it took so long.  I sat in the lounge the previous night - there were about 5 people in there and about 10 staff - no one came over to take any drinks - I had to keep getting up.  The buffet breakfast is appalling, the quality and the selection is extremely poor - for what we would want to eat for breakfast.   We had it included in our price as we were in a suite, however we had to pay for our children and so it came at a daily cost of $48.00 per person, which is extremely poor value.  We asked for a late checkout and even as we rang we knew they would say they had no availability, however I’m pretty certain if we were other guests, they would have bent over backwards to accommodate this request.  On a positve we had dinner at ‘The Koral’ which we highly recommend - I booked this about a month in advance.

We stayed in apurva, in a cliff ocean view suite, for five days and four nights. We only left the resort once, to go to Bali collection to get souvenirs and we only went for 45 min because we couldn’t wait to get back 🤣 

WEATHER. Went in June. Apurva is magical because it’s on elevated ground. I didn’t realise how hot other parts of Bali can be in comparison until I left the resort 🤣 apurva is always breezy and as you’re often in water, shade or in and out of aircon (afternoon tea at cliff lounge….) , the weather is really super nice here. Like another world. 

MAKE YOU FEEL SO SPECIAL. We were celebrating our anniversary. On the first night, the resort had staff deliver a trolley with champagne, anniversary cake, bath salts, bubble bath and bath petals to our room. We dined at izakaya, Koral, reef beach club and all surprised us with anniversary cakes or desserts of some sort. On our last breakfast at the cliff lounge, the chef nining came out personally to ask what food we liked as it was our last day, and whipped up a yummy prawn dish for us. 

HOSPITALITY. every staff we met was warm, friendly. Every request or question we asked was met with “certainly”, “of course” and “my pleasure”. At the cliff lounge breakfast, everyone without exception is so warm and helpful, but in particular Dewi was a delightful face to see - answering our questions and giving us recommendations. 

ROOMS. Private pool very clean, clear water. Suites are clean and well kept. Ants issue in one suite (a lot, crawling on the bed) but they promptly moved us to another and fixed it. They even sent macaroons to our new suite to apologise for any inconvenience caused. Room comfort menu is amazing - we loved the muhldorfer anti ageing pillows. Room aircon is powerful. Shower jets and pressure are great. Everything is luxurious and well made. 

NO MOSQUITOES. I’m a mosquito magnet and came prepared with lots of repellent of all sorts but I didn’t need it. Never saw a single mosquito and didn’t get bitten in five days. 

BATHROOMS. Deserves a separate mention. Your entrance into every toilet, from the departure lounge to cliff lounge to the suite, is greeted by automated TOTO toilet bowl covers that lift up for you. There’s an extensive smart toilet control with all kinds of bidet functions. Best of all, the toilet bowl seats are heated! 

SERVICE. Lady in red concierge (for suites guests) was contactable and responsive through WhatsApp. She helped us make restaurant bookings and spa bookings. From the room, we could also call the concierge at all times. When we moved suite, I called concierge to ask if they could bring my favourite muhldorfer anti ageing pillow the next morning (if it was too late). The team was so kind to bring my favourite pillows immediately. At 11.48 pm. The service here goes above and beyond. They provided us with many glass bottles of water as well. They accommodated our request for late check out (subject to availability). And there is a departure lounge - tastefully decorated and overlooking the ocean - with some basic snacks, and a drinks area, for you to lounge in after check out, while waiting for your flight. 

FACILITIES. Ample pools. A 40 over metres huge main pool with a good variety of sun beds and half-in-water-half-out loungers to lie on. Hydrojet pools for cliff lounge suites guests. A well fitted gym. Bring your exercise gear if you would like to try things like booking a private boxing session here.  The lobby is huge and spacious with live pianist every afternoon. There are a lot of communal spaces to chill at, that I can imagine if you booked a basic room here you would really enjoy the facilities too. At the beach, paddle boarding and kayaking are complimentary. Go in the morning if you are a beginner and wary in the ocean and would like a calm, low tide “lagoon” to try these out in. There are camel rides along the beach, and paid photoshoots if you desire. The resort has complimentary wifi everywhere. 

CLIFF SUITES BENEFITS. This really made the stay 💯💯 for us. Cliff suites guests have access to the cliff lounge. The breakfast buffet is extensive and the quality is really good. I loved the truffle scrambled eggs, lobster scrambled eggs, Taiwanese foie gras congee. The smoked fish and cheese selection is great. there’s really something for everyone - cuisines range from Chinese to Indonesian, Japanese, American, Indian. We looked forward to breakfast here every morning - it’s extended hours till 12 pm, perfect for late risers. Then there’s complimentary afternoon teas from 3-5 pm, and cocktails with canapés from 5-7 pm. As you can see, there’s always something to do and you could easily stay here a week without even getting bored. Cliff suites are located in the main building too so everything was really convenient. Check in and check out are all done at the cliff lounge. Not sure about other places but I saw big air purifiers in many corners of the cliff lounge. Do yourself a favour if you can and book yourself into a cliff suite here!

DINING. There are several good restaurants located in the resort. Koral is a nice experience, they have very cute rays that I never noticed elsewhere. Reef beach club has vibessss with a live band every night, and the staff are all so friendly and sweet. The food is 💯💯 - we had the seafood platter for 3 and the lobster, stone crab and red snapper were relish. Izakaya was good as well. There is a cigar bar which is worth going up to have a look - it’s actually the atelier of a renowned artist who lives at and works out of apurva. 

SPA. Great pampering experience too. Nothing to complain about. Couple room was beautiful with full glass windows overlooking the ocean. Everything felt clean. Consultation was extensive and the masseuses were good. 

MISC. if I had to nitpick, one thing is that their power sockets in the suites are quite poorly planned - they have the 3 pin type that suits Singapore plugs, but the cable box does not have much space. Hence only one can be used at a time, and the cable of the charger has to be squeezed in. The shower area of the second suite I moved to was a bit smelly with one or two drain flies. Could use some maintenance there. The first suite wasn’t (but had ants). But it was tolerable. Do not let this put you off! It is south east Asia after all. 

Room tips (for suites):
- Try to get a suite with sun over the private pool. The pool is already cold and the sun makes it tolerable and enjoyable. 
- The middle located suites have more privacy. The ones at the sides - their pools can be seen from lift lobby or main stairs. In the middle located suites, you have lots of privacy. 
- JUST GO!!!

The Apurva Kempinski Bali is THE hotel of Bali now primarily because of the grand lobby design and also the fame from hosting the G20 event last year. Now the lobby really was the highlight of our stay, the majestic design is truly one of a kind.

We stayed in the two bedroom villas and found it to be adequate however uninspiring. The residential design means that you are walled up inside your villa/pool area and the villa itself is basically just two guest rooms stacked together. Views from the villas are non-existent. There is no additional indoor living/dining space. The Villa bathrooms were beautiful with tons of natural light and the best hotel shampoo/conditioner ever. Housekeeping did a great job cleaning the villa as well as adding some fun touches to the otherwise mediocre surroundings.

 The villa complex is located farthest from the main pool/beach area and buggies are inaccessible, meaning that in order to get any sort of buggy transportation you would need to first exit the villa complex, walk through the lobby towards the elevators, take the elevators to the landing point where the gold carts are located. Buggies were not readily available at the landing point either, they operate this sort of shuttle bus routine so sometimes you will need to wait. This might prove troublesome and difficult for people with mobility issues. 

Staying in the villas includes access to the Villa Lounge which hosts breakfast and afternoon tea. A boutique buffet presentation plus an a la carte menu is available during breakfast. Food quality and taste were fine. What we loved most about the Villa Lounge was the female chef in charge who was always present to oversee the operations and ask about our dining experience and if we wanted anything else. We also loved the barista made coffees and the amazing coffee art that arrived with every cup. 

We dined in several restaurants around the resort and the quality and taste of our meals were fine. Personally, I do not recommend the Japanese izakaya which is so grossly overpriced. $40 USD for a bowl of lobster ramen with a few slices of lobster and lukewarm broth, daylight robbery. Other things we ordered, like sushi rolls, tempura udon, were all just standard dishes with an ultra premium price tag. Honestly, I thought I was back in the Maldives with these prices. 

We visited the spa for their monthly treatment, we enjoyed the massage portion however the heated clay wrap following the massage was very uncomfortable. The service was poor as we needed to chase down several people, including the spa manager in order to get a reservation. Credit where it's due, after asking to speak to the manager she did apologize and offer a minor discount for our treatments to make up for the missed experience. 

The Reef Beach Club Pool area is beautiful with the poolside cabanas and close proximity to the beach however the management there is insultingly incapable and laughable. On our first visit to the pool during the afternoon hours we saw multiple cabanas without people using them, however they all had some sort of belongings just there to "reserve" the cabanas. We settled for loungers beside the cabanas and observed that these cabanas remained empty for 2-3 hours a time. So the next morning we went early, literally when no one was in the area to put our stuff down because we thought that was how things were done. A manager then approached us and told us that we couldn't put our stuff down like that. So you can't put your stuff down at 10AM in the morning where the area is literally empty but you can do it throughout the rest of the day when multiple people are trying to get a cabana but because you got one first it's yours (or your sunscreen, book, towels) to keep? Absurd and ineffective policies. I confronted the manager about the lack of logic in the resort policies and he agreed that there is room for change. But at this point it has already ruined our day. 

The overall experience you get here is more of a luxury factory. You see the beautiful lobby, the stacks and stacks of rooms they squeeze in, and the amount of people and kids at the pool. Everyone is friendly but they just have too many people to take care of. They have this "lady in red" whatsapp account which is supposed to serve as a guest relations/concierge point of contact, which I used to arrange some requests, at first the response rate was ok, but then I was outright ghosted. I had to send an email to guest services to have them chase the team down for a reply. All this, at a so-called five star resort. I would recommend only coming here for a coffee, so you get your instagram pictures and then you can leave to stay somewhere more luxurious, like the Mulia Villas next door where they don't try to cramp multiple villas into limited real estate, where you are not subject to walled up views, and you have a butler who will happily shuffle you anywhere in the comfort of a golf cart.

This is the best hotel we have ever been to.
If you stay in Nusa Dua, book this is hotel. It is by far the best.
I don’t know where to start.
The architecture and simple size of the hotel is breathtaking.
Staff is so friendly - they fulfill every wish you have and this was also the most kid friendly hotel as well.
The suites with pool are just breathtakingly beautiful and really big. Room service and cleaning is on point.
Food is outstanding. The best food we have had in Bali.
Even the ice cream was some of the best I had. And I had some ice cream in my life 😂
We also had lunch at the Koral restaurant - yes, it is pricey but definitely worth it. The menu was absolutely delicious and the experience with the fishes was so nice.
The pools and the beach are just great. There are also a lot of options for kids including several kids pools and slides.
Breakfast here is just the best. I’ve never had better breakfast in my life. The variety and quality are just outstanding. I was sad I couldn’t eat more and try everything. Sushi and Dim Sum are absolutely awesome.
Staff is really friendly and just great.
Would absolutely recommend staying here - it is a bit pricey yes but absolutely worth it.
We can’t wait to come back

We had the most amazing time at this resort. We have stayed many times in Bali at other  lovely resorts but none can compare to the Kempinski. We stayed in a Cliff private pool Villa for 11 nights it was fantastic, the villa, the service, the food it was all second to none. The staff are just so accommodating and genuine and we cannot speak highly enough of them, they were all fantastic!!!
The cliff lounge staff all became our friends but we must make very special mention of Mahaa who made sure our stay was very special even on her days off.
Our room attendants we the most wonderful young men and could not have done more for us so a very special mention to Raka and Gale and I cant remember the other young mans name (sorry) We will certainly be back!!!!!

Kimpenski Apurva’s grandiose building is exceptionally and aesthetically unique and impressive. The people working in this establishment are welcoming, friendly, accommodating and hospitable. We were greeted with a smile and hello wherever we go.  Everyone is attentive and understood exactly what we needed and responded to our numerous requests with haste. The service around Apurva is top notch.
1. Concierge - At first step in Apurva’s vicinity, we were welcomed by their concierge and proceeded to take care of our luggages. We had two rooms and they knew exactly where to take them.
2. Front Desk - We had some mixed up with our room arrangement and Erawati the hotelier assigned to us was apologetic and put her best effort to resolve the issue at their busiest time whilst ensuring that our requests are tended to. She remained calmed and collected. 
3. Reef Beach Club - We had our first meal at Reef beach club which became our go to restaurant for dinner. The ambiance was great with nightly live acoustics. We had the pleasure of knowing Ratna and Wiana. What great individuals to deal with. They both made sure we were looked after. Ratna knew exactly what our preferred table and Wiana knew what drinks we take as soon as we were seated. On our last night, we requested the song fireworks and just as the band was singing it the hotel had some fireworks display. It was magical! It put a big smile on our faces and sadness at that same time knowing that our fun was coming to an end.
4. Pala - I couldn’t begin to describe how great this restaurant in terms of options and taste. It beats all the buffet in Perth (including Epicurean and Atrium) and Vegas. We had our daily breakfast at Pala which serves a range of options from western to middle eastern to asian dishes. For food addicts like myself, it was definitely a sight to see. 😊 I love the fact that I had options each day. We could also pre-order which I did in three occasions. I ordered eggs benedict. It was not the best only because I have personal preference as to how it’s made and every restaurant does it differently. We pre-ordered mango shake and mango slices and served to us as soon as they have been made aware of our arrival.
5. Izakaya - The first bite of the Ebi  Karaage, three crispy black tiger prawns seasoned with balsamic teriyaki mayo enshroud within a black ink squid crisp top with mirin yuzu pearls, I seriously didn’t know what to think of it but the best karaage I have ever tasted in my life. The chicken karaage was just as divine!! I had the next meal and the next and all I can say is that Izakaya is the best Japanese restaurant I’ve been to. The presentation alone is enticing that every bite feels like an exultation. The beauty and joy it brings is beyond me. Devi, our host, looked after us very well which made our dining experience in both occasions even more special. We also had the pleasure of meeting Izakaya’s Japanese chef, Chef Shunji. We were honoured to be graced by his presence. Feels like meeting a rockstar of japanese cuisine. Having a three course meal is usually never perfect. There’s always one dish that is off, but in Izakaya, every dish served was quintessential.
6. Bai Yun - The one thing that stood out for me is the service. They made my dipping sauce catered to my palette. They made separate set of dishes for our daughter also as she was allergic to shellfish and nuts. I’m astonished as to how they pay attention to their guests and prepare meals specifically catered to the individual. When they said that we have an unlimited dim sum, it sure was unlimited. I wish I had room for seconds. Our favourite was egg custard which we all loved. As for the rest of the meals, there wasn’t anything extraordinary about them. It wasn’t mediocre either. They were just like any other high end Chinese restaurant. Like Izakaya, the presentation was delicately put together. Align with Apurva’s standard, the service was top notch.
7. Cliff Lounge - High tea at cliff lounge provides a selection of various savoury and sweets - a perfect afternoon delight after a swim. We had our favourite corner and the ladies were ever so kind to remember our preference and ready to take our drinks order. I enjoyed having my cendol made by their own chef and taking little bites  with my cup of coffee. It was a wonderful afternoon tea after a swim or massage. The cliff lounge has two massive outdoor lounges, a few day beds and sun loungers by the pools with the bar in the middle. We hang out once at the bar for a cold beverage before our massage. 
8. Housekeeping - our room was getting cleaned multiple times a day. Every time we come back to our room, our beds are always made up and everything was in order. I appreciate these guys the most as I find they are the ones doing the hardest work but not getting much credit. Kudos to this team!!
9. Luxury Spa - We had a couples massage therapy on the first session. They took us to a waiting room with beautiful decor and served us with hot jamu. We were then told that our masseuses are ready for us. We were then escorted to our room and we walked through the hallway which gave me that infinite vibe. I love the symmetry and simplicity. As soon as we got in to our room, the view was so impressive. Not that we were looking at the view the whole time, it just gave me peace. A good start before the actual therapy started. They then gestured us to change. I still giggle having to wear disposable undergarment. They scrubbed and washed our feet like any other asian massage therapy we’ve been to. My masseuse was so good that I heard myself snore. So embarrassing. This happened again on another occasion when I was on my own. I felt a weight was lifted off my shoulder that I just felt so relaxed. Kudos to Mega and Dessa - they are so good at what they do. They knew exactly where I’m most tensed. 
10. Lobby - The hotel resort was set up so that the lobby is on the ground floor with an elevated and uninterrupted view of the ocean. It’s massive with high ceiling like a nave of a church. The hotel has east and west wings and I thought the reception was cleverly set up to welcome and check-in guests depending on which wing they are staying. There were lounges everywhere including outdoor sitting for guests to see outside of the hotel. There is a massive stair (almost like a gallery) which takes you to the bottom of the resort with water on each side and landing on a first massive pool. In the middle of the lobby is an opening to the floor below which takes you to the Luxury spa or the east wing elevator. We were taken to another lounge (4 floors below) for guests staying in suites, and similarly there were two lounges with a spiral staircase in the middle that takes you to the cliff lounge restaurant. The hotel was cleverly design that synergistically work together. I love how the east and west wings are mirrored with Pala, the pools, the stair and the lobby are aligned right in the middle. 
11. Facility - for guests who doesn’t mind walking, the facilities can be walked. For those who doesn’t want to break a sweat, the resort has buggies available that can take you to various locations. We did take a buggy to take us to the chapel thinking it was too far. We realised it was walkable.  The hotel has numerous pools from private to exclusive access to communal pools. We had our own private pool with access to a couple of pools at the lounge available only to suite guests. Our kids had semi private pool which is shared with three other rooms. There are three massive pools with slides accessible by everyone staying at the hotel. There is also an infinity pool situated by the beach next to the Reef Beach Club catered purely for adults.  The resort has a gym also with training sessions set up for those needing to maintain their fitness regime. 
12. Our room was in the higher floor with a private pool and a view of the ocean. We had our own balcony with a couple of sun lounger. The room was equipped with basic hotel amenities commonly found in a luxury hotel. The only extra is a bath salt. It’s amazing as none of the hotels we’ve been to offer this (not even Crown Tower). Our toilet has a bidet which is awesome. In fact, the entire resort has bidet on each toilet seat including the communal toilets. The seating area in our room is massive which was convenient when the kids are lounging in our room. The bathroom amenities get replenished on a daily basis. We had unlimited drinks outside our room already, and still our water gets replenished on a daily basis. We had abundance of drinks in the entire time of our stay. 
13. The kids room is slightly smaller than ours but the they had pretty much the same as us. The only difference is their pool is shared with three other rooms; they don’t have access to the cliff lounge pools; and dining at cliff lounge was an extra cost which we didn’t mind paying as it only cost US$16 per person for an afternoon tea. 

The overall stay at Kempinski Apurva Bali is one that’s hard to forget. The stay was worth every penny. What made it even more memorable is the people who made our stay extra special. They welcomed us with a smile and looked after us in a way that it was hard to leave the place. Everyone was so kind and warm and for that we especially want to thank these individuals and their team;

Reef beach club
- Ratna
- Wiana
- Ayu
- Widiana
- Reef beach Club

IZAKAYA 
- Devi

- Juli
- Izakaya Team

Lounge
- Ulan

- Cliff Lounge Team

Bai Yun Team

Massage
- Mega
- Dessa
  
The housekeeping team, concierge team and last but not the least Erawati our hotelier.

Итак мы находимся в этом 5* могильнике хорошего настроения.
вообще я хотел сначала поставить 2 бала ,а не 1)) мне казалось ,что еда тут вкусная ) но увы и ах....мы отравились и не слезаем с их чудо унитаза ...
С чего начать....
Начну пожалуй с того ,что моря в отеле - нет .
Оно как бы и есть, но за в нем никто не купается потому что зайти в него на пляже при отеле - не лучшая идея из за грязи. При этом все чин чином и ходят какие то несчастные люди с граблями ,типа чистят пляж)) но...они не чистят его от слова "совсем" я даже снял видео как они лениво ходят друг за дружкой по пляжу по соседству с верблюдами( а есть тут и они) и ничего не делает...весь день !
мы увидели что у соседних отелей пляжи почище и решили пойти туда...но увы и ах...при заходе в воду вы сразу же по колено увязаете в песке. это очень не приятно . короче мы забили на море и решили искупаться  и освежиться в их "красивом " бассейне
и тут нас ждал второй удар . бассейны невероятно мелкие и мне они были ровно по пупок))) все бассейны. и они были отвратительно горячими.
итак еще минус за отсутствие возможности нормально искупаться.
лобби и консьерж служба требуют отдельного упоминания)

это был последний отель и мне рупии как бы после них не нужны от слова совсем ...плюнув на все эти заморочки решили платить нашей картой сразу

про персонал и качество услуг.... я дважды заказывал бургеры тут и ни разу у меня не спросили как прожарить мясо)) на минутку стоят они 20-30 баксов ))
ну я молчу про коктейли которые приносят в пластиковых !!!! бокалах и их качестве)))это мрак))) кстати кемпински использует до сих пор пластиковые трубочки ! позорище !!! в любой тайской 4 уже давно бумажные )))они явно не френдли к зеленой повестке

я мог бы еще много написать про завтраки на которых не найти свободный стол и ор который там стоит , про то что они ставят по одному флакону принадлежностей в ванной комнате на двоих))) про шум в коридорах после 11 от пьяных гостей их вечеринок в шатрах, про то что курят эти госте везде и всюду вонь....короче это худший отель 5 в моей жизни

It is a great hotel with great location, but the games they play with room upgrades leaves a sour taste.  They have five different room types, not counting the three suite types. The front desk has become a sales desk as they offer you upgrades at €100-300/night!  If the hotel has empty rooms, why not offer your guests a complimentary upgrade?!  If you improve our experience, we are more likely to return.
We had booked through Amex Travel, so were supposed to get complimentary room upgrade. The assigned room was on 3rd floor facing the park, which had a loud music party going on. The next assigned room was on 5th floor and way smaller - but supposedly the same category!  We finally were given a room on the 8th floor, away from the main street and park. It was about the same size as the first one but a little better furnished.
Other than the room assignment, we enjoyed our stay.  Had dinner and breakfast at the restaurant, drinks at the bar and dessert at the Living Room. Great food and service, especially at the Living Room.
On checkout, was told that the breakfast and Living Room charges would not be covered under the AmEx $100 Food & Beverage credit.  When I challenged that, the front desk supervisor blamed the accounting department and credited it!
It didn’t have to be this frustrating!
5 stars for everything! Perfect!

My husband and I have travelled to over 40 different countries and stayed in countless hotels and resorts in the last twenty years, and the Apurva is truly one of the best resorts I've ever stayed at, in everything from the room to the facilities to the food options and quality and most impressively the service I received from the amazing staff.

Even prior to my arrival the service was excellent, with prompt replies to my email enquiries and assistance in making my restaurant and spa reservations. Things only got better during my stay, with everyone I encountered providing warm, personalised and exceptional service beyond what I've encountered before, at check in, in the various restaurants, the spa, around the pools and at the beach, even in the hallways, and most notably in the Cliff Lounge.

The resort itself is architecturally gorgeous, and very impressive, from the lobby to the exterior of the hotel facing the beach. The grounds, public areas and beach are clean and well maintained. The main pool is massive with ample seating around it, and I never had an issue finding a space to sit. I used the gym every day and it was well equipped with modern equipment and very clean. I thoroughly enjoyed my afternoon at the spa, where I had a Sekar Petals and foot massage. This was one of the best massages I’ve had, and my therapist, Yulia, was incredibly skilled and professional, using the perfect pressure and techniques, and I left feeling completely relaxed and rejuvenated.

My Junior Cliff Suite was huge, immaculately clean and elegantly decorated, with high quality amenities and ample drinking water left by housekeeping twice a day, and the fruit bowl and sweet treats on arrival were much appreciated. The bed and linens were so comfortable, and the bathroom was huge with a fantastic shower and tub, and having a private pool was the best!

The other benefit of the suite booking was access to the Cliff Lounge throughout my stay, and this was a massive perk! I was able to complete my check in and check out there, both of which were done with warmth and efficiency. They were even kind enough to get my room ready three hours ahead of check in time due to my early arrival, which was incredibly kind, as well as allow me an extra hour late check out on my departure day. Everything about the Cliff Lounge was exceptional, the breakfast, all-day refreshments, hydro jets pool, afternoon tea and evening cocktails. The food quality and variety was excellent, and my personal favourites were the caramel French toast, the fresh bakery items like the chocolate almond and the cheese croissants, and the awesome char siew pork belly, as well as the scones at afternoon tea. I enjoyed trying all of their different wines on offer for the cocktail evenings, and the hydro pool was also very enjoyable, and mostly empty for much of my stay. 

But the best part of the experience was the amazing service I received from the Cliff Lounge staff. By the first day everyone knew my name, and greeted me warmly whenever I visited. The managers, Amar and Arya, are true experts in hospitality, attentive and thoughtful, frequently checking in with me to ensure I was enjoying my time and to see if there was anything else they could arrange. And their kindness showed again when they learned of my evening departure from Bali and arranged for me to still use the Cliff Lounge, which was above and beyond what I would expect and something I was very grateful for. Mega, Chandra and Kristina were so welcoming and kind, every time I saw them, Mega even remembered my preferred wine for cocktail hour, and so many the rest of the staff were just as attentive and friendly, providing truly fantastic service: Pita, Diah, Wahyu, Eka, Padma, Raos and Kadeem, and so many others whose names I didn’t get to make note of. 

In addition to Cliff Lounge I also ate at several of the other restaurants at the resort. I visited Selasar Deli several times and particularly enjoyed the rich, flaky pastries and the yummy chocolate bonbons and gelato, as well as the friendly service I received. I ordered room service my first night and was blown away by the makasar grilled beef ribs, they were perfectly charred on the outside, moist inside. My lunch at Izakaya by Oku was excellent, with a beautiful atmosphere, attentive service and simple, delicious Japanese food done to perfection, both the gyoza and the wagyu sando, and the apply candy dessert was a work of art that tasted fantastic. I had both breakfast as well as evening cocktails and a light dinner at Pala, and though the breakfast was very busy with lots of other guests (I went back to Cliff Lounge for the rest of my stay), the servers did a great job attending to everyone in a timely and friendly manner, and the food again of a great quality and variety. Dinner was excellent, though I’d recommend requesting a beach view table when booking, I had a reservation but was seated facing the hotel because I didn't do so, but the service was great and so were the two dishes I ordered from the Indonesian-Indian menu, the Balinese pork belly steamed buns and freshly baked truffle cheese naan with tomato chutney. I also had dinner at Koral, which I’ve written a separate review for as it was such an amazing experience!

This was one of the best beach resort holidays I’ve ever had. The sheer variety of dining options and facilities and the comfort and size of the room meant I wasn’t even once tempted to leave the resort, and wish I could have stayed more than three nights. I will be returning again, with my husband next time, so that he can enjoy the same wonderful experience as I did at the Apurva. I’m already looking forward to it!

The Kempinski Apurva Bali is undoubtedly a grand, majestic hotel standing 15-storey tall on a cliff overlooking the beautiful Nusa Dua Lagoon its deep blue sea.
Upon arrival, the amazing architecture of its main lobby and the vastness of space brings awe to every traveller’s face. This makes if of course, one of the most instagramable hotels in Bali currently, which is great for marketing.
As travelers coming from Europe and staying for 7 days at the hotel, we had some amazing and some not-so amazing times at the hotel.
Rooms – great, polished, everything well planned, and everything had its place. Bed is spacious and large, rooms are cleverly designed. Bathrobe, towels, loofahs are placed exactly where you needed them. Views to the pool are great. Housekeeping was a gem, they did little towel tricks for us and filled the ice bucket for us every night for our wines.
BUT – rooms are not soundproof towards the corridor. We had the displeasure of being waken up every single morning at 6-7am by screaming and running kids up and down, going for breakfast. We really have nothing against kids, as we were travelling with an 8-year old. But it would be a good idea to ask guests if they prefer a quieter floor / Block and maybe put all large families with 3-4 young infants together in different areas to those looking for a longer sleep.
 
Grounds – very polished and glamourous. Everything is well-kept, trimmed and maintained to the highest standards. The grounds are very large, the pools are beautiful and the beach is clean and beautiful. There is a large kids pool with slide, two adults pool, a jogging track.
 
F&B – this is where the hotel managed to outdo itself, even for Asian Standards. There is a huge list of restaurants to choose from including a Café, Japanese, Chinese, Poolside, Reef Club, Underwater, main restaurant, roof top bar and  huge extensive room service menu. What we really liked was the fact that all menus were stored under a url where you can just open up on the phone and check. We tried every restaurant while we were there except for the Underwater restaurant as they only accepted children until 13:30 in the afternoon. All were good, we really enjoyed the quality of food and variety. 
Breakfast was however another story. We had booked a room with Breakfast rate, and being unseasoned Asian Kempinski guests (we stay regularly in the Kempinskis in the middle east, Germany and Switzerland) , we were in for a REAL SHOCK during breakfast. Forget bone china, butter croissants, sipping quietly on a coffee and reading a newspaper – Breakfast is OVERWHELMING. There are crying infants, food all over the floor, long queues grabbing all the food they can leaving 90% on the table and floor. Sweat, steam, busy waiters and people shouting across to each other while watching videos on their phones. The hotel obviously cannot do anything about their guests, but some measures like keeping all the huge families (again, this hotel is very popular with large families staying 1-2 nights) in certain areas with kids friendly facilities. Saturday and Sunday was the WORST, Monday-Tuesday was not too bad. We eventually changed on Thursday to the Reef Club after a tip from the staff that was so much better.
Coral restaurant – another memorable mention. We tried to get a table there but children were only allowed to at lunch at 13:30 and there is a minimum spending of IDR 550,000++ per child and IDR 950,000++ per adult in addition to a non-refundable deposit or 1,5 million for reservation. We tried explaining that there is no way we can eat that much after having breakfast at 11am, but there was just other choice than take it or leave it, so we were really not bothered.
 
Facilities – Great choices. The Spa deserved a huge mention here. It’s probably the only place where you get the real Kempinski Experience. The therapists are highly trained, and we could not be happier. For families – there is a huge Kids Club which offered nice activities such as t-shirt painting, kite making etc. We also tried renting the kayaks for a short paddle around the bay, which was great. Other than that, we really enjoyed the facilities and outdoor time at the hotel. There never seemed to be anyone at the pool or beach between noon till 5pm, so we had almost everything to ourselves. Only when 5pm came, the crowds and crowds of people appeared out of nowhere.
 
Staff – this is also one of the hotel’s best sides. Their staff is ultra attentive and very proactive. We keep forgetting the happy hour time as we never had a watch at the pool, so we asked the staff to remind us once happy hour starts and they really did it every day without fail! Some days when we just didn’t feel like getting out of the pool, they delivered our daiquiris & margaritas to the water! Our child could run to any staff member to get ice-creams, burgers, drinks and there was never a problem. Only once when we left the pool the staff would chase us for our signatures, which we found kinda amusing.

The OPERATOR “0” was one of the most horrible experience ever for us. When we checked in on a Saturday evening, it was like a buzzing mall. The guy at the reception gave us a tip for some Sunday brunch, and we would have to call “0” to reserve tables. Indeed we tried “0” all Saturday night – there was just no one. So we turned up at Sala on Sunday morning and say a whole line of people who were not hotel guests waiting for Brunch and we just decided to give up. We tried “0” again a few more times for reservations, but in the end we just gave up and walked to the restaurant to reserve ourselves.

Another bad experience – the hotel does not seem trained in dealing with long-term guests. As we checked-in after a long flight from Switzerland, they asked us for a deposit of 1million Rupiah per night, which we thought for a Kempinski was ridiculously cheap – honestly € 60 per DAY for three persons?
So we didn’t mind, but being the typical Kempinski guests, we stayed at the pool all day, we had drinks, lunch, dinner, massages, snacks all day at the hotel. So we got a Whatsapp on our 4/5 day from the hotel employee telling us the deposit is overdrawn (we tried warning them!) and that we should immediately go to reception to settle this matter. Very bad – we are GHA members and our credit cards are stored in the system, so why not take it from there?  Nope, we had to march all the way from the reef club up to the reception to pay for something the check-in people messed up in the beginning. That was very, very unpleasant for us.
 
We were really contemplating whether to rate this 4 or 5 star, but because of all the hiccups we faced, we have to give this great hotel and the wonderful staff a 4 – hoping that they put in some measures to increase the Kempinski experience for certain personas and not turn itself into a Spring break resort.
It might not have mattered if we were like most guests staying 1-3 nights at most, but it was just a slight let-down as we were really expecting a Kempinski as we know the brand.

This is by far the largest hotel I have ever experienced. 
Hotel is one word "Majestic". The view, the architecture, it is amazing.
Even the standard Deluxe room is spacious.
Breakfast was plenty, many dishes to choose from. Delicious.
However, despite all that, there are still some down sides. 

1. If you don't like walking then this hotel is not for you. Walking here will take you at least 10k steps a day. Which I personally don't mind because I like walking. But for those who don't, beware.

2. If there is a wedding event going on (usually weekends), the wedding guests would just take over the buggies and parking spaces. And I personally had issues parking my car. The hotel told me to park outside on the street. They should realize that I am a paying hotel guest, they should not prioritize wedding guests who are not even staying there in the first place. So, avoid weekends if you could.

3. Room looks nice and clean but not after you get down on your knees. I happen to accidently drop my key behind the TV credenza. So I got down and look behind it, lo and behold, the sheer amount of dirt and dust. I even found some plastic wrappers and a toy car. Even under the sofa, its so dirty with thick dust. There were even foot prints I'm guessing left by the construction workers. 

4. The lifts (the bottom wing lifts) were not really taken care of. You can clearly see by the many scratches and dings. My guess is that the lifts were damaged during the construction. Poor quality control on that area.

So all in all, I had an enjoyable 4 nights stay.
Spent most of the time at the pool and beach. Beach is not great by the way. Nusa Dua beaches are far better. Would I come back again? Probably. But for sure not on the weekends.

Due to 4 days stay, my room was upgraded from Grand deluxe to Grand Deluxe Ocean Court and got a resort credit of 300k The location is in the southern Nusa Dua. There were 3 greeters and one of them took me to the check-in counter. Check-in was fast although the lobby was very crowded because many people were taking photos. We were given cold towels, ice ginger, and sanitize kit containing a botanica brand hand sanitizer, 2 masks, and 2 wet tissues.
The room is very large equipped with luggage space, sofa, work desk, work chair, comfortable mattress, 4 pillows. Big and clear TV, fast Wifi. In the luggage room there are laundry bags, shoe shine, autan and mosquito spray. They served a fruit platter and 5 types of Indonesian snacks. There is a coffee maker, minibar, kettle, coffee using capsules, tea using the Ronnefledt brand (jasmine, English breakfast, green tea and 2 sachets of earl gray each). 4 bottles of spring brand mineral water are provided and free refill. There is a balcony with a sofa, the view we got should have been a pool view but it was blocked by tall trees. thick notes and ballpoint pens are provided. There are many sockets both on the work table and near mattress. Our room had as stuck curtain and was solved by engineering within 30 minutes. Our room is cleaned 2 times during lunch time and at night (turn down service at 17.00-21.00) where all amenities are refilled.
The bathroom is equipped with a bathtub, shower, 2 washtafel and a closet. The shower is equipped with a handshower and rain shower. Amenities are very complete. The closet equipped with a sensor that opens automatically when we approach and there is a heater, washer, and also a dryer. Amenities aroma is orange and the smell is very good. Towels are provided with 3 body towels, 3 hand towels and 2 face towels.
- Gyms and Spas. The equipment is complete with a beach view. There is a Jacuzzi with a beach view and a sauna. 
- The main swimming pool is very large with a beach view, a kids pool with a slider. 
- Nusa Dua beach with free canoeing activities
- Chapel
- Outdoor playground and indoor Kids club 
- Buggy for access
- Many Artwork especially at the lobby. Beautiful and detail artwork. Love the view from lobby balcony to the beach is really good.
- There are 3 restaurants recommended by our Escort staff, Reef beach club, coral restaurant, and izakaya by Oku. Reef beach club because you can relax, eat and see the beaches of Nusa Dua. Coral restaurant with aquarium signature. Izakaya by Oku Japanese restaurant because many guests repeat orders there. 
Breakfast at Apurva Kempinski is the best and most complete breakfast I have ever tasted. Additional breakfast is charged at 400k per person and worth it because what is served at breakfast tastes like restaurant class. 
Kind of
- Asian (Beef bacon, Dimsum, roasted chicken/duck), porridge, and others chinese food)
- Noodle station and Krupuk station
- Japanese (sushi, miso soup, tempura, okonimiyaki)
- Cheese and cold cut section 
- Western (sausage, potato, tomato, egg, vegetable)
- Korean (bimbimbap)
- Indian (Masala dosa, poha)
- Cereal and juice, infused water
- Indonesian food (Sumatra and meatballs)
- Bakery pastry, Fresh Fruit, Oats and nuts, Yoghurt, Madura Porridge 
- Traditional snacks 
There is still a la carte menu, their specials are salmon egg benedict, truffle mushroom omelette, and avocado sourdough, coffee and tea.
Most memorable dishes for me during my stay: Almond Croissant, Pinch cake with hazelnut topping, Avocado Sourdough, Roasted chicken/Duck, Belacan Shrimp, sushi, hakau, sautéed mushroom
All staff are very friendly. They provide extra miles of service. At breakfast, the table is quickly cleaned and always offers what you want to add, and they will update first by apologizing so we can wait patiently. Actually my coffee machine at my room is broke down but I just realized at last day because I prefer tea

Saya menginap di sini sekitar 22 - 24 mei 2021.
Dengan booking kamar (#6150) deluxe pool acces, ini jadi pilihan jenis kamar fovorit anak saya. Karena bisa langsung berenang di depan kamar dan mudah di awasi.

Pengalaman pertama masuk ke properti ini sampai di pintu masuk dan lobby paling depan, yup its normal hotel. Masuk ke dalam dan sampai di resepsionis, kemegahan lobby dengan tinggi mungkin lebih dari 10 meter dan banyaknya ukiran hampir setinggi atap lobby. Ini luar biasa!!! 

Proses cek in awal di sambut seorang staf wanita dengan menanyakan tujuan dan di arahkan ke staf pria di meja resepsionis dan welcome drink disajikan. Semua proses registrasi berjalan baik. Satu hal yg kurang pas adalah saat memanggil bantuan untuk mengantar ke kamar, "panggil trainee ke depan antar tamu".
