Kan kata lain yg lebih baik banyak " Bisa/tolong satu orang antar tamu atau semacamnya".

"Trainee" datang dan mengantar kami ke kamar, selama perjalanan yg panjang bisa di jadiin jogging track. Dia menjelaskan semua sop standar dan sop protokol kesehatan dengan baik. Untuk kamar semua baik, bersih dan terawat. Untuk pintu ke akses kolam sayangnya ada jamur di pintunya dan di bawah dek pas posisi dari dalam kolam saat berenang terlihat. Kontrol AC yg saya kurang paham, disetting hingga 25°C tp tetap kembali ke 21°C hingga setiap pagi pasti kamar dalam keadaan lembab basah.

Dari kamar ke lobby seperti adventure di mana kita berjalan dari kamar ke lift (ada opsi naik buggy juga), dr ground ke lobby di lantai 15. Klo kerja disini dan harus keliling resort ini beberapa kali dalam sehari dijamin sehat tanpa perlu olahraga tambahan lagi. (Pernah ngerasain kerja di properti seperti ini naik turun tangga dan keliling resort sekitar 1,5 tahun). 

Untuk fasilitas kamar, sempat bertanya ke housekeeper yg bersihkan kamar. Ternyata ada pilihan bantal kepala yg diinginkan kalau kita mau, ga sempat nyoba pilihannya karena ada beberapa panggilan di ponsel sehingga lupa untuk memilih bantal. 

Kolam renang utama terlalu banyak orang sehingga agak takut juga untuk berenang. Jadinya memaksimalkan kolam renang depan kamar.

Kids clubnya bagus interiornya dengan tema seperti rumah pohon dan taman bermain di lantai bawah. Ada fantasi adventure juga dimana anak (3) dan keponakan (7&3) saya di ajak masuk ke coral restaurant dimana anak2 di ajak berinteraksi dan melihat jenis2 ikan di aquarium. 

Untuk makanan, izakaya by oku termasuk makanan jepang yang enak. Room service mencoba sop buntut buat anak saya, rasa kuahnya agak asin hingga anak saya makan dengan daging dan sayur yg saya pisah dari kuahnya. Untuk breakfast masih okelah, karena agak kepikiran juga dengan banyaknya orang yg sarapan yg hampir bersamaan.

I checked in on 21 Dec ‘20. The lobby as a lot of people agreed on is indeed grande and has a Javanese unique style - it was crafted beautifully. However, seeing so many people in the lobby made you feel you wanna get away from there asap especially during Covid time like this. 

The check-in process was quick and the lady escorted me to my room right after (she is a nice brand ambassador. Really friendly and informative about whats going on the hotel). But, it took an hour to get the luggages to the room. 

No turndown service given on the first day.

Foods at Pala (All Day Dining) are not worth the restaurant in 5 starts hotel title. Poor menu rotation - boring selections with a really average taste, lack of taste tbh. I also happened to know that we can actually choose various menu of eggs instead of regular one but none of the waiter informed me abt this. I believe Kempinski can do co much better than this.

Poor taste can also be found on the Pala Rooftop Bar. I ordered pizza and fries - I would not recommend you to order the pizzas - as I was coming with some friends and no one took more than a slice of it. The margarita was also bad. 

Reef Beach Restaurant offers a small portion and a really average taste of Ravioli - not worth the high price. Also, i would understand if the beach and the pool are crowded with people during the festive times, just if it were no covid issues. But seeing how swarmed it was made you wonder does the hotel really follow the 50% maximum capacity policy?

Went to three restaurants in Apurva and none of it shown 5star quality and taste. Extremely disappointed.

When I came to the main pool that turned out will be closed in the next 15 minutes (closes at 7 PM), I was encountered by a pool guy to notify me about the closing time. However, the way he says it was really poor. Perhaps he was so tired due to so many people during the day.

I was assisted by a male front desk upon my check-out. Its such a shame that the 5 stars luxury hotels service wasn’t carried out. I would understand the bitter intonation and a rush gesture should I stayed in some budget hotels. But this definitely could not be expected from Apurva Kempinski - I mean, It didn’t cost you a $100 to spend a night here. What a way to say goodbye :) It may sound exaggerating.. but it is not should you happened to experience the real 5 stars luxury services from different brands.

Also, I took me more than an hour to get my luggages after check-out. When getting my car from from valet service, times also seem to be the trouble. Slow services.

Just wondering if they cant handle lot of people with manpower shortage then why keep accepting customer? Quantity over quality? It decreased the quality smh. Disappointing.

I booked this hotel for a weekend as an anniversary gift for my boyfriend and our stay was amazing! This was still before lockdown so I cannot comment as to any changes since. 

I booked through Amex and paid approx £330 for 2 nights (excl breakfast) which included a $100 SPA voucher as they had an offer on with Amex at the time. This is an extremely reasonable price for the level of care and service offered especially for those used to Western Europe prices. I should add that I booked this fairly late and also this was Valentines weekend! 

The staff are extremely polite and welcoming, they speak English perfectly and were able to accommodate a special request - I called in advance to ask for a bottle of champagne in the room upon arrival and it was there as agreed, no reminders needed! We also received a room upgrade (I think this was part of the Amex offer though). 

The food was excellent, on our arrival we ordered room service which was delivered quickly and was delicious! 

The location is perfect as it is so close to the centre and all the landmarks as well as a lot of top restaurants. 

We also used the SPA where we had a couples massage and time in the SPA (excellent big pool and steam room). Our treatment was great, both me and my boyfriend enjoyed it very much and the pressure was exactly how each of us likes it. 

This place really offers you a VIP service. Inside the hotel is luxurious but tasteful and staff are always there to help! They also offer airport transfers if required. 

Staying in such an incredible hotel really made our visit to the beautiful Budapest even more unforgettable!

Провели в этом отеле 2 недели в феврале 2020 года. Архитектура отеля поражает роскошью в стиле дворца махараджей из восточной сказки. Заселили быстро. Как и во всех балийских отелях, на старте попросили депозит, заблокировав на кредитной карте определенную сумму. Когда депозит подошёл к концу, попросили подойти на рецепцию и пополнить. Номера. Стандартный номер большой и комфортный, единственный минус. В описании номера на различных сайтах есть два вида - на сад, и на бассейн. Стандартов с видом на океан в отеле нет в принципе. В действительности вид на сад - это вид на забор соседнего отеля, довольно не комфортный, а вот на бассейне - это вид внутрь отеля, на его прекрасную, ухоженную территорию, поэтому если у Вас просят доплату за вид внутрь отеля, на бассейн, оно того стоит. В отеле может не быть возможности сделать апгрейд на месте. Тоже касается и номеров с выходом в лагуну. Те которые обращены внутрь отеля, однозначно лучше. Уборка, стирка - безупречны. Каждый день в номер приносят воду. Кондиционер, несмотря на заверения, что можно выставить температуру, на Ваше усмотрение, живет своей жизнью, но все комфортно. Унитаз с подогревом. Холодильник холодит, а не просто гоняет воздух вентилятором. В номере есть кофемашина, чайник и весы. Меню рум сервиса скудное, но это компенсируется тем, что его разнообразят по Вашей просьбе. Например, вместо заявленного в меню салата Цезарь с курицей, можно заказать с креветкой или слабосоленным лососем. На территории два больших бассейна. Возле бассейнов бесплатно вода со льдом. Интернет на территории отеля  для подростков, заинтересованных в играх, оставляет желать лучшего. Рестораны, бары - в них есть всё и даже больше. Более разнообразных завтраков, Вы не встретите нигде. Меню европейской и азиатской кухонь - все на высочайшем уровне. Как-то за 5 минут до окончания завтрака (длительность с 6.30 до 10.30) окончились понравившиеся мне димсамы с креветкой на шведском столе. Набрав дугой еды, я села за стол и продолжила завтрак. Через 3 минуты ко мне подошла официант и принесла на тарелочке димсамы. В другой день, вечером, я забыла около бассейна крем от загара. На следующий день, была несколько огорчена, так как крем люксовой марки и в магазине при отеле купить его не смогла бы. В течение 30 минут крем нашли и мне вернули. Такие приятные мелочи, очень важны для комфортного отдыха. Безопасность в отеле на высоте. Спокойно оставляли на пляже и около бассейна телефоны и т.д.Рядом прекрасные возможности для сёрфинга. На территории ресторан в аквариуме. Порции маленькие, сеты дорогие, но учитывая, уникальность места приема пищи - один раз мы сходили. В целом, интересный опыт.
Отдельно хотим поблагодарить Наталью, русскоговорящую сотрудницу отеля. Помогала все время и это сделало наш отдых максимально комфортным. Дала нам перевод на русский меню из всех ресторанов, помогала бронировать ужины в ресторанах и процедуры в СПА, нашла для нас контакты фотографа и визажиста для фотосессии, помогала при заезде и расчете при выезде. Отвечала на наши вопросы, даже в свои выходные дни и делала максимум для нашего комфорта. В сравнении с отелем, где не было русскоговорящего сотрудника, было ощутимо комфортнее. Спасибо отелю, что имеет русскоговорящего сотрудника и Наталье, которая работает по очень высоким стандартам сервиса. Отдых превосходный. Мы были гостями Кемпински в Сент Мориц. Отель на Бали превзошёл ожидания и укрепил во мнении, что если в стране есть отель этой сетки, остановившись в нем, Вы проведёте свой отдых без неприятных сюрпризов.

My husband, daughter and I experienced a 7 night Luxury Escapes package staying in a Cliff Private Pool Ocean Junior Suite at the Apurva Kempinski from end January 2020. 

The hotel is currently the newest kid on the Nusa Dua block and it is architecturally beautiful. The hotel lobby alone is breathtaking and immense and you can’t help but be impressed by the grandeur of it all. As the lobby is completely open to the outdoors, the lobby temperature is generally the same as the outside temperature. There are no ceiling fans to help circulate the air, I assume because of the enormity of the lobby and they would probably take away from the design aesthetic. You realise just how warm it is when you step into the elevators as they are air conditioned to icy cold. 

If you stay in a Cliff Private Pool Ocean Junior Suite, check in takes place at the Cliff Lounge on level 9 of the hotel. The check-in process is fairly straight forward and comfortable in the Cliff Lounge. You’re provided with a cool drink and cold, scented hand towels. To create a tab for your room, you are asked to leave a deposit of appproximately IDR 7,000,000.

Check-in time is 3pm at the hotel - as we arrived at 2pm our room was not yet ready. However, we were escorted to the Cliff Lounge Dining on level 10 for afternoon tea which takes place from 2pm to 4pm daily for guests with a Cliff Private Pool Suite. The afternoon tea is fine but not the best around. There are plenty of Indonesian treats to taste, pastries, and fruit, but if you’re after anything else, there isn’t a huge selection. You have the option to sit inside or outdoors. Inside is lovely and cool.

Our suite was lovely, very spacious and well appointed. The room had a large dressing room, a sitting area, a king bed, an enormous bathroom, a big deck and an undercover pool (approximately 6mx3m in size). The pool is accessible from the bathroom via a sliding door set about 1 meter off the ground or from the outdoor deck. The bathroom has a marble floor - whilst beautiful, it is not practical as it is extremely slippery when wet. However, the bathroom contains Toto bathwear, including a Toto toilet with all the rinsing, cleansing and drying technology you need - I just loved it!  As requested, a crib had been provided for our 11 month old baby (together with some thoughtful extras - a baby blanket, baby pillow, and some Johnson & Johnson mini products). The hotel also provided us with a bottle steriliser. The mini bar contained limited drinks and snacks but was re-stocked daily. Bottled water was provided daily by house-keeping and if extra bottles were requested, they were provided free of charge.

We had to call/visit the Lobby several times during our stay for the following issues:
- our tv remote control was not working (twice)
- the water level in our pool dropped overnight (once)
- the pool filters did not turn on for a couple of days and the pool was getting dirty (once)
- the bathroom lights could not be turned off due to an electrical issue (once)
- to get a mosquito zapper because I was bitten 17 times by mosquitoes inside our suite one evening ( once).
These issues were all resolved without delay. However, it surprised us that there were so many matters requiring attention in such a short stay.

In addition to the above, we noted that there are water marks on some of the suite walls and ceilings, suggesting that there are water leaks. In fact, there was also water dripping onto the deck from the suite above ours - perhaps a leaking pool above? Based on the state of the pool in our suite, it wouldn’t surprise us if this hotel experiences significant water issues soon across its suites.

The other thing which we thought was interesting is that the hotel has not yet been open for a year and poor workmanship and wear and tear is already very visible. There are tiles that have fallen off the main pool, there are scratches on the interior surfaces of the lifts, the large glass feature which should stand in the middle of the pond at the entrance is missing, the cameras in a couple of the lifts are water-logged, the timber floor boards in our suite which meet the entrance of the bathroom are warped and swollen from water damage and the seals on our suite pool are lifting. Whilst these do not seem like such big issues now, we’re concerned about the state of the place in another year’s time.  There are also some ponds on either side of the stairs from the lobby to the main pool that do not appear to be finished. 

There are four restaurants on the property - Pala, Koral, Reef Beach Club and Izakaya by Oku. Pala and Reef Beach Club are the only all day dining options. 

Of the four, we thoroughly enjoyed Reef Beach Club - the food was great, the staff were lovely, the ambience was relaxed and comfortable, and the view of the beach excellent! A big shout out to Surya who was our little one’s holiday crush - he was so attentive, gentle and playful with her. She loved him! 

We had buffet breakfast and dinner at Pala as well as an a la carte lunch. The buffets were fine but I would skip the a la carte lunch. The menu is limited and the portions small.

Izakaya by Oku is also lovely. The food was decent and it was pretty good value for money. However, it just lacked a wow factor. 

Koral - Bali’s first aquarium restaurant - is absolutely over-priced. The food is good but the portions are very small. My husband’s main for example, had three gnocchi on it and one small prawn. In terms of the aquarium, it’s fine but the water is murky and the fish appear a little stressed. I also think the ambiance and vibe of  the restaurant which is pretty relaxed, just doesn’t suit the formality of the menu. In my opinion, it would be a great grill/diner or casual seafood restaurant.  It was also very warm in the restaurant the night we dined there, so cranking up the air conditioning would probably be a good idea. Unless, you really, really, really want to dine in an aquarium, I wouldn’t bother booking this restaurant.

We were able to have our daily buffet breakfast at the Cliff Lounge Dining, Pala Restaurant or the Reef Beach Club. Initially, we opted for breakfast at the Cliff Lounge Dining which is quieter than Pala and the Reef Beach Club. However, we realised later during our stay that Pala provided the best buffet breakfast - the food was hot (rather than lukewarm) and the food options available are greater. 

All up, the restaurants are nice but there are better places to dine in Nusa Dua such as Boneka at the St Regis or the Cafe at the Mulia. 

The spa at the Kempinski is very impressive - visually appealing and offering great treatments at fairly good prices. I had 100 minute relaxation massage which was lovely and I also had my hair styled one evening at the beauty salon. Both experiences were great. 

Given the size of the property, the Kempinski has buggies which can take you from hotel lifts to restaurants. We would make use of these during the hottest times of the day. 

If you’re wanting a hotel with a beautiful beach, the Kempinski is probably not the place for you. Whilst the beach is ok, it isn’t crystal blue waters. In fact, it’s full of sea weed and has a man-made wall to stop the sand from being swept away. At high tide, it’s fine for water activities such as kayaking or paddle boarding. At low tide, you can explore the rock pools. 

There’s only one shop at the hotel - a lovely souvenir/art gallery. It offers some great local items at decent prices. However, there are no other boutiques. This is somewhat surprising - I’s expect some fashion boutiques on the premises given the hotel markets itself as a luxury escapeZ

In terms of the staff, they are very welcoming, friendly and helpful. It was lovely to feel looked after during our stay.

Overall, we had a lovely stay at the Apurva Kempinski. It’s a beautiful hotel with lots of positives. I would give the hotel a 4 out of 5 rating - perfection for me would be a hotel that combined the beauty of the Apurva Kempinski architecture and design with the quality and polish of the Mulia and the St Regis.

The scale of this resort is mind-blowing and it must have been quite a reveal from drawings to the real thing as it stands today.  The aesthetics are spectacular and vista views from the sprawling lobby is simply magnificent.  Being that the resort has only had its soft launch and not fully 'complete', I'm a little more accepting of some of the little setbacks to what could well be a fabulous luxury resort.  Rooms and facilities are as expected of a resort of this caliber though some minor details need to be sorted out - the 'box' holder hiding the power outlets on the desk are useless as any kind of laptop charging adaptor will not fit in there.  More outlets are also definitely needed at the bedside tables since the cordless phone already uses the only one available on one side.  Sound insulation for the room doors are desperately needed as it's murder to be woken up by early checkouts, housekeeping in the wee hours of the morning as the noise comes through loud and clear.  The long corridors leading to the only elevators on both wings of the resort will need better ventilation and air-conditioning as it gets quite stifling in warm weather.  While we all want to slow down the pace when we're on holiday, the wait for a table for the breakfast buffet can be a problem during busy days, and if you're thinking 'I'll head down for brekkie at 9:30am' at the Pala where breakfast service runs till 10:30am, then you might not be too thrilled to have to wait 15-20 mins for a table, get seated and find out that they stop replenishing the buffet and only get to scrape the bottom of trays for the popular items.... Quality of food is good, though I expect that the menus will change and hopefully expand as they figure things out. The pools are very nice and inviting and the sheer size of the resort means there's plenty of space of everyone and fights for loungers and day beds not an issue at all.  A big plus!  I hope they sort out the beach a little because the sand is not pristine and the beachfront less inviting than I hoped.  Staff - no problems here at all.  Polite, attentive, helpful, they're all trained to be thorough.  Patience is key as rushing is definitely not the norm here.  You're on holiday, relax!  Would I recommend a visit?  Definitely!  I hope this resort doesn't fall victim to it's own design where the sheer size forces compromise elsewhere where it matters most.  It can be very good!

This brand has a high reputation that has been tarnished quite severely by the inadequate service delivered by this Nusa Dua offering. Despite the grand entrance and marbled floors, all that glitters is not gold. The service degradation continued post checkin beginning with the food quality throughout the resort. The Pala restaurant has years of improvements to get it any where near the 5 star tag advertised. Sub-quality food is superceded by food courts in shopping centres. Breakfast was very disappointing and to be told that the restaurant doesnt have bircher when the reef restaurant has it, means a poor coordination between FNB staff and managers. The food variety is well below that of most nusa dua resorts of which we have visited over the past 17 years. The dinner buffet is catered for chinese,japanese and indonesian guests not for other foreigners which made up a large proportion of the hotel occupancy demographic. I can’t even put 1 star because that is excessive for this place. The property is only great for taking photos, everything else is absolutely disgraceful. The food at Pala is disgusting, the quality of the food and the service is astounding. They always run out of things, like english breakfast tea and decaf coffees and the staff’s best answer is “we don’t have”, we run out of this and that. The chefs should be removed from this place and hire someone that is creative to work on quality and the art of food not quantity. I waited for my Bircher for 40 minutes and when it finally arrived, it had approx 250g of honey or condensed milk, it was so sickening and hard to swallow, it made me dry retch. The head chef attended our table and said that this is the standard of the bircher, and I asked what would happen if someone is diabetic? I asked if he tested it and if he could eat than maybe I could. He said thanks for the feedback, we will improve for next time. Why improving next time -do you think there will be a next time. Oh no! Seriously, we had so many issues in this hotel, for staff removing our belonging to extremely poor food quality. Stay away from this property, there are various hotels that offers world class service and the staff pays attention to details and they operate with high integrity and empathy. It’s the most disappointing trip that we had to Bali. The only saving grace for this hotel was the Japanese restaurant which had excellent service and high quality food. Thank you to Oka for the perfect recommendations.  Koral restaurant was overrated for all the hype and over priced for an aquarium view which you could get from various places in the world. The reef restaurant has different dress codes for males and females which is shocking in this day and age, with a lady allowed to enter with a singlet but not a man, and the excuse was "in case hair drops in the food", which was sad and funny given the amount of people with long hair in the place, and i have virtually no hair. The reef is a pool bar by the way for the people that dont know. The food was not cooked properly, the meat was still raw and the pizza was doughy, and the chef told us that the pizza oven was not ready yet to make them crispy. Why open a service when you are not ready, its like having a hospital with no equipment or doctors, you just wouldn't do it.

There are a few staff that do deserve a mention, such as Yudi in Pala who was very good and should be promoted to train others in customer service and attention to details, he was very attentive. Vali as FNB manager is very hospitable and empathetic and knows what the service needs to be like, and her team needs to step up and provide the service that guests expect

At checkout, was another experience with the assistant front office manager asking about our stay and when he was told that we would never return to this hotel due to poor service he didn't know how to respond and walked off. 

I could write much more on the issues this hotel has, but don't have anymore time. I don't want the standard robotic reply from management and QCI should be done throughout the stay.

So I'm actually writing this as I currently sit in my Apurva Kempinski room, but honestly I look forward to checking out tomorrow and moving to a different hotel as the stay is quite underwhelming. So you can get a sense of how the stay is going for me if I'm taking the time (about an hour) writing this review instead of being out of the room enjoying the resort.

Let me start by saying that the views from the lobby are very beautiful. It's the same sense you'll get from the Ritz Carlton next door (which after this stay I'll be staying at the RC next door for future visits, great stay at RC last year). It's a very high level scenic lobby, with elevators on the wings that bring you down to the beach level. 

Check-in: 2/5. Called to see if I could check in earlier, couldn't accomodate. Came at 
 a little before 3pm, had to wait for about 15 minutes as all the desks were full. Concierge took my bags from the car; didn't get a tag (even though I specifically asked if I needed a tag --turns out you need the tag. Am a frequent stayer at Kempinski Jakarta, so was hoping for an upgrade but didn't get it. Also turns out there's supposed to be an activity sheet at the reception, which I didn't get even though I asked. The person checking me in said it can be found in the hotel room -- which is not in the room. After check-in, someone else guided me to my room. Bags took FOREVER to get to my room. Knowing it would be so long, I wouldve brought them down myself. After waiting for like 30 mins.

Room: 4/5 Rooms are smaller than they are at the Ritz Carlton next door. I told them I'm celebrating a special occasion, but the room was not really prepared to be 'wow'ed. They ended up sending some snacks and a local wine (which I'm very thankful and appreciative for). AC works well and is cold, but seemed hard to control. Room cleanliness/house keeping could be improved. I remember killing a small spider behind the tub yesterday, and it's still there right now. 
Pleased with the turn down service, but it's quite early at 5pm to do turn down service. 

Restaurants: 1/5
So I visited the Koral restaurant during non-business hours, quite nice and happy to be able to take pics with the fish. Prices are quite high and there's a minimum charge of 500k IDR per pax to dine there. Specific tables cannot be reserved, and are first come first serve. 
I tried going back at night (without a reservation), but they were fully booked, so I went to check out Izakaya by Oku. I came without a reservation, but turns out Oku also fully booked and I was turned away. I do admit maybe not making a reservation was my fault, but it goes to show that walk-ins cannot/will not be served here.

Then I went to Pala restaurant (the main restaurant between the pools), but turns out they weren't doing ala-carte that night and only offered the dinner buffet option (460k ++) with nothing too impressive, so I went to Reef Beach club. 

Reef Beach Club had some live music playing outside, but it was super hot (even at night), so I wanted to sit inside. Tables were full, the circle ones with nice views were reserved. I initially sat at the bar (with my back facing the kitchen), but it was just really hot there, and the staff inside the bar seemed uncomfortable with me sitting there, so I lost my appetite and ended up returning to my room. Ordered room service, took about 30 mins, okay quality food.

Breakfast at Pala restaurant: 2/5 There can be a line to get in for breakfast (which is a bit of a turn off), so they will try to push you to have breakfast at the Reef bar on the beach. Foodwise a wide variety of food, taste is okay/a bit bland. Beautiful views if you are facing the beach. Staffed well, it was easy to call someone if you needed something. High end food offerings offered in other top hotels (Raffles, Mandarin Oriental, Mulia) like Egg Benedict, Smoked Salmon, NY Bagels, Cream Cheese, Ice Cream, Crepes, custom made juice, fresh coconuts (like in Rimba by Ayana), etc are non-existent. The pork bacon was not good. Nothing really left a 'wow' impression with me. I bought a nanny with me (as do most Indonesian families with small children); almost all Indonesian hotels don't mind if a nanny has a few bites of food, but the staff at Apurva asked me to pay the full breakfast price for my nanny -- which is okay, this is on me and I happily paid -- but I must say that this is the first time I've experienced this having traveled with children and stayed at dozens of different hotels.   

Kid's Club: 4/5 They are quite proactive and friendly in approaching guests during breakfast and informing them of the various activities for kids throughout the day. I happily signed up my children for the fish feeding at 9.15am, got a certificate. The Kids Club is currently in a temp room as it is still in soft-opening phase.

Swimming Pool: 5/5 Just the water scene from the lobby makes the resort beautiful. Kids pool is nice. There's also another shallower pool that is hot (not sure if intentional or unintentional). Easy to find a day bed. Ice cold water surrounding the pool (even though the water and cups were sometimes empty). Children's pool fun for the kids. 

Fitness Center/Gym: 1/5 I walked in at about 10, there was no one at the front desk. I walked around back and forth until I finally found a staff member cleaning the gym equipment. I was hoping to use the sauna, the gym pools, etc but apparently they have different hours from the gym, from 10am-9pm; where as it's written the gym hours are 6am-11pm. Not sure why the sauna's and pools aren't open the same time like every normal hotel (think Hyatt Jakarta). Tried to shower in the male locker room; water did not turn warm/hot. Shower Gel in my shower was empty, had to walk dripping wet to an empty shower next door and grab that shower gel bottle. Gym has a lot of classes, but dissapointed in the missed expectations here.

Sleep Quality: 0/5 So this is different than the room rating above. The bed and pillows are fine, but these walls are SO THIN. Holy crap have I never stayed in a luxurious hotel with this kind of sound pollution/cheap quality building material.  I stayed in room 61XX, and gosh you can hear someone walk by in slippers. I can hear the people in the room above. People talking walking by my room I can hear them clearly. There were a couple times I had to come out my room just myself to shut people up. I also tried to flag the security staff to help keep the noise level down to no avail. 

Check Out: Still TBD. I asked for a late check out just to see if they can accommodate, they said will get back later. We'll see if the concierge takes forever or not to come get my bags. Seems near the fitness center there's a departure lounge which I'm guessing if you have an evening flight or something.

So overall my conclusion is this: It's a beautiful resort, def 5 stars. My experience with the restaurants, the noise you can hear in the hallways/upstairs, and the fitness center made it a 1 star for me. I personally probably wouldn't pay the money again to stay here, I would choose one the resorts next door. But if you're curious about all the hype of the Apurva, then def give a chance and learn from my experience above.

+toller Check-in
+sehr grosse Suite
+hervorragendes Frühstück (Club Lounge)

-viele Unterhaltsprobleme
-Check-out

Lage:
Das Hotel liegt am Rande von Nusa Dua an einem Cliff. Für zum Beispiel einen Ausflug in die Bali Collection Mall muss man den kostenlosen Shuttle nutzen (2mal am Tag) oder man nimmt für rund 30 bis 50'000 IDR (rund 3 Euro) ein Taxi. Dasselbe gilt für Restaurants, wenn man nicht im Hotel essen mag. 

Die Reception befindet sich oben am Cliff. Im Steilhang hat man Zimmer mit eigenen Pools gebaut. Unten befinden sich nochmals Trakte mit Zimmern. In der Mitte der Anlage eine grosse Poollandschaft, die für jeden was bietet. Das Hotel hat einen schönen und auch ruhigen Strand. Der Strand eignet sich auch für Spaziergänge, jedoch ohne befestigten Weg. 

Check-in:
Als Suite Gast hatten wir einen super tollen Check-in. Wir wurden schon bereits beim Aussteigen vom Auto begrüsst. Anschliessend ging es in die Lobby, wo uns ein Willkommensgetränk gereicht wurde. Der Hotel Manager begrüsste uns ebenfalls, was ich persönlich sehr schätze und mich auch jedesmal in einem Hotel freut. Danach ging es direkt aufs Zimmer, wo dann die Check-in Formalitäten erledigt wurden. Einfach genial. Genau so muss ein Check-in ablaufen. 

Zimmer:
Wir hatten die Apurva Prestige Suite. Eine wirklich tolle Suite mit rund 200 Quadratmetern, eigenem Pool und eine tolle Aussicht zum Meer hin. Das Design ist sehr schön. Auch zum wirklichen Verweilen in der Suite gemacht. Unterteilt in ein Wohnzimmer mit mehreren Sitzmöbeln und TV, Gäste WC, ein Schlafzimmer mit Sofa und Schreibtisch und TV, ein riesiges Bad mit Dusche und Wanne inklusive fantastischer Aussicht, ein Dusche-WC. Ein riesen grosses Ankleidezimmer gehört ebenfalls dazu. Es gibt aber auch noch Verbesserungspotential: so wäre ein Telefon und ein Abfalleimer im Wohnzimmer bequem. Des weiteren könnte man auf der grossen Terrasse noch eine kleine Sitzecke mit Tisch machen. Eine Essecke fehlt gänzlich in der Suite. 

Hotel Anlage: 
Die Anlage ist gross und am ersten Tag etwas verwirrend. Aber nach nicht mal 1 Tag habe ich mich sehr gut zurecht gefunden. Von der Lobby aus kann man das ganze sehr gut überblicken. Eine Etage unterhalb der Lobby befindet sich der Spa und Fitnessbereich. Von dieser Ebene aus kann man auch zu Fuss die Treppe hinunter zum Pool nehmen (ungefähr 200 Stufen) . Aber auch nur ein Bild machen ist von da aus toll. Eindrücklich ist auch der künstliche Wasserfall der über das ganze künstlich angelegte Cliff via Stufen hinunter rauscht. 

Unten gibt es Pools für jeden. Für Kleinsten gibt es einen Babypool, für die etwas grösseren Kinder einen Pool mit Rutschbahn und anderen Wasserelemente. Der Erwachsenen Pool liegt etwas weiter zum Strand, durch ein Restaurant getrennt von den Kindern. Sehr gute Lösung. So können die Kinder spielen und stören dabei die kinderlosen Erwachsenen nicht. Die Gartenanlage ist relativ schlicht mit Palmen und Wiesen gestaltet, aber sehr gut unterhalten. Leider gilt dies nicht für das ganze Ressort. 

Alle Wasserelemente scheinen etwas mehr Pflege nötig zu haben. Bei praktisch allen Pools gibt es defekte Unterwasserleuchten (auch beim Suite eigenen Pool). Die Überlauf-Auffangbecken sind ebenfalls nicht 100 Prozent sauber, voller Blätter und z.B. bei unserem Privatpool schon mit einer leichten Schlammschicht versehen. Auch das Wasser selbst hatte viele Schwebstoffe enthalten. Noch sichtbarer beim künstlichen Wasserfall, wo extremes Algenwachstum von vernachlässigte Pflege zeugt. Ein Bisschen Algen ginge ja noch, es sollte schon natürlich wirken, aber Algenteppiche und starke Schaumbildung sollte nicht sein. Bei Nacht fallen dann auch noch die defekten Lampen der Fassadenbeleuchtung auf, auch das sollte in so einem Hause nicht unbedingt sein. 

Restaurants:

Izakaya by OKU
Ein japanisches Restaurant mit hervorragender Küche. So gibt es auch die Möglichkeit sich selbst ein Menü zum Fixpreis zusammenzustellen. Unbedingt probieren. Japanische Küche besteht nicht nur aus sashimi und shushi. 

Reef Beach Club:
Hier gibt es für jeden was. Von Steaks über Fisch oder Burger bis zu indonesischen Spezialitäten findet man hier alles. Direkt am Strand mit Sitzplatz aussen oder innen. Die Küche macht einen guten Job, der Service ebenfalls. 

Pala Restaurant:
Ein Buffetrestaurant. Habe ich nicht besucht, da ich nicht so der Buffet Gast fürs Abendessen bin. Da gehe ich es lieber etwas gemütlicher an und nehme mir Zeit. 

Koral:
Ein Unterwasserrestaurant, welches ich leider nicht besuchen konnte. Selbst während meinem insgesamt 10 tägigen Besuch in Nusa Dua  unmöglich einen Tisch zu bekommen. Nicht mal als Gast von einer der grössten Suiten. Das kenne ich andernorts anders. Nach einem schriftlichen Statement an die Lady in red, gab es plötzlich einen Tisch, das am 31.12. Wir lehnten diesen Termin ab, da es an diesem Abend, wie in vielen anderen Resorts auf der Insel, nur ein überteuertes Menü.

Frühstück:
Als Suite Gast nimmt man das Buffet im Club ein. Hier findet man eines der tollsten Buffets das ich in den letzten Jahren gesehen habe. Die Auswahl ist gross. Es gibt westliche wie auch asiatische Spezialitäten, verschiedene kalte Sachen, verschiedene Brötchen und Gebäcke, Eierspeisen ab Karte, etc. Einfach nur toll. 

Club:
Den Suiten Gästen steht ein Club zu Verfügung mit separatem Pool und Lounge. Ganztags gibt es hier alkoholfreie Getränke umsonst. Nachmittags gibt es Tee mit Sandwiches, am Abend alkoholhaltige Getränke mit Canapés. Leider ist hier die Auswahl an Speisen sehr dürftig. Auch das Service Niveau ist schlechter als z. B.  beim Frühstück. So ist die Qualität massiv unterschiedlich: mal bekommt man ein feuchtes Tuch, Mal nicht. Mal bekommt man Nüsse gereicht mal nicht. Von 3 Besuchen am frühen Abend gab es einmal Tüchlein und Nüsse. An den anderen beiden Tagen sassen wir jeweils am falschen Tisch. Bei gewissen anderen Tischen wurden Nüsse und Co gereicht, uns nicht. 

Check-out:
Der Check-out war schlimm. Wir haben 2 mal bestätigt bekommen, dass unser Gepäck abgeholt werde auf dem Zimmer. Das klappte nicht. Als wir versuchten telefonisch jemanden zu erreicht, nahm niemand den Anruf entgegen. Also blieb uns nichts anderes übrig als das Gepäck selber zu tragen. Das darf definitiv nicht passieren!
Superb hotel. Period. From any aspect.

Set in the heart of Budapest and bustling downtown, sits the Kempinski Hotel Corvinus behind a facade of glass and stone. It is differentiated from the 19th century buildings around it as the Corvinus is a modern twist on the neighbourhood. It is front and centre to Erzebit Park and the Budapest Eye, a giant Ferris wheel ( so I can go around in circles). The terrace side of the Corvinus backs on to Fashion Street with designer stores like Tommy Hilfiger, Perry Ellis and the like as well as quaint cafes. The envious location of the hotel makes it a great kick-off-point for exploring top attractions like the Parliament buildings, the Chain Bridge and the Danube. The architecture of the glass clad Corvinus is structured to assume the shape of the letter 'C'....the Big C.

The hotel entrance is slightly hidden, but once you are in, the lobby deserves an impressive.. wow. It is refreshing open, with high ceilings and circular space.Two large wall panels shoot up to the ceiling, projecting outward to form an elaborate and eye- popping canopy. The dark marble floor and deep earthy tones inspire a cool and calm attitude. A beautiful, piano-key looking, multi-coloured, inlaid tile, walk-way snakes around the floor leading to a modern staircase. The ground floor has been designed as a mini promenade with retailers such as deli's, cafe's, a gift shop and a gallery.

Rooms are sizeable, and decorated with classic creams and gold trim. Art Deco design predominates as with the headboard and the radius engineered furniture. The beds are Uber-comfortable and with pillow menus, this makes for a delightful sleep. A high-tech infotainment system and a smart LED TV top off the room's electronic appliances. Baths are decked in shiny marble, adding a dab of pop to the walls and floors and are further embellished by steel fittings.The Corvinus has a fine repertoire of rooms with additional upscale amenities as the category rises.

The Corvinus comes with a chest of choices to enjoy culinary pleasures and liquid bread.The Es Bistro is a great eatery with a brasserie ambience and open for all meal day-parts. This buzzy restaurant serves up a fantastic breakfast and is also a relaxed place for dinner with good regional food. There are lots of chalk boards with quips and menu specials. When the weather cooperates, the street side terrace on Fashion Street is popular. Nobu is the 'new kid on the block' as far as Budapest restaurant's go, but enjoys critical, worldly acclaim for its culinary delicacies. The menu is a melange of Japanese-Peruvian with Hungarian ingredients. I tried the Black Cod in Misco....super duper. The Blue Fox is the place for your custom crafted cocktails, and in addition, on the weekend this place rocks to the sound of a DJ's curated playlists or live performances. The Living Room is a Hungarian Coffee House (puts Starbucks to shame) offering teas/coffee strudel and other sweet niceties.

The Spa, an opulent Asian oriented facility designed to rebalance and rejuvenate the muscles after a hectic day of sightseeing.There is a fine array of therapeutic treatments at the Spa as well as a modern gym and pool with whirlpool tub functionality.

The hotel is home to the Unique Corvinus Gallery, which specializes in Modern Hungarian Art. As well, rooms and public spaces are dedicated to the Kempinski's wide range of curated art.

Service was top-notch and the moxi of the staff proved instrumental in the exceptional execution of their chores. The concierge team was amped up to provide attraction guidance to help guests resolve their sightseeing issues.

So, if you want to stay at a star hotel, with plenty of in-house food options and a location that promotes easy access to the must-see attractions...then try the ............Kempinski...
Hotel Corvinus......The Big C.

Der erste Eindruck ist überwältigend und wird durch die riesige Empfangshalle gekrönt. Die Holzarbeiten sind echt beeindruckend.
Leider lief der check in nicht reibungslos. Wir wurden einfach vergessen. Wir wurden vom Auto zu einer Sitzecke geführt und dort einfach vergessen. 
Das Gebäude ist echt riesig, was zu entsprechend langen Wegen führt. Manchmal sind die Fahrstühle um die 15 Etagen von Strandhöhe zur Rezeption zu überwinden ein echter Engpass mit langen Wartezeiten verbunden. Insgesamt wird immer noch gebaut am und im Hotel, zum Teil sind sogar schon Reparaturarbeiten im Gange. Das Restaurant Koral war auch im September 2019 immer noch nicht eröffnet.
Die Zimmer sind auf jeden Fall 5 Sterne, sehr groß und im Bad gibt es die wirklich praktischen japanischen Toiletten. Einziges Manko am Nachttisch gibt es keine Steckdosen oder USB Ladestecker.  
Der grosse Infinity Pool ist ebenfalls toll und es gibt ausreichend Liegen und Sonnenschirme. Der Strand ist nicht so toll und künstlich angelegt. Zum Wassersport und schwimmen eigentlich kaum geeignet.
Das Hauptrestaurant Pala kommt morgens schon an seine Grenzen hinsichtlich der Belegung. An mehreren Tagen war es morgens mit Wartezeit verbunden. Die Auswahl zum Frühstück ist gut und ausreichend.
Abends zum Büffet würde ich das nicht behaupten. Das Büffet ist sehr aus Gäste aus dem Asiatischen Raum ausgerichtet (China/Indien). Aus europäischer Sicht findet sich dagegen wenig Auswahl. Auch frischen Fisch sucht man meist vergeblich. Die Varaiation innerhalb einer Woche sind sehr gering, d.h. das Büffet ist fast jeden Tag identisch. 
Aus der Beach Bar könnte man deutlich mehr machen, tolle Lage aber nur wenig Auswahl an Essen (mehr Snacks). Das sollte ausgebaut werden.

Alles in Allem ein tolles Hotel, zu 5 Sternen reicht es jedoch nicht. dazu ist das Hotel vielleicht auch einfach zu Groß um einen solchen service durchgehend zu erfüllen. Dabei war das Hotel im September nicht voll belegt.

From the check-in to the check-out we felt like we were in heaven. We had a hard time leaving the resort. The main building is enormous a lot of Balinese details are incorporated in the building. The lobby is so beautiful, when you enter the hotel for the first time, you do not know where to look. 
The left and right wing are symmetrical. The architect did do a great job and now the hotel management is also doing a magnificent job.

The hotel is still in the soft opening phase. This means the villas are not open, one of the restaurants is unfortunate not open and not all the rooms are available. 

We stayed in an Apurva Ocean view suite. This suite is 200m2 and has a separate living and bedroom. The pool on the balcony is very large. The pool is semiprivate. From some parts of the hallway you can look into the room we had, but mainly it is private. The decoration of the room is beautiful done.
When we arrived some cookies, snacks and fruit were placed in the room. A bit too much to eat everything. The next day the cookies and snacks were taken away from the room. I think it’s better to place every day some snacks instead of a lot of them and take them away.  

The bathroom consists of two showers, I loved this. The toilets are TOTO toilets and have warm seating. Wow! 
In the room was a kind of walk-in closet. I missed some shelves, the hangers were in abundance.

Pool service is very good but varies from day to day. Some days we got a lot of cold refreshments other days only one refreshment was given. Maybe this is on purpose, but for me it did not look consistent, but who cares as I loved the refreshments, this is something you hardly get these days in hotels anymore. So I loved the concept. What I really loved are the corners at the pool (niches) were you can lay. The pool is large, but because of the layout it looks secluded. Well thought off!

All the restaurants are great. They close early. Saw on the information card that they close at 10PM, thought 10PM is the last entrance possibility but it means 10PM is the last order. One night we were having main course and the lady came to the table and asked if we wanted to have dessert or coffee as the kitchen was supposed to close.  Try the Japanese restaurant, this is perfect and they have a great menu concept. 

We missed a real evening bar in the lobby level. The only evening bar is rooftop with stunning views, but there was a lack of ambiance. Maybe this has to settle in the near future, but for us it was too clean / neat. And because of the layout it was difficult to see if others are at the bar or not.

The beach is hardly used, they do have great cleaning of the beach and have a dam to break the waves. This is well done. 

Internet all over the resort is ok.  It’s not strong.  Had difficulties uploading my pics from the Cloud. When I checked in into another hotel, the pics were immediately synchronized. They need to upgrade the speed in my opinion.

In my opinion the other 5-star resorts need to look out as this hotel has the possibility to become the best hotel of Nusa Dua when fully operational. We loved it very much. We will definitely return to this hotel when we will stay in Bali again. It’s not cheap, but it’s well worth it.

9 night family stay with a 5 year old and a 3 year old child in a lagoon access room (we  found the upgrade to lagoon access worth it but not to the cliff suites as didn't look suitable for young kids). Staff kindly made up a sofa with sheets free of charge for our 5 year old and provided 6pm check out after some pushing.
Stunning architecture, comfortable rooms and great pools. Large kids pool with 2 slides for toddlers and 2 slides - 1 double - for older kids; adjacent shallow baby pool; 60mt main pool with shallow sides with lounges and 40m pool outside Reef beach club. Beach is pleasant but has seaweed as is common in the region. 
Reasonable complementary 'extras': free kayaks and paddle boards; afternoon rosella drinks and jelly by the pool; self-service infused water and sunscreen by the pool; yoga/meditation at 7am and 5pm and unlimited bottled water in rooms. Kids club staff were lovely and complimentary activities were good: face painting; dress ups; crafts; kite making/flying; pool activities. Playground is great but not shaded till around 4pm. Permanent kids club will be adjacent to the playground and won't be finished before December. While it feels like a trek to the lobby (250 steps are fun if you're up for it; otherwise there are lifts) as the main pool area is flat and not that big, we never found the need to use a buggy.
Staff were all friendly but the resort isn't quite there yet with a truly 5 star service offering. We had several miscommunications ordering food and pricing/sizing inconsistencies with the limited kids food offering. On our first night, we questioned the AU$40+ charge for 2 kids pastas at reef beach club which was reduced to AU$30 but for about the same price the Ritz next door offers a 3 course kids menu with a drink (admittedly at a great price). Staff don't seem trained to go the extra mile and the resort was missing some extras associated with a top 5 star hotel - no kiddy welcome on check in (but got a lovely card and stuffed toy as a farewell from the friendly kids club team) or explanation of services offered; no turn down treat; regular order not remembered; random checking of room number while lounging by the pool. Falls a bit short of the Ritz service next door but rooms are commensurately cheaper (food at Kempinski seemed more expensive and a bit overpriced but not extortionate by international 5 star hotel standards). Didn't quiet nail the family offering nor the couples offering based on other reviews - Reef Club not providing a kids menu but allowing kids is the prime example. But good for families and couples (the club lounge looked nice and more couples-focussed with its own pool but allowed kids) if you're relaxed in your approach. 
Luxury escapes package with daily cocktail (can order at Pala rooftop and have delivered to Pala restaurant downstairs) and 1 daily 3 course lunch or dinner (and free 3 course meals for accompanying kids under 6) was a great deal. We liked breakfast and dinner buffets at Pala - we thought it was truly international with Indonesian, Chinese, Japanese, Indian, Middle Eastern and 'Western' and great pastries, ice cream (dinner only) and desserts. Reef Beach club has an all day menu with quite a lot of choice including on the luxury escapes package. We found that 1 meal voucher at Reef beach club was often enough for 2 adults (especially as we had 2 kids under 6 who could get 3 courses each free but always shared fewer courses). You can ask for iced water at Reef beach club rather than bottled. Our go to lunch was nasi goreng and nasi lemak by the pool (the cheapest main at ~AU$15). We went to the Japanese Izakaya once for dinner - pleasant but not exceptional Japanese and not as relaxing with young kids as had a fine dining atmosphere and only 1 kids choice of teriyaki chicken and miso. The luxury escapes included airport transfer can be taken from the hotel to the airport. Have attached photos of activity schedules and some menus.
Would consider coming back on the luxury escapes package but might hesitate booking without half-board given high food prices- especially for kids. Not an issue if you're happy to go out or order in. For our young family, so far the Sofitel has the strongest pull - for about the same price, we got a pool suite with kids sleeping in the separate lounge room and we could have drinks and a light dinner at the club lounge while our kids were fed and entertained at the kids club.

We stayed for 6 days in late Sept 2019 and like to experience new resorts hence why we chose the Apurva. 
We have been to Bali before and stayed at other 5 star resorts so my review is based on what my expectations of the Apurva should be so it can be competitive with the likes of the Ayana and the Mulia and other high end 5 star resorts, especially when their rates are about the same

There is no doubt the building is impressive and so is the foyer, the gardens are young still but once they grow they will be lush and tropical, but there is lots of open grass area that may have better been utilised with tropical garden beds . The building is built to mimic a temple as it slopes up the cliff and some Balinese style sculptures etc, would look good in the gardens, these are lacking throughout the resort.

A few staff mentioned to us that they are still  in “soft” launch mode which seems strange after receiving guests for 6 months now. I understood what this meant when we got a note that they had to close all the hot water from 11:30pm to 3:30pm the next day for maintenance. Also one morning we had no water pressure at all and had to have maintenance come to fix it, they did this effectively and quickly but these kind of basics should have been sorted out by now. 

There are 3 pools, all very big and spacious but somehow lack the resort pool vibes, they really needed a pool bar built in the main pool. Many resorts that we have visited worldwide that have pool bars have great vibes, it’s a great way to get guests to mix as well. Really need some music too around the pool areas. The Mulia does this very well.

The pool near the Reef beach bar has nice cabanas to lounge on but seems very sterile. Also the steps to get out of the pool is at the end where the deck and eating tables are, that means you stumble out wet over guests sitting nearby, the ergonomics of this does not seem planned out properly, way too hard to get in and out if your not fully mobile.

The timber decking around the pool areas and at breakfast area was showing sighs of wear already. In some places it was totally uneven and had huge gaps, this was definitely a sign of poor workmanship.

In the main pool as you enter, there are a series of steps till you get to the main depth of the pool, due to the colour of the tiles, they are very hard to see and on many occasions I saw people missing them. 

Fumigation needs to be stepped up around the resort too as on many occasions these has been ants on the sun beds and tables, especially if there has been food or drinks nearby. The same happened one morning when we ate outside for breakfast.

The staff are very friendly and really try their best, some seem a little inexperienced and this may be their first jobs in hospitality but they do go out of their way to make your stay as comfortable as possible. What seemed to lack were leaders who can direct others.

We left the resort on 2 occasions in the evening and it was easy to get a taxi to do so, waited only up to 5 minutes.

The checkin process was quick and smooth, a host shows you to your room and there is a buggy service too as some rooms are quite a walk away. Our room was great, could not fault the bathroom or sleeping area, we even had a extra bed for our teenage daughter and still had plenty of room, lovely balcony with view to the pool and couch area too. Housekeeping was very efficient and the standard of cleanliness was high.

The beach is nothing special and I hardly saw anyone in the sea water during our stay.

We ate at all places available during our stay. The underwater Koral restaurant was still not open, this will definitely be a draw card  when it’s up and running.

We had breakfast at the Pala every day, huge buffet selection but overly geared to an Asian cuisine and this is obviously due to the majority of guests being Asian. Would have been good if the western area was a bit more imaginative and not the same every day. It did get very busy at around 9am daily so sometimes there was a wait for a table. The café latte was of good quality too. When they get busy they find it hard to accommodate guests and have been directing guests to the Reef beach restaurant that serves a ala carte breakfast menu, I did notice some guests not liking this as it’s obviously not the same as the buffet.

One evening we had the buffet at the Pala for dinner, it was totally Asian, once again, not really catering for western cuisine. The offering is no where near the quality and variety that the Mulia offers for the same price. This is definitely an area that needs some improvements to be made. The dessert area at the was not extensive either, I expected more.

We loved the Japanese restaurant definitely the best of all the eating areas. Food and service was amazing, definitely worth a visit during your stay. 

The Reef Beach Restaurant opened in early September, so the menu looks like it was trying to be finalised still, our meals were ok, but nothing fabulous, the calamari lacked seasoning. In the evening there has been a live band playing on the sand which was great for creating some ambience 

The pool menu was not extensive and the meals were a subset of the Reef Beach restaurant and too heavy, some lighter pool bites would be better. Pasta around the pool, surely not. Pool service to get drinks and food was slow at times, one day we were given free ice cream as the food took too long. Also the pricing on some menu items seems not right, vegan californian rolls for 180++, yet fish and chips was around 195++ and Nasi Goreng 120++

The pool drinks menu was very limited too, I’m at a resort, I want lots of choice of exotic cocktails and mocktails, that’s what holidays are for, some indulgence . Only 6 cocktails to choose from, surely this can’t be right? Also the presentation of the drinks is nothing too exotic, it’s quite basic actually for a resort offering. 

At times it was hard to get a pool menu, would be much easier to have them at the table/seat areas so it’s easier to access. And when you got the menu, it was then not easy to track someone down to order. Free Pool service during the afternoons offered small shots of drinks and your sun glasses cleaned too, which was a nice touch.

On one occasion at the pool we were greeted with a welcome drink and a cold face washer, this only happened on one day of the 6 days, so if it’s meant to be a welcome procedure then it’s definitely not followed consistently.

The bar above the Pala Restaurant has so much potential but nobody is utilising it, once again the drinks menu is not extensive and there is no vibes to stick around, some live music or maybe some offers may entice people. The views are great from this roof top bar.

There are some great areas to observe the views from the lobby but it was not easy to work out how to get a drink up there, we finally found the Deli and had a coffee but so much more can be done to utilise that amazing foyer area and viewing deck, a high tea would be a great offering.

I did not try the spa as I thought the prices were way too expensive, I could get cheaper services in my home country, some specials to entice clientele would have been great. A facial worked out to $160 AUD inclusive of taxes and service charges. I know this is a 5 star resort but these prices for Bali are just way over the top.

Overall I’m glad we chose to stay here but after 6 months since opening I would have expected that everything would be running a lot more like a true 5 star experience. They really need to catch up and do it quickly as there is so many great choices at a similar price point. 

You can’t run this hotel on the grandiose building and foyer, you need the staff, amenities and processes to make it 5 star

this is roughly my 20th visit to budapest for business, and my first and hopefully last stay at the kempinski.

the positives -- reception staff was very friendly, and check-in process was quick.  went to nobu and the food was decent (would never be confused for Tokyo or NY, but still, flavors were interesting and tasty).

the negatives -- not sure if the entire hotel is this way, but my room was desperately in need of a refresh.  dated decor looks like it was the original furniture from 30 years ago, and lacked the style and design of competing hotels in budapest.  air conditioner in room did not work well.  would not cool down no matter how low i set it.  breakfast buffet was below average - everything i tried was overcooked.  even though my coworker and i went pretty early, the cheese was "sweating", i.e. it'd been out at room temperature for ages already. 

worsts -- my room was entirely missing a shower (as in there was literally no shower head)...and now, i just came back from dinner at 10:00pm and noticed my room had not been turned down - curtains open, no water, no bath mats laid out, etc.  i called reception and asked for turn down service -- the receptionist said, "yeah, sure."  25 minutes later, a woman showed up at the door, said hello, and literally handed me 2 bottles of water, 2 towels, and a bathmat and said "bye bye"

there is no universe where that is even 3-star service.

will not stay here again until they get professional staff and a renovation.

Nachdem ich die mitunter negativen Kommentare der vorherigen Gäste gelesen habe, muss ich hier meine Eindrücke von unserem Aufenthalt festhalten.

Das Hotel ist ein fantastisches 5* Luxushotel! Wir waren schon in einigen 5* Hotels in Europa, aber das The Apurva Kempinski auf Bali ist überragend.
Ab dem Zeitpunkt der Ankunft haben wir uns rundum professionell und herzlich umsorgt gefühlt. 
Die Lobby ist einzigartig und im Zusammenspiel mit dem Ausblick aufs Meer atemberaubend.
Auch unser Zimmer war wirklich sehr komfortabel und mit allem Nötigen ausgestattet. Für unseren Honeymoon wurde außerdem zur Anreise ein Treatment bereitgestellt, worüber wir uns sehr gefreut haben.
Die Anlage selbst wirkt sehr groß, allerdings haben wir nie den Eindruck gehabt, dass die Gäste des Hotels geballt an einem Ort (abgesehen vom Frühstück) aufeinander treffen.
Das nächste Highlight war das Hauptrestaurant, welches mitten am Pool gelegen ist. Das reichhaltige Buffet war einfach unglaublich gut und der Service dazu sehr aufmerksam. 
Am dritten Abend hatten wir Pasta, da wir an den anderen Abenden bereits das Buffet hatten. Leider waren die beiden Speisen nichts Besonderes, sondern schmeckten eher einfach nach Öl. Daher empfanden wir dieses a la carte Abendessen als überteuert und nicht genügend für ein 5* Hotel.
Ein anderer Kritikpunkt ist die viel zu kalt eingestellte Klimaanlage im Restaurant und in den Aufzügen. Es ist unverständlich weshalb die Räume so sehr heruntergekühlt werden, dass man tatsächlich friert und sich sehr unwohl fühlt!
Auffällig war, dass die asiatischen Gäste dieser Umstand wohl nicht störte und sich während des Essens drinnen aufhielten. Alle anderen Gäste saßen an den Tischen im Außenbereich.

Positiv hervorheben möchte ich das Fitnessstudio! Es war das beste Hotel-Gym in dem ich bisher war und kann mit jedem Gym in einer europäischen Stadt mithalten.
Meine Frau hat auch eine Spa Anwendung genutzt, wovon sie ebenfalls begeistert war.
Der große Pool lädt sehr zum Schwimmen und Verweilen ein. Auch der Service dort war sehr aufmerksam und hilfsbereit.
Das Schwimmen im Meer ist ebenfalls möglich, allerdings fanden wir den Pool besser, da im Meer einige Plastikstücke schwimmten und die Strömung recht stark (aber nie gefährlich) war. Der Strand selbst ist aber sehr sehr sauber und man kann Kayak fahren und Standup paddling probieren.
Sollte man einen späten Flug haben, kann man auch die Departure-Lounge mit Duschen, Umkleiden, Snacks, Drinks und einem Aufenthaltsraum nutzen, was wir gerne genutzt haben und als sehr angenehm empfunden haben.

- Hervorheben möchte ich noch den Servicemitarbeiter Dewa! Er hat unseren Aufenthalt zu etwas ganz Besonderem gemacht und sich sehr gut während des Dinners, Frühstück und am Pool um uns gesorgt. -

Der Aufenthalt war ein wunderbarer Abschluss unseres Honeymoons!

We have just completed an 8 night stay at this property and can honestly say that we loved it. We had read a lot of negative reviews regarding the state of the property(under construction), and the service level (lack of). After staying here i can say that I don’t know where these reviews come from as the property, even though it is not complete is amazing and the service level and genuine friendliness of all of the staff is some of the best I have experienced anywhere in the world. So to break it down:

Hotel Lobby/Checkin: A breathtaking lobby greets you on arrival and the check in process was quick and easy including welcome drinks and cold towels. We stayed in a cliff top junior suite so check in was at the executive lounge. 

Rooms: large and well laid out. Lots of PowerPoints and amenities. Well cleaned daily, and serviced twice a day. Nice private pool to dip in the afternoon and brilliant ocean views. Large comfortable bed and good tv with lots of channels if that’s your thing.

Pools: some of the best I have experienced. The pool is large but it’s design makes your area feel private and relaxing. Good temp and nice infinity feel looking out to the ocean.

Food: due to the soft opening it is a little limited right now but will have 2 more options opening before the end of the year.  What they do have is generally of good quality and quantity. Nice restaurant environment too.

Executive lounge: If you can get access through your room type to this area you won’t be disappointed. Staff are awesome and helpful, food for breakfast is great, afternoon drinks all day and cocktails at night which are not your average one cocktail of the day at happy hour watered down but a big selection of cocktails, beers, wine and mocktails. Canapés nice also.

Service: nothing is too much trouble or too hard for this team and they make you feel special in every interaction. Spa was also excellent.

You won’t regret experiencing the Apurva Kempinski. Will definitely be back.

I stayed in this property for 4 nights recently .At the beginning it was a bit of a disappointed for the hotel theme, ambiance and decor.
The check in was very swift and the ladies in the red Ms Anna and Ms Dijana were very efficient and attentive to the guests request . They upgraded me to a junior suite which was a pretty small room and it did not feel comfortable at all. They were kind enough to show me 2 other types of room till I chose the one which was recently renovated and was brighter with a well lit bathroom.
There were certain flaws in this hotel which I  addressed directly to the Front office Manager Mr Gyorgy Podhoranyi who amazingly handled the situation and compensated for the deficiencies in a very hospitable way.
He also generously offered vouchers for the breakfast buffet for 2 days.
He  definitely proved and demonstrated  excellent  guest service experience.

The hotel surely needs to improve the guest service operating service as unfortunately they did not have separate buttons for the different departments and after 10 pm the calls were directed to the reception who were disaster at times and could not fulfill the guest requirements.

The bathroom was well lit and bright with easy uncomplicated gadgets only point was the hand shower was fixed and could not be adjusted in length or  change the direction of the flow,in other words would have been nice to have the freedom of choosing the type of flow and have a massage shower instead.
the lighting was  also nice and bright only not to many electric sockets present .
The sound proofing  needs to be improved too as you could hear a lot of disturbances from the corridor outside the room.

The location was directly on the shopping street with many restaurants around but unfortunately no view of the river from the hotel.

The concierge staff were one of the most unfriendly men I have come across.
We tried to experience them twice only and then decided to manage by ourselves by the reception they give us.

The breakfast was good nothing exceptional and the staff were very friendly and helpful.

The spa is definitely a MUST to try especially the body massage given by the therapist Erica was super. She surely had magical hands which would literally destress you and put you fast asleep.

The manager Ms Sophia was one of the most friendly and welcoming human being ever with a great sense of humor.

She would welcome you with a lovely smile and offer you a herbal ginger tea which was so refreshing.

I would strongly recommend this to all guests.

Začnem asi príchodom do hotela, ktorý bol naozaj veľmi pekný. Na recepcií bola celkom milá paní, ktorá nam dala karty od izby. Neplatil som nič, prišli sme na jednu noc, rezervoval som si hotel na portlali, v cene som si zarezervoval aj vyhlad na mesto a ranajky. Avšak k mojmu prekvapeniu som dostal výhlad na starú budovu... 
Povedal som si dobre, zrejme to bude teda stiahnuté z konečnej sumy. Izba bola veľmi čistá, dobré bolo aj to že nás uvítala chyžná s papučami a mineralkou. 
Prešli sme sa mestom, ktoré nás ohromilo hlavne svojou plnosťou, avšak celkový dojem zničil aj to že sme sa nemali kde poriadne najesť. Samé fastfoody a podobné ... Tak sme sa s priateľkou rozhodli, že navštívime reštauráciu pod naším hotelom, ktorý patrí k hotelu. Výber jedál som však čakal úplne iný. Ani jedno tradičné maďarské jedlo v jedálnom lístku. Povedali sme si fajn a náš hľad riešil mcdonalds. Deň druhý nás vyrušila chyžná tým že zaklopala a hneď vošla dnu. S takýmto prístupom som sa nestretol snáď nikde v zahraničí. Chyžné by mali mať zoznam kto na raňajkách je a kto nieje. Nevadí... Boli sme zvedavý na raňajky. Šli sme dole, a videli sme že majú celkom chaos, nestíhal absolutne nikto. Slečna ktorá nás išla usadiť obkrúžila celú reštauráciu lebo nevedela kde ma volné miesto. Usadila nás k rozheglanému stolu, po chvíli sme si presadli. Nitko sa nás neprišiel spýtať či chceme kávu. Tak sme nahaňali čašníkov pohladmy. Uloženie buffetov bolo chaotické, medzi cerealiami boli vajcia na tvrdo, čo som tiež nikdy nikde nevidel a prepáčte ale aby za 35e na osobu na raňajkach bola obschnutá zelenina o osmej ráno? Nedoložené salámy, a bar zahltený ovocím a žiadne šťavy nič? 
A napokon pri odchode ma skasíroval pánko na recepcií kde mi do ceny naskladal parkovanie, ranajky, a vyhlad na mesto :) 
Celková cena za dvoch 375e ( asi najdrahšia noc čo sa týka strednej europy )
Jednoducho asi to zhrniem do jedného. Budapešť a zvlášt kempinsky corvinus už nikdy viac!

The scale and size of the resort is overwhelming. Has to be seen to believed as photos cant do it justice. The Main accommodation and public areas are set into the cliff and dominate the whole resort. We had a cliff ocean view mini suite with private pool. Fabulous view overlooking the resort and the ocean. The luxury escapes package included accommodation, breakfast, lunch or dinner,  and lounge access. As other reviewers have mentioned they are still in a soft opening stage so all facilities are still not completed. Some finishing work still going on (hammering, drilling etc) at time during the day. Cold water was turned off from time to time without notice.
The Good:
Our room was a good size and bathroom was excellent. Bed was very comfortable. Staff were all very friendly and helpful. Lounge access was very pleasant. A la carte food in the lounge and Pala restaurant were very good. Plenty of bottled water provided.
Not so Good
The private pool was not heated and as the weather was rather mild we didn’t use the pool much. Access to the pool was difficult as there two large steps and no hand rails for entry and exit.
Food at the lounge and Pala restaurant buffet was average.
The lounge and two chairs in the room, while stylish, were uncomfortable (very wide and low). 
TV could only be watched from the bed.
There was no iron nor ironing board.
To be fair,  most of these issues were only minor and may be fixed when they go to full opening.

Finally, take care when using the phone. I called room service for 4 tea bags. We received 4 pots of tea and were later charged $A60!

Just opened in around Feb 2019, the hotel seems not fully ready to open yet. There are some facilities & area still under construction. Located exactly between Hilton (ex Nikko hotel) & Ritz Carlton hotel, opened many years before in South Nusa Dua area. 

From grand lobby area to our room 6132 is a bit far away by walking & room numbering system is unique. 6132 is located building #6 on 1st floor. If people missed, we would go to the 6th floor instead. Golf car is available standby to send guests around the complex. 

Some details work such electrical plug next to the sofa in our room is not in perfect condition. The room is spacious with 55" TV. Twin beds 140x200cm still could accommodate 2A+2C, but make sure it's ok & inform when check in. Wardrobe closet would be nicer mpre convenient if located inside or next to the bathroom instead of outside the bathroom, next to the entry door. Room lightings needs more brighter though installed in every corner. 

Room amenities & faculties are 5 stars standard.

Sauna, hot/cold whirpool & gym room is on 15th floor, while the Lobby is on 16th floor. A bit far away walking from our room & need to go to the lobby first then go downstairs to the gym below. Not convenient at all. Facilities & amenities inside gym are nice & still brand new equipments.

Pala Breakfast area is a nice hotspot for photo among other photo vista points. Beach side are nice for sunrise or sunset. But there is no pathways along the beach walk for joggers. 

Prices are usually set above USD 200/night depending of the class & location of the room. 

Its a 5* hotel, but some facilities area need more attention & detail works look out.

Had the fortune of being able to go to Bali on holiday and experience what this hotel is like. The only information that I was aware of was that this is a new hotel.

As far as first impressions went, Apurva Kempinski couldn’t have created a better one on me. If the large pool and traditional jugs won’t impress you, the hotel’s lobby will. It is a palatial structure with a high ceiling roof with the soundtrack of gongs and distant chimes to create the illusion that one is back in an ancient kingdom. At the end of the lobby there is a long staircase leading down to the bottom of the hotel.

The hotel itself is of a large size. It is possible to clock 1 KM walking from where the cars drop you off to your room. The lesson here if that if you’re leaving your room to do something, remember not to forget anything behind.

We got a deluxe room. This meant a room with a small sitting room at the side with a sofa that can be converted on a sofa bed. The room design once again emphasized Indonesian culture with the structure of a candi and a gong situated above the sofa. This gong is by the way a real gong if you would take the time to knock on it.

The facilities are numerous though incomplete. There are numerous swimming pools where one can lose time and end up spending the entire time there and there is also a sauna and jacuzzi that offer spectacular views of the ocean; the sauna and jacuzzi being part of the fitness center located immediately below the lobby. There is a Kids’ Club though I doubt that this will be the permanent kids’ club given that it is situated in a room where the furnishings has been disassembled.

The breakfast could not be faulted. There are so many options of food to choose from. Those inclined towards Indonesian, Indian, and Chinese food will be spoiled for choice during breakfast. That said the room service menu does not appear to be fully functional. It has a limited amount of choice and the kids’ portion of the room service menu seems to be a copy and paste of most of the room service menu. The Japanese restaurant is still closed though staff members can be seen holding meetings and preparing something in the kitchen.

Regarding the service, I can say that the staff is so friendly and polite, always greeting you as you walk past. That said coordination and communication in providing service needs to be improved at the hotel. I had to ask the front desk 4 times before housekeeping sent someone to organize the sofa into a sofa bed for me. I also was frustrated when the receptionist said that the sauna would be closed at 11.30 PM, the notice on the sauna said 11 PM, and the person on the duty said 10 PM. These are little things, but things which I think a hotel of this magnitude could do better.

The consensus of the reviews that I’ve read on TripAdvisor was that this hotel could not be considered to be fully opened yet. Based on what I’ve observed above with the kids’ club, the room service and the general service, this seems to be the case. 

That said, I think this hotel will become one of the best once it has gotten the glitches out of its system and I’m looking forward to visiting again.

Wir waren mit der kompletten Firma ebendort. Aus Wien habe ich bereits die Zimmer bestellt und bezahlt. 

Das Hotel haben wir gegen 14:30 Uhr betreten und wollten auf einen Schlag alle unsere Zimmer haben. Hier war etwas verwunderlich, da zuerst die Rezeptionisten mitgeteilt haben, dass unsere Zimmer erst um 15:00 Uhr frei wären, im selben Atemzug aber gefragt haben, ob wir die Zimmer mit einem Doppelbett oder zwei getrennten Betten wünschen. 

Das ist für mich eher ein Zeichen, dass es genug Auswahl gab, auch hat das Kempinski durchaus eine stattliche Größe, sodass es kaum vorstellbar ist, dass um 14:45 Uhr nicht sieben Zimmer frei sind. Zur Ehrenrettung der Rezeption ist festzuhalten, dass mein freundlicher Hinweis, es ist 14:50 Uhr, ich denke es werden alle Zimmer machbar sein, dann verstanden wurde. Der Rezeptionist hat uns zu verstehen gegeben, dass er uns Zimmer nebeneinander geben wollte, so waren wir auf Stockwerke verteilt, was aber völlig unschädlich war. 

Zum Hotel selbst: Wirklich ein top Hotel, die Zimmer geräumig und freundlich, auch sehr modern eingerichtet. 

Wir hatten die kleinste Zimmerkategorie bestellt, dennoch ist mitzuteilen, dass die Räume ausreichend groß sind, um sämtliche Koffer und dergleichen auszubreiten und in den Kästen unterzubringen. 
Auch ist im Zimmer noch ein kleiner Platz für einen Schreibtisch und eine Sitzgarnitur, wirklich eine ausreichende Größe.

Lassen Sie sich nicht von der Fassade abschrecken. Die Fassade entspricht eher einem Mix aus einem postmodernen Ostblockstil um die 90er Jahre, innen sieht das Hotel so aus, wie ich ein Fünf-Sterne-Hotel erwarte. 

Das Frühstück ist ebenfalls sehr großzügig und gut aufbereitet, wenn Sie Wurst entnehmen, steht dahinter Personal, das frische Wurst aufschneidet. Ein kleiner Wehrmutstropfen: zu den 32€ pro Person (ist nicht unbedingt der günstigste Preis) kommt noch dazu, wenn Sie besondere Eier wünschen, dass Sie diese auch extra zu bezahlen haben. Hier sollte das Haus ein wenig die Vorgangsweise überlegen. 

Die Bar: in meiner Welt ein wenig laut, es ist aber auch festzuhalten, dass die Bar des Hotel Kempinski scheinbar prädestiniert ist, dass viele Ungarn scheinbar am Abend dort ein Getränk einnehmen. 

Laut ist sie deswegen, da dort recht laute Musik gespielt wird. Dies soll jedoch zusammenfassend kein Kritikpunkt sein, wir haben es ja gehört und sind dennoch hineingegangen. Jedenfalls gilt auch für die Bar mitzuteilen, dass sie ebendort perfekte Arbeit leisten. Als wir am ersten Abend einerseits auf die Bestellung, andererseits auf die Rechnung gewartet haben, kam von einer sehr freundlichen Barmitarbeiterin die Mitteilung, dass die Getränke aufs Haus sind und dass wir eingeladen sind. Mehr kann sie nicht machen, um die doch recht lange Wartezeit, die wir hatten, um ein Glas Sekt und ein Bier zu bekommen, wiedergutzumachen. Die Wartezeit hat sich ergeben, weil die Bar auf wienerisch gesagt grammelvoll ist. 

Zusammengefasst: Ein wirkliches Tophotel, perfekte Zimmer, freundliche Bedienung, ebenso eine perfekte Lage. 

Gezeichnet, 
Peter Nemeth

This hotel is on its way to be a world renowned luxury destination, joining the likes of Emirates Palace Hotel, Burj Al Arab, Mardan Palace, The Atlantis Bahamas, The Palms Vegas & many others that offer unprecendant luxury and amenities all in one place.
