The moment you enter the hotel you will be in awe at the sheer scale of the construction and the architecture that captures the balinese hertiage and culture, truly there is nothing like it in Bali. Keep in mind this hotel is still under construction so alot of amenities are not ready yet. To keep things nice and simple for the readers i will break down the rest into a pro/cons list as follows:

Pros:
Incredible architecture and resort design capturing Bali's heritage and culture
Massive scale of the resort
Multiple swimming pools and water bodies
Spacious rooms, beautiful interior design
House Keeping did a good job cleaning daily
Great food selection and quality at breakfast/Dinner (Pala & Cliff Lounge Restrauants)
Massive potential for the future once all the resturants, clubs, bars open up.
Cool quirks such as automated toilet seats
Staff for the most part were friendly and professional (other than certain parts which i mention next)
Balinese Massage at the spa was one of the best i've ever had and i've experienced alot of massages around the world.

Cons:
Various issues with checkin process at cliff lounge check-in staff/process. Checkin was slow took almost an hour 

Poor communication by Cliff checkin staff no follow ups about various issues, having to chase up every request. Level of service and hospitality not up to Kempenski and/or 5 star standards

Our request for a higher floor room kept getting ignored / not taken seriously despite having stayed for 6 days plenty of time to shuffle rooms around. While they continued filling newer guests in higher floor rooms, We were provided with a lower floor room with "partial ocean view" since we had a building partially obstructing the view, the fact we arrived on a special occasion and staying 6 days was not taken into consideration or priortized by the Cliff check in staff, we were promised a level 12 room and then the next day it wasn't suddenly not available (changing stories). I gave up at one point as to focus on enjoying the rest of our stay and decided to move on.(serious reforms and training required at Cliff checkin staff, quality and processes)

Poor water quality at the main swimming pool with various types of debris on the floor and dirt floating (can be noticed more at night time), other pools also had issues. 

Room i was provided was unfinished and not up to Kempenski / 5 star standards, there was white paint all over the plunge pool tiles, among other things. 

Was not provided or told about the daily activities going on at the hotel when i checked in, this was a huge dissapointment for us as we missed out on them, i only found out on my last day there by accident while i was talking to the lobby floor staff. (i suggest staff to provide printed out material of all daily activities and various specials to all guests upon checkin)

NOTE: There are many other issues that i haven't listed, and they do not do justice and don't keep up with Kempenski and/or 5-7 star Standards, HOWEVER the General Manager of this hotel who i met on the last day of my stay has personally apologized to us for the experience we received and has promised to take steps and serious action to address them in the future, he also invited us again to come back when the hotel is near completion, which is why i also rated this review higher than what i would have originally.

I hope to revist this resort sometime soon once it's in a better shape and the service is improved and reformed, and look forward to re-writing a glowing review  :)

Stayed for 2 nights in 2 suites with a business partner and his kid from overseas.

Knowing this hotel is very new, i called in to ask n make sure before making reservation that everything is up and running before booking with an agent.

First night, the adults want to hang out at 10pm and find nowhere to lounge so they went back to the room.

In the morning, looking for the gym. Husband was told its on the 15th floor. He went there and it was on construction (picture attached). Went to the lobby again to find another person n informed that the gym was not ready. So No gym.

Feeling bad because were with a guest and this hotel isnt ready, my husband decided to move to Ritz next door. 

I called to speak with the manager and found Mr Pringga, explained that i felt bad to pick a hotel that is half ready for my guest, and ask if my 4 commencing night somehow can be replaced for next time when Kempinski are ready. He said cant be done. 

He then said that they do have the gym and if my husband goes to the lobby, he can show him where the gym is. 

Mind you, It is inappropriate to have a disappointed guest come n visit the manager at the lobby. I asked mr Pringga to find and explain to my husband instead what their ready facilities are. Which he agreed to do so.

The gym was there. And after speaking to Mr Pringga, my husband said its okay to stay and not moving to Ritz.

I called Mr Pringga again and said “pls send them something as an appreciation and apology gesture” n he said “we will give them fruit platter” i was speechless, fruit platter is a standard in almost every hotel i go to for member or for suites. 

As far as i know, every hotel has fruit platter, spa massage, dinner or lunch, upgrades etc as a gesture of apology. In this case, giving fruit platter for appreciation gesture is a joke. We know how 5 star standards are, and Kempinski is being very cheap. Have you given a wine, or a spa voucher, it might end up not being used, but its a more appropriate gesture of appreciation for the guests.

I flew in later in the day and saw the handwritten letter of apology for the inconvenience. Kids went swimming at 5pm the second day. They liked the pool with the slides. I wanted to order snacks for the kids and told that we cannot order because its 5pm! And i asked again are you sure? I havent been having any difficulties ordering food at any time while the sun is up in other hotels.

The attendant come back and said: the chef said you can only order pizza. I told him pizza then and fries are also very easy. But he told me the chef said only pizza. At this time my guest came and i didnt want him to order n find that Kempinski cannot accept his order. So pizza it is. No fries n no juices. And i asked for a young coconut which they can provide. 

So the Cons comes down to these:
- Some staffs and manager are not well trained and not well informed 
- pool need to be able to order snacks and juices just like other normal hotels do
- complaints need to be handled appropriately, as you are in a hospitality business and Kempinski holds a big name.
- pool is dirty, possibly might be under treatment as they are not 100% ready.

Pros:
- huge and impressive lobby
- rooms are good 
- very thorough downturn service, thanks to the turndown service team!
- breakfast quality in the lounge is good
- slide pool is fun for kids
- most staffs are trying to be nice even though theyre lacking of information.

I might come again when the Apurva is ready and become Kempinski.

After having a wonderful experience with the Kempinski in a different city, I was really looking forward to our stay here in Budapest. However we had multiple disappointing experiences during our 3 day stay. Our air conditioner in the room was broken, and when we notified hotel staff they said they would send someone to investigate. When the repair main came he held his hand up said, “air conditioner is working max that’s all it can do”, and left. It was approx 80 degrees outside and not much cooler in the room, with the air conditioner definitely not working. This led to two nights of restless sleep from being too hot to even use the sheets. Every other room in the hotel including common areas and the hallway outside our room was much cooler. We checked with the front desk multiple times with no resolution or follow up on the problem unless we called again. Upon check out the staff did admit that the AC was broken but that they were fully booked so couldn’t have moved us to another room and also said they couldn’t give a discount on the expensive rooms despite the lack of AC through the duration of our stay. 

We also had difficulty with the concierge asking them to order taxi’s but not having any ordered and then having to ask multiple times before they finally called for one which left us late to dinner reservations. 

The breakfast buffet was very good, and the staff did give us complimentary breakfast for two days as the coffee machine in our room was broken. 

Overall the lack of AC, poor communication, and less than ideal service left us not very satisfied especially for such an expensive and well known hotel brand! I will stay with Kempinski again, just not at this location. The Ritz next door seemed to have better service and probably AC.

This is a beautiful hotel in a prime location with great rooms and facilities, several wonderful restaurants, amazing breakfast and several lounges and areas to relax. Unfortunately though, customer service is inconsistent and does not live up to the 5 star status of this hotel. We had booked two connecting rooms directly through the hotel, which upon check in, the front desk person was not aware of until I mentioned it and after she went back to the booking to verify the information. At that point, she indicated that one of the rooms was not ready, even though we arrived at 5:30 PM. She indicated that they could give us one of the rooms and that house keeping was already in the second room cleaning it up and it would only be 20 minutes. Well, house keeping was not in the room and the 20 minutes turned into 2 hours. We had been traveling all day and exhausted. There were four of us and only a double bed in one of the rooms, so this was very inconvenient, After 2 hours, I asked for the manager and she was apologetic about the situation and offered a breakfast coupon. She did also call a couple of days later  to check if everything was OK which I appreciated. The friendliness of the staff was variable. Housekeeping and the breakfast staff were very warm and friendly, the concierge and deli staff not so much. After booking an airport transfer with the concierge for our departure, I decided to stop by the desk the night before to confirm the reservation. He ruffled through different pieces of paper laying on his desk and then said that he couldnt find a reservation. I wasn’t surprised given the system of making reservations on loose pieces of small paper. Never apologizing, he indicated that the person we made the reservation with must have forgotten to write it down and that it happens. Seriously? By the way, the concierge indicated that the transfer with the taxi would be 30 euros, but upon arrival to the airport, the taxi driver wanted more. I didn’t budge from the 30 euros and told him that’s what the hotel said. He took the money and walked away yelling something in Hungarian. Anyway, upon check out, we were charged for 4 breakfasts even through we had only had three breakfasts and used a coupon for one. One of the charges for breakfast was on our day of arrival- we arrived at 5:30 PM! I was glad I double checked the bill. The front desk person did make the changes, but again it’s a sign of sloppy service. The hotel is quite large and very busy. There were several tour groups staying there and one day we had to line up for the elevator. So I guess, things get lost in the shuffle. Also, no small touches at this hotel. We stayed at the Aria Hotel in Prague and Hotel Bristol in Vienna and their service was on another level. Chocolates on the pillow, little touches here and there, staff who were attentive to your every need- no such thing at the Kempinski. But again, I have to say that the hotel is modern and very well kept and the location couldn’t be more central. We walked everywhere- no sight was more than 15-20 minutes walking. So I would recommend it for the location and facilities but don't expect 5 star service.

We booked the Kempinski through Expedia. We have always had excellent service with Kempinski in Europe.  This hotel was a bit of a mixed bag. 

Upon arrival, we learned that while we had been told we could have 2 rooms with a view, only one was available.  We were also told we could upgrade our rooms to fabulous newly-renovated rooms for more money. We opted not to do so. Upon arrival on our floor, it became evident that it was in the midst of renovations.   Several burly men were working to renovate the rooms. Our daughter (who was staying alone) asked to change her room as she felt uncomfortable being on a hallway with all the workmen.  The decor in our room was dated but this did not concern us. 

The hotel is across from the Erzsbet Park so if you are looking for river or Castle views, this hotel doesn't have them.  It's in a great location which allows you to walk to several everywhere or take the Metro.  We had a myriad of great dining options very close by and we enjoyed the ES bistro at the hotel.  There is an ES deli where you can get coffee and breakfast items. 

We did take advantage of the spa (excellent treatments and staff!) and the fitness center was also excellent.  

While the rooms we had were dated, they were clean and the staff did a turn-down service providing water each evening.  The front desk staff and concierges were extremely helpful and knowledgeable.  

The real issue was the renovations.  During the day, we were out and did not hear noise.  However, upon returning in the late afternoon/early evening the workers were still there and on several occasions we lost power in our room.  One night at 10 p.m. our room went dark and I opened the door (to see if power had gone out in the hallway) to find one of the workers who apologized and said it would be back on shortly.
We were also surprised that a) we were assigned to a floor undergoing renovations (we felt sure we were going to come back and find our room had been dismantled!) and if they couldn't close the floor or a section of it b) why they would renovate in the summer during their peak tourist/occupancy season.

We mentioned this at checkout and management was kind enough to make an accommodation for the inconvenience.  

Without living in the middle of renovations, I would have given this a 4.  I really wish I could do 3.5 . I think the hotel will be lovely once the room renovations are complete.  We would have preferred not to live through them.

The Kempinski is well located and has appealing public spaces. I have stayed several times over more than 10 years and watched it evolve. The renovation of the lobby was a net positive. That said, the rooms very tired and the renovated rooms only had a superficial makeover, In a hotel that is 25 years old the rooms need a full makeover. That is obvious if the hotel claims to be a 5 star property. I summarize as follows:

Positives
1. Average room size good.
2. Free internet
3. Large bathrooms
4. Helpful concierges
5. Good bell staff
6. Nice coffee lounge
7. Location
8. Nobu in lobby
9. Decent but sometimes overcrowded hotel bar

Negatives
1. Awful front desk staff. Constant issues with bills and general duties.
2. Indifferent, non responsive and contemptuous view of honest complaints. They do not care at all. I think if you were shot dead in the lobby they would be mad at you for being shot.
3. Es bistro service is extreme variable from good to plain awful.
4. The rooms are tired, renovations are very superficial
5. Only business news channels in English and that awful CNN.
6. Rooms are loud and noisy with street noise.
7. In summer rooms can be hot as Air Conditioning is not cold.
8. Crowded with tours and cruise guests
9. Lack of outlets
10. Small gym, lack of cardio machines

Overall I find the hotel a decent choice but wish greatly they would live up to the potential of the physical spaces, rooms and location. The rates do not always reflect a fair price to value ratio in my view. Hopefully, they will fix the noise in rooms which is likely caused by old window fixtures and actually renovate the rooms completely. It would be nice if they offered some more English channels like Discovery, Nat Geo, not just news channels and perhaps a direct US news channel like Fox News as the British/Business ones are dull while CNNI is plain insufferable.

I would rate this hotel as good - as opposed to what is reflected in the overall rating of average since TA does not have this category.
Strengths
The facade, lobby and the interior are excellent and worthy of the Kempinski label. The spa and business center's are also very good with staff working in these places, eager to please and generally accommodating.
Areas for improvement (quirks)
Marketed as a 5 star property under the luxury category, is in my opinion an espoused value based on my personal observations during my 4 day 3 night stay. The quality of my stay did not quite match the premium rates charged for this hotel.
My Room
The Jr Suite I occupied is not a traditional suite generally used by other 5 star hotels.  It is literally 2 interconnecting rooms converted into one.  The layout of both rooms were similar in nature with the exception of the bed, the sofa and the living room having an Nespresso type coffee machine. Hence, I had 2 bathrooms, 2 TV sets, two closets, 2 work desk  2 safe's and yes, the rooms required 2 sets of keys inserted on either side of the rooms to activate power. Although I did not personally view the other suites, I suspect the rooms sharing the same corridor as mine - 900 to 918 all share a similar fate. If by definition of 2 separate rooms with one containing the bed while the other, a sofa, then the suite I resided in fits the bill. Guest used to the regular definition of a suite may wish to clarify with front desk/reservation before placing a reservation less left battling negative emotions on check in for wrongful/inaccurate representation.
Other quirks within the suite I found issues with are the size of the TV, the position of the safe's, the lack of power sockets which accepts international devices and the missing bed stands which did not allow me a place to put my glasses. (see attached picture).
The size of the LCD Philips TV was only slightly larger than my screen on my flight which was kind of disappointing. Sadly, I cannot review any of the channels on offer as I did not bother using it.
The safe is placed in an awkward position requiring one to get down on their knees to safe keep valuables. Not great for those with joint issues. Unsure if those involved in the planning and design of the sites take these issues into consideration.
Upgrades are definitely needed on the power sockets that are friendly to International travelers. Sure adapters help but in this day and age where one has multiple devises that requires charging, this suite has not delivered in this area. I looked through the posting of other travellers and am delighted to learn that other rooms do come equipped with power outlets friendly to International devices. Sadly, I am unsure which category of rooms these are found in. I can vouch they are NOT found in Jr Suites.
To the duty/PR manager reading this review, renovations are required. Rather than have 2 separate equal size bathrooms with identical features, why not make the bathroom in the master bedroom wider and more spacious with facilities further apart with the decreased remaining space in the living room just containing a standard toilet and wash sink. Not only is this practice more environmentally friendly, it will also cut down on unnecessary work on your housekeeping staff. Could you also ensure there are 2 bedside tables flanking both sides? This current arrangement is akin to my luggage only having wheels on me side and is not a good reflection of this brand name.
This hotel, as with the major tourist spots within Budapest, is struggling to cope with the influx of tourist. I have seen at least 1 tour group that makes use of this property as their choice of accommodation. The check-in process and the public areas guest of this tour groups mingle in can be better managed for the comfort of your other residents. Being delayed in the lobby due to the lack of room for maneuver is not an image you want to leave guest with  I am curious to learn how your team plan to problem-solve this issue and your commitment to looking after the welfare and comfort of ALL paying guest.
Brekky
The spread in ES Bistro is extensive with multiple stations offering something different. Well-done in this regard. I love the fresh juice section which allowed me to mix orange and carrot/tomato etc. Your effort to cater to diabetics with sugar free jams on offer is also worthy of mention. Why not complete an already great spread by throwing in an omelette station rather than a ala carte paid option. Another quirk perhaps.
My observation of waiters extremely keen to clear cutlery and crockery of guest is consistent with the postings made by others on TA. Perhaps, it may be due to the fact of being overworked and clearing after tourist groups on a schedule that has resulted in service being functional. One notable exception is Adrianne, who made an effort to recognise returning diners, engage guest with small talk and exuded warmth in that process. Such a joy to be ushered into the brekky table by her.
Please do compare this review with others posted on this property and make up your own mind if this hotel is worthy of the marketed tag of a 5 star establishment.

Having been to Kempinski Cathedral Square in Vilnius several times, and the late lamented Hybernska in Prague, we have been very impressed with our Kempinski experiences, and so, my partner and I eagerly anticipated our stay in Budapest at the Corvinus.

Alas, the discovery that in a FIVE STAR HOTEL, there was no tea and coffee making facilities in our Superiorroom immediately put a damper on our stay. I wasn't expecting a Nespresso machine, as this was only available in Grand Deluxe and above rooms, but to not even have a kettle?? Having to phone guest services 3 times to resolve issues that really shouldn't have happened was also annoying, and made us feel like complainers, rather than guests in a top hotel.

We had requested a park view, but our room overlooked a few shrubs and trees atop the central atrium, rather than a view to the front of the hotel, where there was a park!

The hotel website stated that ALL bathrooms have underfloor heating. Ours didn't, and when we asked a member of staff, he said that our room didn't have it.

Laptop size safe just wasn't, unless you have a teeny laptop/i-pad.

The prohibitively expensive €32 breakfast charge made us opt for room only. Why so expensive for breakfast? Room service breakfast menu was very confusing, we gave up and decided to go round the corner to a burger bar for breakfast and COFFEE!!! 

On the bright side, we had drinks in the Blue Fox bar after our evening out, and the lady who served us could not have been more attentive and friendly.

Club sandwich ordered from room service late at night came promptly, was hot, and came with a good selection of condiments.

As ever, the staff were very welcoming and endeavoured to put right anything that was wrong. It's just a pity that there were any problems at all.

לא בטוחה שהייתי חוזרת כי הם די עצבנו אותי. 

היינו במלון במהלך חודש אוקטובר, זוג ל-5 לילות.

חדרים יפים, נקיים, מרווחים, נעימים ושקטים. 
הסבונים והקרמים שהם מספקים בריח מהמם!!
מספקים מגבות, חלוקים ונעלי בית.

ובצד הפחות חיובי:.
על אף שהיו אנשי צוות נחמדים, ציפיתי בגדול לשירות הרבה יותר טוב בדברים כה בסיסיים, אז עוד יותר ממלון של 5 כוכבים.

השקעים של החשמל בחדר מאוד מוגבלים וממוקמים בצורה לא נוחה שזה אפילו די מוזר.
אין שום ערכה להכנת קפה בחדר, משהו שקיים אפילו במלונות של 3 ו4 כוכבים, ביקשנו מהם מראש שישימו לנו ואכן אמרו שישימו, אך ביום שהגענו אמרו לנו שפשוט נגמר להם והם מתנצלים, ועל אף שהבטיחו מראש לא הציעו ״פיצוי״ או תחליף, או שידאגו לכך לימים הבאים.
אח"כ גילינו ששמו אותנו בחדר עם ״נוף״ לקיר של בניין תחת שיפוץ, למחרת ביקשנו לעבור חדר ובאמת העבירו אותנו. 
השיא מבחינתי היה שבערב שבו חזרנו לחדר ראינו שהמנקה לא שמה נייר טואלט בשירותים, התקשרנו לשירות חדרים ואף אחד לא ענה דקות ארוכות, חייגנו שוב אז ענו, אבל אף אחד לא בא. צלצלנו שוב ועדיין לא הגיע אף אחד, אז כעבור שעה... אני מוצאת את עצמי יורדת לקבלה ומבקשת נייר טואלט. שם אף אחד לא  מתנצל על כל התרחיש ההזוי הזה, ועל העיכוב המטורף בזמן שבכלל התכוונו לצאת לבלות. יותר מאוחר התלוננו לאחראי בקבלה, והפיצוי שהציע- משקה לכל אחד בבר של המלון. שלמען האמת זהו פיצוי די מזערי לרמת הפאדיחה שהם עשו. 
דבר מרגיז נוסף, ביום האחרון ביקשנו שידאגו לנו למונית לשדה התעופה עם נהג שמקבל תשלום באירו, אז אמרו לנו שאין בעיה. אנחנו מגיעים איתו לשדה - מסתבר שהוא לא מקבל אירו!

לגבי המתקנים הנוספים במלון:
ניסיתי את הספא שלהם, הצוות של הספא היו מאוד מאוד חביבות, הספא עצמו לא ממש גדול, הבריכה היתה קרה לצערי, והמסג׳ - ציפיתי למשהו יותר טוב.

מסעדת נובו המפורסמת שקיימת במלון- ממש רציתי להתלהב ממנה הרבה יותר ממה שקרה בפועל.

ארוחות בוקר שם לא אכלנו כי זה בתוספת תשלום והעדפנו בקרבת המלון.

הבר של המלון למען היה ממש אחלה, המלצריות שם מאוד שירותיות ונחמדות, קוקטיילים טעימים ובאמת שאני ממליצה עליו.

בשורה בתחתונה - ממלון 5 כוכבים הייתי מצפה לשירות ברמה הרבה יותר גבוהה ומזה התאכזבתי.
לגבי הנוחות והמיקום אין ספק שכן עונים על הציפיוות.

I stayed with my husband, he was here for work and we took an extended trip. This is a very nice and posh feeling hotel. Everyone speaks English very well and I believe several other languages. It's located in a high end shopping district, pretty touristy with many restaurants, cafes, and shops nearby. It's also very centrally located (on the Pest side) and just outside a metro stop. It's very convenient to travel near or far within the city. The AC is good but we did get a little hot in the evening even with it on high. The room is large for a European hotel room, the closet is huge actually. The bathroom is also spacious. The water is hot, and the sink has a lot of counter space for toiletries etc. Everyone is very friendly and helpful. We had an issue with returning our rental car when we arrived and the valet doorman went above and beyond allowing us to leave the car in front for an extended period of time. My only complaint is there was some hair (not mine) and mildew in the shower on the wall. It looked like they had cleaned the floor of the shower and bathroom but not the walls.

From start to finish, our experience of this hotel was nothing short of fantastic. The size and cleanliness of the rooms is brilliant, including an evening turn down service where a complimentary bottle of water is supplied each evening. The ambiance of the hotel is lovely, staff always great you on arrival and meeting you in the hotel.
The location is fantastic, take a right out of the hotel and you're en route to St. Stephens, the river, and the tourist spots. To the left (less than a 5 minute walk) you are in the heart of an array of restaurants and bars, as well as many clothing shops. It wakes up at night and is the place to go no matter what your taste.
The beds were comfy, although it would have been nice to have a double rather than two singles together. The duvets and pillows were thick and comfortable. The air conditioning worked perfectly, and the room was a comfortable temperature throughout our stay. Very little noise can be heard from adjacent rooms.
Wifi is good, the connection rarely broke. There's a good supply of essentials including a vanity kit, hair dryer, slippers and dressing gowns for extra comfort. 

The cons are few but worth mentioning. There were no tea and coffee facilities, which is a staple for me and my partner when we go away, so that was slightly disappointing. There also weren't many power points, especially by the bed which was a little inconvenient.

Overall, the staff were thorough, the hotel was a fantastic location (although don't expect any views), and we wouldn't hesitate to stay again. This hotel really made our trip!

My boyfriend booked us a weekend at the Kempinski Corvinus for my birthday as a surprise. It was a hit :) The hotel is extremely well located right on the Erzsébet tér square, so you have many options to get here from the airport. We chose the minibus which dropped us at the door for a fixed price (taxi will cost you more than 3x as much btw). 
Check in was smooth, we were offered an upgrade to a kingsize room for €50 which we declined. Although we were a few hours early our room (queensize bed) was already available which was nice. We were traveling super light (just a small backpack each) so happy to have plenty of toiletries in the bathroom. The room itself was fairly large and everything was nice and clean. Shower was great too.
Breakfast wasn't included in our booking, and to be honest it wasn't entirely clear to us what we could order and how much that would cost us (we wanted to try the buffet if that was possible). But plenty of alternatives, you've got the Es Deli shop in the hotel and there was an Easter market outside where we had expensive hotdogs. We did go for dinner one evening in the Es restaurant (but that's a separate review). I was excited to see Nobu located inside the hotel and originally wanted to have my birthday dinner there, but after a day outside in the cold I didn't feel like sushi after all. Nobu does roomservice too which sounded awesome. We also had tea in the "living room" next to the fireplace one evening and that was really nice and cosy. They close at 10 pm but you're allowed to stay until you finished your drinks. 
We returned to our room at night to find our beds "turned down" with complementary bottles of water on the nightstands - that was a nice touch. Didn't hear any sound at all from hallway nor neighbouring rooms. Our room overlooked a rather empty side street of the square, but due to the central location you will occasionally get random drunk people shouting outside at night. Better isolation of the windows is about the only thing I would have wanted to change in the hotel. And yeah, the queensize bed was a bit narrow for us in the end (have a kingsize at home so we tend to sprawl out) but that's our own choice I suppose.
Check out went smooth as well, and concierge was happy to store our little backpacks and helped us book the minibus again. I'd definitely would want to stay here again if I return to Budapest - hopefully including a Nobu dinner!

This Kempinski offers a lot under one roof. The first idea, there is NO KETTLE in Deluxe Room (higher than superior room). Very inconvenient for me during winter time, sorry. The room was great in size and quality. The furniture looked a bit outdated, but still nice. The bathroom was large and offered separate bath and shower (shower with fixed shower head, duh!). Bed comfortable as one would expect from Kempinski. Elevator to garage was broken, duh! Luckily the service made it up for this inconvenience. The Lady in Red looked gorgeous in her Hungarian style costume, well done Budapest! Check in and check out very comfortable, attentive and friendly staff. Lobby bar very enjoyable. NOBU restaurant in house offers room delivery, great! Breakfast was at ES Bistro, well in my opinion could be a lot better. At 10h30 sharp they started packing all breakfast without prior information on last opportunity to eat more, as customary, all this for 32EUR. Blue Fox bar offers some world class drinking experience and the service is very nice. Small but very convenient spa. An outlet close to bed would be nice for charging phones. Overall my experience was good, please think about installing kettle in the rooms, its really 21st century. Congratulations on the staff, they are a true asset to this hotel.

I think that this hotel is 3 stars at best.  We had a king-sized bed room confirmed.  The first 3 rooms each had two twins, though they assured us that each of these rooms had a king sized bed. We were finally offered a room with a king sized bed and told we would have pay extra.  It was the same room that every other guest was offered in the group.  We did resolve this issue.  

 It was 96 degrees and the A/C did not work. We had the engineer in our room three times.  He told us that the circulator did not work, but he had resolved the issue.  We were given a fan and that was the best we could do
.  
I would not recommend this hotel.  The service was mediocre at best and my feeling was that they do not care for American guests.  We were advised by a waitress at the bar that only Americans on Tauck Tours stay there.  There is a lot of smoking in and around the hotel.  

The hotel is well located. The breakfast which is included with the room is good. I would not stay here again.

We were SO thrilled to have picked this hotel, as it was the perfect location!!!  Other hotels that we had considered were Corinthia Hotel & Boscolo, Autograph Collection --- so glad we didn't stay at either of these, as they're too far out and location of these were much more sketchy when walking in their neighborhood.  We also looked at the Four Seasons, which was close to Kempinski, but not worth the extra $$$.  Kempinski Hotel Budapest is a luxury hotel with great service, great location, & excellent value for the price.

Tip --- Taxi ride from the airport to the hotel should only cost you around 7,000 HUF.

From the moment walking into the hotel, you're treated like royalty.  Service with a smile and a can do attitude.  Very upscale, extremely luxurious hotel.  The rooms are very clean, and are incredibly spacious.  No pool, but a decent spa (we didn't partake in the spa, but did walk through it).  There's a Nobu (Japanese steakhouse) in the hotel (we didn't eat there, but have dined at other Nobu restaurants and they have always been excellent), as well as another restaurant called ES Bisztro, which was our WORST dining experience in Budapest by far.  Overpriced for subpar food; there was even a long black curly hair on top of my burger bun --- eww!!  Avoid this restaurant at all costs.

Excellent location, right next to Vaci Street, with tons of great shopping & restaurants, a metro stop basically right outside the hotel, walking distance to the Danube (5 minutes), right next to Erzebet Park (nice park w/ Ferris Wheel), basically right across the street from St.Stephen's Basilica (tons of restaurants & shopping in that area as well).  We couldn't have picked a better location for our stay in Budapest!  It was in the center of everything that we wanted to do.  Parliament only about a 10-15 walk away, same w/ Dohany Street Synagogue w/ all the ruin bars / nightlife in that district.  Also, a 5 minute walk to the chain bridge, where you can cross the river and then take the funicular (adorable tram that goes up to the top of the hill, similar to the one from the movie Grand Budapest Hotel) up the hill to Buda Castle / Castle Hill / Fisherman's Bastion / Matthias Church.  The only things we had to take the metro / taxis for were the bath houses (Szechenyi & Gellert) & The New York Cafe.

TIP --- This hotel is in the heart of an expensive tourist shopping area.  For cheaper souvenirs, walk down to the end of Vaci Street (15 minute walk), where there's a market (Central Market Hall) with local fruit, meats, & pastries on the 1st floor and then all sorts of cheaper souvenirs on the second floor.  You won't find classier souvenirs there, such as Herend Porcelain or Varga Crystal, but great for picking up little things like paprika, shirts, purses, magnets, dresses, dolls, postcards, etc.  It was kinda neat to see the Central Market Hall, with all the colors and different kinds of food.

Besides ES Bisztro, the only other negative we can say about the Kempinski was the fact that you needed to swipe your room key in the elevator each time for the elevator to function properly.  It was annoying, as sometimes it would take a while to register and once swiped, you only had a few seconds to push your room floor or else you'd have to try to swipe your card again.  I can see the security benefit, as it kept anyone that didn't belong from going up onto the hotel floors, but it was annoying none-the-less.

The beds were very comfortable, the rooms were quite spacious, the bathrooms were magnificent - all done up in marble.  Housekeeping in the AM to clean up the room and then turn down service as well.  Excellent shower pressure, nice smelling shampoo / conditioner, lotion.  We enjoyed the rooms very much.

Excellent staff, superb service, clean & spacious rooms, fantastic location.  Highly recommend this hotel!!

TIP --- Ask for a High-Floor w/ Park view room.  Excellent view of the Ferris Wheel, as well as St. Stephen's Basilica.

Stayed for a long weekend for my wife's 60th birthday. We were blown away with it all. From the complimentary chocolate birthday cake in our room on arrival, with handwritten welcome note ( nice touch ), excellent room service inc complimentary water, as well as mini-bar. Big rooms, well decorated ( we overlooked Fashion Street ). Quick check-in, very helpful staff everywhere, especially friendly bar staff. I have seen reviews saying bar music is too loud, but did not find this the case, drinks not too expensive, Es Bistro is a good place to eat, but did not try Nobu. Very central location, easy to walk almost anywhere. Impressive entrance lobby etc, etc.

You MUST stay here if you visit Budapest.

Die Lage des Hotels ist sehr gut, zentral, mit vielen Einkaufsmöglichkeiten, jedoch ohne Donaublick. Die wichtigsten Sehenswürdigkeiten sind zu Fuss innerhalb von 30 Minuten zu erreichen. Das Hotel sieht nur von Aussen schön und modern aus, im Inneren ist nur die Lobby und die zwei Restaurants elegant und zeitgemäss. In einem modernen Lift endet auch die Moderne und Eleganz dieses Hotels. Vom Lift ausgestiegen, lässt schon die muffige Flur mit hässlichem Teppich und Blick auf billige Zimmertüre erahnen dass man im Zimmer nicht viel erwarten soll.

Das Superior-Zimmer war sehr klein und sehr, sehr altmodisch eingerichtet. Das Mobiliar war vielleicht vor 30-40 Jahren modern. Der Teppich, die Vorhänge und die Bettdecken im gold-braunen Tönen waren von allen am hässlichsten. Ausserdem war das Zimmer sehr ringhörig, das Bett sehr unbequem und zu kurz. Es gab nur eine einzige freie Steckdose im Zimmer was das Aufladen der elektronischen Geräte zur Herausforderung machte. Das Badezimmer hatte keine getrennte Toilette und keine Duschecke separat. Die billigen Armaturen waren zum Teil verrostet, Abfluss verstopft. Das Zimmer bietet absolut kein Komfort!

Der Spa-Bereich war auch eine Riesenenttäuschung - nur ein Mini-Pool, kein Whirlpool, einzig der Saunabereich war schön renoviert. Trotzdem kann man hier keine Entspannung finden, es gibt nur sehr wenige Liegestühle (8), die alle permanent besetzt sind. Für eine Stadt mit ausgeprägter Badekultur ist der Spa-Bereich ein absolutes Flop.

Die einzige positive Überraschung war das japanische Restaurant Nobu. Das Restaurant wirkt sehr einladend, das Speiseangebot ist ansprechend und sowie Qualität der einzelnen Gerichte, obwohl die Krevetten mit Knoblauch und Wasabi-Sauce hätte ich mir schärfer vorgestellt. Der Service war ok - der einzige Kritikpunk geht an den Weinservice - der Wein wurde einfach im Glas an den Tisch gebracht, statt vorher die Flasche präsentieren und vor dem Gast einschenken. 

Das Frühstück im Bistro Es war ok was den Auswahl angeht. Der Service liess aber den Wünschen übrig. Pancakes waren nicht frisch und dazu noch sehr unappetitlich präsentiert, statt Schokoladensauce bekam ich ein beschmiertes Schälchen mit Nutella! Der Cappuccino war eine bodenlose Frechheit - eine undefinierbare hellbraune Brühe in Kaffeetasse - so etwas bekommt man nicht einmal am Bahnhof serviert (siehe Fotos)!

Fazit: absolut nicht zum Empfehlen, da kein 5-Sterne Standard. Diejenigen, die das Hotel als überdurchschnittlich gut, renoviert (die Renovierungsarbeiten stehen erst bevor und werden laut Rezeption erst 2017 abgeschlossen), top und weiss noch nicht was bezeichnen, die haben absolut keine Ahnung. Die sollten sich bisschen mehr umschauen, z.B. Kempinski Adlon Berlin, Palais Hansen Wien, River Park Bratislava, das heisst dann LUXUS oder 5-Sterne Standard!

- 3rd time at this hotel (3 nights) traveling on business (corp. rate so not helpful on TA)
- moved room mid-way through due to essential construction, but without any hassle at all
- Bar is perfect for the end of a long day (I doff my hat to Mark and Melinda)
- Restaurant surprised me with the quality of their steaks
- Rooms entirely functional, but spacious, clean and comfortable

Sometimes you just want a business hotel to be psychic about the balance between anonymity and welcome. This one does it for me. The front desk are polite but not OTT, and the bar/restaurant staff are attentive without being rushed or too "in your face". It ticks all the right boxes for me.

The rooms are not dissimilar to any other high end hotel, but because it's in Budapest it's eminently more affordable, hence appearing on my firm's 'approved' hotel choices. The wardrobes are large enough for most business travellers, the bathrooms are functional and most have a separate bath+shower, and shower cubicle. Not essential to me, but it gives an idea of their size.

There's no view to speak of (rooms are arranged in a semi-circle, looking inwards upon each other), but the windows are large enough to let in plenty of natural light when you're using the room's desk during the day.

I moved mid-way through as mentioned above from 3-something to 803 at the far end of the 8th floor corridor. Apparently it's a slightly smaller room but in all honesty I couldn't discern any difference in size. It was ideal.

I really like the hotel bar. Traveling solo on business isn't glamorous, so I typically look for somewhere to people-watch at the end of a day, but where if I need to make calls, batter through the day's e-mails and so on, I can do so undisturbed. The Blue Cafe ticks that box. It does get busier (and the music louder) after about 8pm, but that's a good signal that it's time either stop drinking and go back to the room...or capitulate and stop working. Invariably Mark's drinks helped make up my mind on that score. The bar team really have got their act together. They're super-efficient and all smiles. And they have a smoking terrace outside.

I rarely eat in hotel restaurants on business trips, but at the Kempinski I have no hesitation in recommending theirs. I ate a Hungarian cote de boeuf doused in herbs that infuse it nicely. It was cooked to perfection and with a garlic mash to die for, if not kill colleagues with the next day. Joking aside - a surprisingly casual and un-hotel-like restaurant.

I'd highly recommend this hotel for business travel (if not for holiday-makers wanting a view over the Danube).

Ficamos, eu e minha esposa, no Kempinski Corvinus em Budapeste em fev/2015.  O Hotel é lindo, luxuoso, com quartos muito confortáveis, bom restaurante, com localização privilegiada. 
A rua atrás do hotel é a "Fashion Street" de Budapeste e, apesar do nome, tem lojas para todos os gostos (caras e baratas). O hotel fica bem no centro, perto de tudo. Budapeste tem serviço de taxi muito bom e também um eficiente transporte público. É muito fácil chegar às principais atrações de Budapeste a partir deste hotel. Para jantar, há diversos bons restaurantes que se pode chegar a pé, além do Nobu e do Es, que ficam dentro do hotel. 
Ficamos encantados com o atendimento do staff. Check in/out muito eficientes. O Concierge nos deu indicações de atrações e restaurantes muito boas, que nos permitiu visitar monumentos e comer sem gastar muito e sem perder tempo. O quarto é luxuoso e muito confortãvel. A internet é gratuita e oferece bom serviço. Na mesa de trabalho há todas as tomadas que precisamos para o notebook, celular e câmera. 
O restaurante "Es" do hotel oferece um ótimo café da manhã e um jantar descontraído, mas com comida muito boa. A única ressalva que fazemos é em relação ao SPA, que tem serviço impecável, mas a massagem oferecida é mais parecida com um tratamento de pele (desfoliante e creme hidratante). Como tratamento de pele, pareceu ótimo, mas não se trata de massagem.
Outro aspecto a se destacar deste hotel é o preço das diárias. Para um hotel tão luxuoso e tão bem localizado,  o preço é surpreendendemente baixo. Hoteis semelhates a ele em Praga, por exemplo, podem facilmente custar o dobro. 
Definitivamente, recomendo a hospedagem no Kempinski de Budapeste.

Not quite sure where to start with this review as the Kempinski is so good!

I have just returned from a 3 night conference (I can see one of my Uk colleagues has already posted a glowing account!) which took place aminly within the hotels conference facilities and ES bistro.

We arrived on the Monday evening to find proper doormen, in top hats no less, out front to help you sort out your luggage etc with the bell boy.

There were 3 staff on reception checking people in so we only had to wait in a minute or so to get room cards etc.. in fact throught my stay I could always find a staff member within about 20 seconds.

I had room 331, no great views (I wasn't able to choose my room as this was a corporate booking) but I did look out onto a sort of roof garden which was nice.

The bedroom and bathroom were both large, I had twin beds pushed together like a lot of rooms in mainland Europe and good quality duvets/pillows. I also had a desk and work chair plus 2 easy chairs and a footstool. I know some of my colleagues had corner suites in their rooms with twin beds so seems like the hotel is set up for friends travelling together as well as single business and couples. I also had a fully stocked mini bar and a large safe which took my full sized laptop plus several other things like phones, kindles, bags etc... plenty of wardrobe space and an iron / ironing board.

The bathroom had both shower AND bath.. brilliant plus a bathrobe and slippers which I love when I stay in a hotel as for some reason, it makes it feel more "homely"

There was also shampoo, conditioner, body wash and body lotion, decent hairdryer and lightling which was good enough to do your make up in.

And.. a set of scales, I am not sure if a set of scales is a good idea bearing in mind the excellent food served in ES Bistro... you'll have to make your own mind up on that !

The conference facilities were excellent, with a number of rooms which were kept at an ambient temperature and lots of drinks and snacks provided with more staff to help you with a wide selection of coffee machines. I think the service was second to none in between meetings. 

ES Bistro served excellent food every morning and lunch time... plenty of choice from hot and cold buffets and all of it delicious. An example of how great this place is... at breakfast they had boiled eggs in a large glass container, one side they were 3 min eggs and on the other 6 min eggs, so even have your preference for runny, non runny was covered!

We had our gala dinner inside the hotel in the main meeting room with the dance floor in it. Once gain the food was excellent, I rarely eat dessert but I did on this occasion and was glad, and again the service was slick and professional.

There was a gym which some of my colleagues took advantage of and the WiFi is free for 24hrs, you just renew it each day.  

The only tiny thing which wasn't perfect was the lack of tea and coffee making facilities in the rooms which anyone who reads my reviews will see is a bit of a bugbear with me (a nice lady delivered water every night though) 

I've not looked at the rack rate for this hotel but if i come back to Budapest I will definitely try and stay here.

The hotel is located in the centre and we walked down to the river for a boat trip one night... this is great trip make sure you it... and then to the oldest restaurant in Budapest. It seems like a compact city and while we only got out briefly during our stay we managed to find bars, restaurants and a supermarket all within walking distance. I would consider coming back to Budpest for a mini break as there is plently of culture and sight seeing to be done.

One of the main reasons we chose this hotel was after reading other comments about it’s good location for sightseeing. Just a few moments walk east of the river and a stones throw from St Stephen’s Basilica it didn’t disappoint in that aspect and provided a great base for a city break, since it was easy to return to throughout the day. There is a metro station very close by but a taxi from the airport took about 25 minutes in weekday rush hour and cost 10,000HUF including a tip.

Door staff were on hand to take care of the luggage straight away and the check in process was speedy. There was no wait at reception and he was friendly and very welcoming. The lobby area can be seen in many photos of the hotel, but there were plenty of staff available there throughout our stay to help with questions and the light airy area with gorgeous smelling lilies is a fantastic introduction to the hotel.

There are four modern lifts in the hotel which were fast and never required a wait to catch. The wide corridors were clean and displayed some interesting artwork & sculptures as well as a few seating areas should you wish.

Our Superior Room was a decent size and it was a delight to find a proper large king size bed, rather than two singles placed together. Fans of a firmer mattress would be happy and there was also a range of different pillows available on request. The wardrobe was big with fifteen hangers plus there was plenty of drawer and shelf space for a number of nights. A large desk, armchair with foot rest and of course TV, safe and mini bar were also present. The TV had a great picture quality but only two UK channels; BBC News and Sky News.

The bathroom had a separate shower and bath along with a toilet divided off from the rest of the room with a glass door. The shower had very good power and remained hot throughout long use. A full range of toiletries were present although it was a bit ‘hit and miss’ whether these were replenished during the daily turndown service. It was also a shame that there was only a single dressing gown on the first night, however this was then added to on the second day. It is also worth pointing out that the air-conditioning in the room was useless - no matter what setting it was placed on the room would not cool down, so instead we resorted to leaving the windows open to help slightly. The wifi was reliable but selecting the free option required you to log back in every 24 hours. Unfortunately the biggest down side to the room was the lack of any tea / coffee making facilities. For a high end five star hotel it isn’t unreasonable to expect a small coffee machine or kettle at the least; ordering and paying for room service early in the morning for a single cup of tea seems a little ridiculous and not something I’d associate with perfect service or convenience. 

The hotel has two main restaurants and the ES Bisztro was fantastic. We chose to dine there on the first evening after arriving relatively late and every member of staff was fantastic - friendly and explained the options in a relaxed way making us feel comfortable. There was a slight problem during the wait for our main courses but the situation was handled perfectly and full credit to the waiter that evening. The food was delicious and it would definitely be somewhere we’d return to should we be in the city again. Even if not staying in the hotel this restaurant is somewhere I’d recommend a visit.

The two bars of the hotel cater for both a relaxed and more formal feel. The Living Room was great to relax in during an afternoon and the waitresses were attentive and friendly. A couple of English newspapers were available and plenty of books to help wind down. With plenty of sofas and seating available plus accompanied with the fire it really did feel like being relaxed at home. The Blue Fox is more the evening venue with live DJs on the Friday and Saturday night we stayed, although it remains more of a bar than ‘nightclub’. The range of cocktails each has a unique twist although the range is somewhat limited. I have no doubt that they could make anything to request though since the choice of spirits and liqueurs was massive. It never did quite to manage to have an intimate feel but probably would feel more suitable for groups. Full marks must go to the bar girl Melinda though; her attitude and friendly nature kept us entertained and laughing all evening. Her knowledge of the drinks and cocktails, and everything Budapest, was remarkable and made our evening.

Overall the Kempinski Corvinus is a modern and clean five star hotel. During our stay though there wasn’t anything which ‘blew us away’, and small issues like the lack of an in-room coffee machine stop me giving this review a matching five star; other hotels at the same level are able to provide such touches and it makes all the difference. For example the room lacked any form of ‘hotel directory’, something simple which was just another detail overlooked. Not major but “what time is breakfast served”, “are there shoe shining services offered” etc; questions unanswered and services desired but unknown about. On that subject it may also be worth pointing out that shoe cleaning machines in the corridors aren’t present in this hotel, something which frequently proves useful and often present in other five star hotels. For the convenient location, cleanliness and lovely staff I’d recommend the Kempinski Corvinus, as long as you’re not looking for a top hotel which ticks absolutely every box and gives a true ‘experience’ feel.

Habitués à cotoyer des hôtels 4* et 5*, nous avons été déçus de notre séjour au Kempinski de Budapest, entre les 30/12/14 et 3/1/15.

A notre arrivée, nous nous attendions à un accueil chaleureux et regorgeant d'information et de proactivité quant à nos besoins d'information éventuels. Nous avons eu affaire à des automates, très froids et peu aidant.

Notre plus grosse déception : avoir choisi la destination en fonction du programme proposé par l'hôtel pour son réveillon de la Saint Sylvestre. Le site internet promettait un repas dans l'un des trois restaurants de l'hôtel + l'accès à différenteS salles dans lesquels des DJs et différents styles de musique. Au final, notre soirée était pliée à 22H car ce fameux accès aux autres salles était sujet à la possession d'un ticket pré-commandé. Ambiance nulle, ringarde, pompeuse, condescendante, c** pincé. Jeune couple de 28 et 25 ans, nous aurions facilement pu nous endormir avant les douze coups. Nous avons dû nous rattrapper sur l'une des places avoisinantes, bondée de monde et à la bonne ambiance.

Comme de nombreuses chaînes se prétendant de standing, tout est payant. Vous désirez visionner un film via internet sur votre écran de chambre ? Vous souhaitez surfer sur le web sur l'un des ordinateurs de l'hôtel ? Tout ceci vous sera gentiment facturé.

D'après la communication web, nous pensions que le Blue Fox, bar lounge au sein de l'hôtel, était un bar où nous pouvions passer nos soirées avec de la bonne musique et du mouvement. Le bar est sympa et les serveurs les seuls souriants de l'immeuble. Cependant n'escomptez pas vous y défouler.

Le Nobu est un endroit élégant et où l'on mange bien. Mais 50 euros pour 12 sushis et 10 sashimis rendent vite la note salée.

2 visites par jour des femmes de ménage nous ont paru trop intempestives, laissant parfois leur travail inachevé.

A part cela, l'hôtel est luxueux, les bagagistes très soigneux et avenants, les chambres sont belles et le Spa bien équipé également. On vous oblige néanmoins à cotoyer les NUDISTES qui semblent avoir priorité sur les autres. L'on vous y jette vite des regards condescendants, alors que ces mêmes personnes n'ont pas la courtoisie d'ôter leurs serviettes des quelques transats à disposition.

Bon hôtel mais le service peu courtois, peu avenant et snob en sont les gros points faibles.

Room: We were upgraded to a Deluxe Room; very well appointed but missing a tea/coffee maker in the room. Bathroom was fabulous...2 bottles of water daily. We asked for a kettle and an ironing board with iron which were not there in the room, and they were generous enough to not charge us for any of these. Beds were very comfy for a good night's sleep. Room extremely quiet despite being a street side room. Housekeeping staff very very polite and did their job very well.
Location: Cannot ask for a better location at this budget. Of course, the Four Seasons Budapest would have a better view from all its rooms...but it would be a different category altogether. Café Gerbeaud - the famous coffee shop is a 3 min walk at a leisurely pace. A fantastic pizzeria 2 min away. Metro closeby - we didnt try it so dont know the exact distance. Shopping street of Budapest - 4 min walk away.
Breakfast - the best we saw on our 15 days trip of Europe...between Salzburg, Vienna, Prague and Budapest at similarly priced hotels, Kempinski Budapest was the best by miles.
Restaurants - Nobu...fabulous...although very very pricey...but then its a Nobu. The bar was a tad pricey as well, compared to other things in Budapest...but we enjoyed the one evening we spent at the Kempinski bar, although I wouldnt go there again. Much better options available for the money you would spend there.
For us the best part of the trip was the Concierge team (they were members of Les Clefs d’Or - the crossed gold keys team) - There was pretty much nothing that they couldn't do for us. Since this was the last bit of our holiday, we decided to splurge a bit and give our tired legs a break....so we took the 3 hour guided tour in a limousine provided by the hotel. That includes a driver + a guide separately and they'll cover the entire Budapest in those three hours...with a lot more info than a guidebook would give you. Surprisingly, none of it seemed rushed or overwhelming at all. It is pricey, but was well worth it for us. 

The people all across the hotel were very polite and helpful. If you are looking for a stay where you want to feel cared for and are willing to splurge a bit, you can't get a better deal. The stay - the room and breakfast, by itself is great. We would easily recommend this hotel to a friend or go there again ourselves.

Budapest in general a great place to go to...lots of history and loads of things to do. Only the shopping options and malls were a bit of a let-down...but apart from that, the city has lots to offer. Market Hall is a great place to go to for the buy and the experience.

Stayed there 3 nights, as part of the delegation of the company I work for during a Finance Conference in Budapest.

Very nice hotel, well located, with friendly staff and clean and spacious rooms.  Just 2 blocks away from the river and around the corner of a nice shopping area.  On top of that, good room service in speed and quality. I ordered breakfast and dinner and definitely, very good........however, as a recurrent and permanent business traveller, the hotel had some things, which really bothered me

1. no coffee machine in the room - today, in almost every hotel which pretends to attract high profile business travellers, an espresso machine is a must. I never go to restaurants for breakfast, and drink a lot of coffee in the morning. Here was no possible.  Definitely an area with a clear improvement possibility

2. No iron and no board in the room.....what is not a problem, because I understand that I can call and ask for this.....what I did......the problem was that the first day I called, they brought the iron and board, then I came back in the evening, needed to iron a new shirt for  a dinner....realized they took board and iron away.......had to call again.....then went for dinner, came back,...... wanted to iron a shirt for the morning....but iron and board was not there again....so I had to call again.....next day the same.....the same.....and the same......every time the room was cleaned or the bed prepared, they took iron and board away !!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!! I told the reception, and they ignored my comment......hence, in 3 day I had to call 5 times for an iron and board. This is not high standard level......and by the way, the board they brought me the last day, was broken, completely destroyed.  

I think that next time I will try the Intercontinental or the Four Seasons

The hotel location is fantastic; close to retail and the Metro, as well as walking distance to Chain Bridge, Parliament, Buda Castle...  Considering it's "Luxury" rating, I expected more.  The service was fantastic, you can tell that their guests are extremely important to them.  Check-in was easy and quick.  Room was not huge but clean.  Buffet breakfast was fantastic! Majority of staff spoke good English.

Here were my issues:  King bed was actually 2 twin mattresses on a King box spring, so there was a definite gap..also separate bed linens for each side?  Is this a European thing?  Hallway noise was a minor issue 2 of 5 nights, oh, and too few electric outlets available.

No mini-fridge (big minus), but free Wifi (a plus).  Turn-down service was a nice benefit but I would have preferred a mini-fridge.  Bathroom was large, and tub was nice.  However, shower pressure was below average, and water drained slow.

The fitness center was TINY considering how much Spa space there was, and not well equipped for a property claiming to be luxury.  Locker room was small and had only one shower stall, and no towels.

All in all not impressed, but haven't stayed in other European hotels for comparison.  No-go on a repeat stay here, sorry, great service only goes so far.

Dopo aver trascorso un soggiorno eccellente presso l Adlon Kempiski di Berlino non ho esitato a decidere per il Corvinus di Budapest vista anche la posizione centralissima.All arrivo rimani ben impressionato dalla bella hall con accesso diretto al modaiolo e piacevole ristorante Nobu e al discreto bar.Di sensazione opposta l ingresso alla superior no smoke al 6* piano. La camera é ampia ma scialba nell arredamento con un letto non grande e senza nessun plus che ti aspetteresti da un hotel di questa categoria.Oltretutto all arrivo la camera era allestita come singola.Il servizio pomeridiano insufficente visto che al primo passaggio trovata la camera occupata non é stata riassettata malgrado al successivo passaggio fosse libera ma hanno solamente messo una targhetta dicendo che non era stato possibile.Buona la Spa e gentilissimi i dorman.Probabilmente il mio giudizio non é allineato con quello degli amici di TA poichë viziato dall esperienza berlinese ma non mi sento di dare un giudizio oltre la sufficenza viste le normali aspettative per un hotel di questa categoria.

LHW and Kempinski think that they can camouflage their old and tacky rooms with a trendy lobby and expect guests to keep coming back?

The corridors smell of smoke, the bedcovers smell of smoke, the bedsheets smell of smoke, the carpets are torn and tacky, YUCK is not the word for this Kempinski and LHW property.

In case you thought we were in a base category room and had paid peanuts, let me correct you that we were in a junior suite and had paid over Euro 200 for this room. The whole floor smelled of smoke. Even when you slept in the bed, instead of the bedsheets smelling of fresh detergent, they smelt of smoke. 

Being Leaders Club members, our breakfast should have been free, but we were adviced that only Continental breakfast was free - 3 pieces of bread, 1 cup of tea / coffee, 1 glass of juice. So much for paying top notch prices. 

WIFI - we could connect only 3 devices - which era are they living in? Each member in the room today travels with 3 devices. What happens to the other members if you have a 4th device? Too bad. They still live in the Dark Ages.

The bathrooms need to be remodeled. The quality of the bath towels was used and used and used. Did they not have money for frequent changes? Well evidently not. 

This hotel is definitely passe. There are far better choices in the city.

Nos alojamos por 3 noches, muy cómodo, habitaciones modernas, 6 ascensores, todo funciono según lo esperado, buena asesoría para las actividades turísticas, muy buena ubicación. Muy profesionales, perfecto ingles. Recomendado!

We stayed four nights (one night free) in this hotel, thanks to a very affordable British Airways package, which included breakfast (a must, because it would cost the proverbial an arm and a leg if you paid for breakfast separately) . When I say "breakfast,"  I am talking about a breakfast to remember - six or seven courses if you've got the stamina. The Kempinski is centrally placed and most of the attractions on the Pest side near the hotel are well within walking distance.  Apart from the obvious luxury of the hotel - we were upgraded on arrival ( I don't know why but we didn't complain!) - one of the most memorable aspects of the hotel was the friendly and helpful staff. Nothing was too much trouble for them. The reception desk team made check-in easy and we were immediately impressed. We went equipped with the usual collection of guide books and maps, but it's still daunting trying to figure out how to find the best place to eat or how the metro works. Almost every day, our problems were solved by the concierge desk, which was able to provide an answer to any question you could throw at them. They were great. Apart from the breakfast and one cocktail each, we didn't eat or drink in the hotel because the meals and drinks are pretty expensive, as you would expect in a five-star hotel, but as a luxurious base to explore Pest it could not be bettered. We also tried the spa, though I thought the pool was a bit on the small side - feeling crowded with 10 people in it. The only other slight negative was that we thought the temperature in our room was too hot and what ever setting we chose on the control unit it made no difference. Couldn't be bothered with the hassle of asking someone to fix it, especially as we could open the windows. Good also to see that BA has finally got its act together, providing reasonably-priced city breaks to take on the likes of Expedia, Easyjet etc. I would recommend this hotel as the perfect choice for a city break if you can find the right package.

The Kempinski is a good hotel but our four night stay was not without its disappointments and irritations. We were given a room on the eighth floor which was the smallest we had ever encountered in a five star hotel. So small, in fact, that when we found only one pair of slippers and one robe in the bathroom our first reaction was to assume that we had been assigned a single room. During our stay I twice had to report that the call button at the eighth floor was discharging static electricity on the last occasion with a clearly visible spark. Not a major problem but there were children staying in the hotel and the experience I had may have been quite upsetting for them.
One morning, at 04:20am, we received a wake-up call we had not requested.The hotel's response was an apology and then to forget all about it. The final and unavoidable irritation came in the public rooms on the ground floor where, to us incomprehensibly, dubious 1980s power ballads were continually played through the hotel's speaker systems. Whilst this ensured that we did not spend our evenings in the hotel, passers-by who were not spared this "treat" must have wondered if they were walking past a sophisticated hotel or an over 50s disco night.
On the plus side the hotel's spa was excellent, the breakfast was very good and the hotel's location in this fascinating city is convenient for many attractions. We certainly intend to return to Budapest but it is unlikely that we would choose to return to the Kempinski.

I have been staying at the Kempinski Corvinus many many times. 
I still really like it.. Its still a very good hotel.. And i always have a good sleep here.. But its not as good as it used to be..

Checking in quite late at night. The guy at the reception told us, breakfast is till 10 o´clock, actually its till 1030. 

Arriving at the room, I immediately called for a second duvet, our king size bed, only provided one large one, and we prefer to have two separate. The girl at the phone didn't really understand what i wanted, but after a while i guess she understood. It was quite late and we wanted to sleep.. After waiting more than 25 minutes i gave reception a second call... and then after another 10 minutes wait the second duvet finally arrived..... 

The room itself didn't change since the first time i stayed at the Kempinski maybe 15 years ago.. Its still very good maintained, very very clean, housekeeping is doing a good job !!  But.. after the refurbishment of the public areas, (versy fancy and nice... beautiful flower decoration) the room seems quite dated.. Its really about time for a refurbishment of the rooms.. 
And what I also didnt like is that the curtains are not really dark, in the morning the room turns bright !!

I asked the guest relation girl if she knew when the rooms will be renovated, her answer was : I don´t  know !  so i guess no futures plans to renovate :-(

Next day I asked guest service for an iron to be brought to my room.. After again waiting almost 30 min and nothing happened, i called again, and got the answer, that i was in the waiting line to get an iron..... WTF... sorry I have never experienced this at any Kempinski Hotel. Ok it maybe can happen that there is no iron available, but at least i expect to get a call to inform me about that...... !!! After another 10 minutes wait it was finally my turn to get an iron...

Gym : very small room but with OK equipment, just wish everything would work. 3 devices were "out of order"..... not acceptable..

Breakfast at the new ES Bistro, very very good products especially the cheese and the ham.. really a good breakfast.. Loved it.. But...
The room was way too air conditioned... all tables at the window or the walls was directly under the very cold air condition... And what is not acceptable for a Kempinski Hotel and for a breakfast at that price was that some egg dishes had an extra surcharge... 2-3 Euros... Sorry Kempinski that is picky and not 5 star !!!

Had dinner at the NOBU which was delicious, but very pricy for small portions.. but its ok,, its what we expected... we still liked it... :))

To sum it up.. Kempinski is still a very good hotel.. but maybe its time to try something new in Budapest !!
